TITLE: Mangusta 80 vs Smaller Charter Yachts in Marbella: Why 24 m Changes the Day
DESCRIPTION: Mangusta 80 (24 m, 12 guests, free jet ski) vs the 15–18 m luxury yachts typically chartered in Marbella. Honest comparison on deck space, water toys, range and cost-per-person.

Home › Guide › Mangusta 80 vs Smaller Charter Yachts in Marbella: Why 24 m Changes the Day By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers The Mangusta 80 — our "Nina" — is a 24 m Italian sport yacht in service since 2020 and the biggest yacht regularly available for day charter from Puerto Banús. This page is the honest comparison vs the 15-18 m "luxury" yachts most Marbella charter brokers will steer you towards, with a free jet ski thrown in. The Marbella luxury-yacht segment below 24 m When you ask any Marbella broker for a "luxury yacht charter", you typically get pitched in this size class: Princess V58 / V60 — 18 m British sport cruiser, 10 day guests, single helm, no jet ski standard. Sunseeker Predator 60 / Manhattan 60 — 18 m sport-cruiser/flybridge, 10-12 day guests, premium fit-out. Azimut 55 Fly / 60 Fly — 17-18 m Italian flybridge, 10-12 day guests, large flybridge. Pershing 56 / 62 — 17-19 m Italian high-performance, sleek interior, 10-12 day guests. Galeon 500 Fly / 560 — 16-17 m Polish flybridge with side terraces, 10-12 day guests. All beautiful boats. All charter in Marbella between €3,500 and €5,500 for a 4-hour half-day. All are 17-19 m and 10-12 guest capacity. The Mangusta 80 sits one full size class above. At 24 metres / 80 feet , she has roughly 50% more deck and interior volume than a 17 m Sunseeker. And she comes with a free Sea-Doo jet ski on the swim platform. Deck space — the most tangible difference A 17 m yacht has roughly 65 m² of usable deck (cockpit + flybridge + foredeck combined). The Mangusta 80 has roughly 120 m² . Concretely: Bow: two full-width sun-pads vs one sun-pad split for a windlass on the smaller yachts. Cockpit: dining for 12 vs dining for 8 cramped. Aft deck: bench seating + table + tender garage opening with the jet ski underneath. Side decks: walk-around access vs side-mounted on smaller sport cruisers. Sun-pad/lounge total: sleeps 6 in sun at once vs 4 on the smaller yachts. The jet ski — €800 of value, free Every other luxury yacht charter in Marbella charges €200/h for jet ski rental as an extra (often a separate booking with a separate captain). On the Mangusta 80, the Sea-Doo is on the swim platform, ready to launch, free for guests to use throughout the entire charter. Over a 4-hour booking, that's typically €800 of jet-ski rental value baked in. Crew — captain alone vs captain + deckhand The smaller 17 m yachts run with one captain. The Mangusta 80 needs two crew (captain + deckhand) for safe handling at 24 m — and the deckhand becomes your service person too: drinks served, water toys deployed, swim platform manoeuvres handled. The smaller yachts can't match this without paying extra for a hostess. Cabins and overnight Boat Cabins Overnight guests Chef-grade galley Princess V58 2 4 No (compact galley) Sunseeker Predator 60 2 4 No Azimut 55 Fly 3 6 Compact Mangusta 80 3 6 Yes — full galley, marble worktop If overnight comfort matters, the Mangusta 80 has a proper master suite with king bed, plus two guest cabins, plus a saloon convertible — and a galley that can actually produce a cooked dinner, not just reheat. Range and offshore capability The Mangusta hull was designed for high-speed offshore work — twin diesels driving water jets, 30+ knot top speed, comfortable in seas that would stop a 15 m sport cruiser. For a Gibraltar day trip from Puerto Banús (100 NM round trip), the Mangusta does it in 7 hours of total time at planing speed. A 17 m yacht needs 9-10 hours and the captain looks at the forecast much more nervously. Cost — per guest on a full boat Boat Half-day 4h (peak) Guests Per person Princess V58 ~€3,500 10 €350 Sunseeker Predator 60 ~€4,200 12 €350 Pershing 56 ~€4,500 10 €450 Mangusta 80 €4,719 12 €393 (incl. jet ski + 8 extra metres of boat) On a full charter, the Mangusta works out to within €50 per person of a 17 m alternative — and you're getting 50% more boat plus the free jet ski. When the Mangusta 80 is the obvious pick Big celebration days — birthdays, anniversaries, milestone events. The deck space and crew service deliver the experience. Groups of 10-12 who want everyone to fit comfortably (not crammed on the cockpit). Guests who'll actually use the water toys. Jet ski + paddleboard + donut all included. Overnight on board — three cabins and a proper galley. Long-range trips — Gibraltar, multi-day to Cádiz, the offshore work where 17 m boats start feeling small. Compare across our fleet If 12-guest superyacht scale is overkill, our 12.5 m options sit at €749/2h — see the Astondoa 40 (9 guests, Spanish-built classic) and Azimut 39 (11 guests, Italian flybridge). For the full Mangusta 80 details, photos, gallery and the chef-platter video: Mangusta 80 page . Frequently asked questions What other 'luxury' yachts can I charter in Marbella? Mid-tier luxury segment from Puerto Banús typically runs 15-18 m: Princess V58, Sunseeker Predator 60, Azimut 55 Fly, Pershing 56. None are 24 m, none include a jet ski free, and most don't have the chef-grade galley layout the Mangusta has. Is the Mangusta 80 really the biggest charter yacht in Marbella? For day charter from Puerto Banús, yes — at 24 m she's the largest yacht regularly bookable for a day or half-day on the Marbella stretch. Bigger yachts (30 m+ Sunseekers, 40 m Benettis) berth in Puerto Banús but are private or full-week charter only. What's the cost difference between Mangusta and a typical 16 m luxury yacht? A 16 m Princess V60 charter runs ~€3,500-€4,500 for 4 hours. Home › Guide › Mangusta 80 vs Sunseeker Predator 74: Which Superyacht Wins in Marbella? By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers When planning a luxury day on the Costa del Sol, few decisions are as important as choosing the right boat. Two of the most sought-after superyachts in Marbella are the Mangusta 80 and the Sunseeker Predator 74 . Both turn heads in Puerto Banús, but they offer very different experiences. In this Mangusta 80 vs Sunseeker Predator 74 Marbella comparison, we break down size, speed, capacity, amenities, and price to help you decide which superyacht wins for your charter day. Size and Capacity The Mangusta 80 measures 24 metres (79 ft) and can carry up to 12 passengers. The Sunseeker Predator 74 is slightly shorter at 22.5 metres (74 ft) and typically also carries 12 passengers. The Mangusta has a wider beam (5.5m vs 5.2m), giving it more deck space for lounging and dining. If you're a group of 10-12, the Mangusta offers more room to spread out, especially on the flybridge and aft deck. Speed and Performance The Sunseeker Predator 74 is built for speed. With twin MAN V12 engines (1,550 hp each), it can reach a top speed of 38-40 knots. The Mangusta 80, powered by twin MTU 16V 2000 M93 engines, tops out at around 32 knots and cruises comfortably at 28 knots. For pure adrenaline, the Predator wins. But the Mangusta's cruising speed is more than enough for a relaxed day hopping between Marbella's best anchorages like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés. Design and Layout The Mangusta 80 is a classic Italian open sport yacht with sleek lines and a spacious flybridge. Its layout includes a large aft deck with a dining table, a forward sunpad, and a well-appointed saloon. The Sunseeker Predator 74 has a more aggressive, sporty profile with a lower profile and a cockpit that feels like a sports car. The Predator's interior is more modern and minimalist, while the Mangusta feels more traditional and luxurious. Onboard Amenities Both boats come with a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and a full drinks package (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava) plus light snacks. The Mangusta 80 goes a step further by including a jet ski free of charge for the minimum 4-hour booking. The Predator does not typically include a jet ski. For entertainment, the Mangusta has a high-end sound system, while the Predator focuses more on the driving experience. Charter Pricing Our Mangusta 80 (available in grey and white) starts at €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours. This includes the jet ski, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT. The Sunseeker Predator 74 is not in our fleet, so pricing is on request from other operators. Expect to pay a similar or higher rate, often without inclusions like a jet ski. For the best value, the Mangusta 80 is hard to beat. Which Boat Is Best for Your Group? Choose the Mangusta 80 if you want a spacious, luxurious day with a jet ski included, ideal for families or groups of friends who want to relax and have fun. Choose the Sunseeker Predator 74 if you're a speed enthusiast who prioritises performance and a sporty aesthetic. For most charterers in Marbella, the Mangusta's combination of space, amenities, and price makes it the winner. Where Will You Cruise? Both boats depart from Puerto Banús , Marbella's iconic superyacht marina. Popular routes include heading east to Cabopino and Marbella Marina , or west to Estepona and Sotogrande . Anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for swimming in crystal-clear waters. The Mangusta's shallow draft (1.2m) allows it to get closer to shore than the Predator (1.5m). For more ideas, browse our Marbella boat rental guide . Final Verdict In the Mangusta 80 vs Sunseeker Predator 74 Marbella showdown, the winner depends on your priorities. The Mangusta offers more space, a jet ski, and a better all-inclusive price. The Predator offers more speed and a sportier look. For a day of luxury, fun, and value on the Costa del Sol, the Mangusta 80 takes the crown. Check availability and book your Mangusta 80 charter today . Frequently Asked Questions Which is bigger: Mangusta 80 or Sunseeker Predator 74? The Mangusta 80 is 24m long, while the Sunseeker Predator 74 is 22.5m. The Mangusta has a slightly larger beam and more deck space, making it better for groups wanting to spread out. Both can carry up to 12 passengers. Which boat is faster in Marbella waters? The Sunseeker Predator 74 is typically faster, with a top speed around 38-40 knots thanks to its sportier hull and twin MAN engines. The Mangusta 80 cruises at 28-30 knots, but its top speed is around 32 knots. For pure adrenaline, the Predator wins. Can I charter a Sunseeker Predator 74 in Marbella? Yes, you can charter a Sunseeker Predator 74 in Marbella, but it is not in our fleet. We offer the Mangusta 80 (grey and white) and other superyachts. For a Predator, you would need to contact other operators. What is included in the Mangusta 80 charter? The Mangusta 80 charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). A jet ski is also included free for the minimum 4-hour booking. How much does the Mangusta 80 cost to charter? The Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours. This includes the jet ski. For longer durations, pricing is on request. All prices include VAT, skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. Which boat is better for a party in Puerto Banús? The Mangusta 80 has a larger flybridge and aft deck, making it ideal for socialising and parties. The Sunseeker Predator 74 is more focused on speed and sporty lines. For a party, the Mangusta's spacious layout wins. Can I visit Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés with either boat? Yes, both boats can anchor at Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés. The Mangusta's shallow draft (approx 1.2m) allows it to get closer to the beach. The Predator's deeper draft may require a bit more distance, but both are fine for those spots. Which boat is more luxurious? Both are high-end superyachts, but the Mangusta 80 offers a more traditional Italian luxury feel with teak decks and spacious saloons. The Sunseeker Predator 74 has a more modern, sporty interior. It's a matter of taste. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: Marbella Boat Charter Group Size Limits: Capacity Rules Explained
DESCRIPTION: Discover Marbella boat charter group size limits: capacity rules for 5–12 passengers. Find your perfect yacht at Puerto Banús. Book now.

Home › Guide › Marbella Boat Charter Group Size Limits: Capacity Rules Explained By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Planning a Marbella boat charter with friends or family? One of the first things to check is your group size . Every boat has a strict maximum passenger capacity, set by Spanish maritime law and the vessel's licence. Exceeding it isn't just inconvenient – it's illegal and unsafe. This guide explains the capacity rules for our fleet in Puerto Banús, so you can pick the right yacht for your party. Why group size limits matter Capacity limits aren't arbitrary. They're based on the boat's design, safety equipment, and stability calculations. Spanish authorities enforce them rigorously. If your group exceeds the limit, the skipper cannot take you out. That means wasted time and money. Always book a boat that fits your exact headcount – including children. Our fleet: passenger capacities at a glance Here are the maximum passenger numbers for each boat in our Marbella fleet. All depart from Puerto Banús . Ferretti 94 (29m) – 12 pax Canados 86 (26m) – 12 pax Maiora 26m (26m) – 12 pax Mangusta 80 (24m) – 12 pax (also Grey and White variants) K80 (24m) – 12 pax Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) – 12 pax Azimut 39 (12.5m) – 11 pax Astondoa 40 (12.5m) – 9 pax Fairline Targa 12m (12m) – 10 pax Pershing 46 (14m) – 10 pax Lagoon 380 (11.6m) – 10 pax Bandido (11.5m) – 8 pax Red Tide (11m) – 6 pax Speedboat (7m) – 6 pax Mariah SX21 (6.4m) – 5 pax Dubhe (8m) – 5 pax How to choose the right boat for your group First, count your guests including children. Then match that number to a boat with capacity equal to or greater than your group. For example: 5 people – Dubhe or Mariah SX21 are perfect for a cosy day out. 8–10 people – Bandido, Fairline Targa 12m, Pershing 46, or Lagoon 380. 11–12 people – Azimut 39 (11 pax) or any of the 12-pax yachts (Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Ferretti 94, Canados 86). If your group is smaller than the maximum, you still get the whole boat to yourselves – no sharing with strangers. What's included in every charter Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You don't pay extra for the skipper – he's part of the package. Prices start from €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (Tier A). Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 start at €4,719 for 4 hours and include a jet ski. Popular routes for different group sizes All charters depart from Puerto Banús . From there, you can cruise to: Cala del Faro – a quiet cove 15 minutes away, great for swimming. Cala Cortés – another scenic spot, ideal for anchoring. Marbella Marina – the town's main harbour, 20 minutes east. Estepona – 30 minutes west, with its charming old town. Cabopino – a smaller marina with sandy beaches. Larger yachts (12 pax) can comfortably reach Sotogrande or even Gibraltar for a full-day trip. Pricing by group size: get the best value Because we charge per boat, not per person, larger groups often get better value. For example, a 12-person charter on an Azimut 58 Flybridge costs the same for 8 people as for 12. So if you have a full group, you're maximising your spend. Smaller groups might prefer the Dubhe or Mariah SX21 for a more intimate, budget-friendly trip. For boats outside Tier A and Tier B, pricing is on request. Just contact us via WhatsApp and we'll send a quote within minutes. Frequently asked questions What is the maximum group size for a boat charter in Marbella? The maximum passenger capacity across our fleet is 12, on yachts like the Mangusta 80, Azimut 58 Flybridge, Ferretti 94, and Canados 86. Smaller boats carry 5 to 11 passengers. All limits are set by the boat's licence and Spanish maritime regulations. Can I bring more people than the stated capacity if they are children? No. The passenger limit includes everyone on board, regardless of age or size. Infants and children count towards the total. This rule is enforced for safety and insurance compliance. Does the skipper count as part of the passenger limit? No, the skipper is not included in the passenger count. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, so your group size refers only to guests. For example, a boat with a 12-passenger limit can carry 12 guests plus the skipper. Which boat is best for a group of 10 adults? For 10 adults, we recommend the Pershing 46 (10 pax), Fairline Targa 12m (10 pax), Lagoon 380 (10 pax), or the Azimut 39 (11 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús and include drinks, snacks, and a skipper. Are there any boats that can carry 8 passengers? Yes. The Bandido (8 pax) is an 11.5m motorboat ideal for smaller groups. It departs from Puerto Banús and is priced on request. Other options for 8 include the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) and the Red Tide (6 pax – too small). What happens if we exceed the capacity limit? Exceeding the capacity is not allowed under any circumstances. The skipper will refuse to depart if the group is larger than the boat's licensed limit. This is a legal requirement for safety and insurance. Always book a boat that fits your exact group size. Can I charter a boat for just 2 people? Yes. The Dubhe (5 pax) and Mariah SX21 (5 pax) are excellent for couples or small groups. Both are available from Puerto Banús. You can also book any larger yacht for a private charter – you pay for the boat, not per person. Do you offer per-person pricing or only per-boat? We offer per-boat pricing only. The rates quoted (e.g., €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40) cover the entire vessel for your group, up to its maximum capacity. There are no per-person charges, so larger groups often get better value. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: Marbella Skipper &amp; Captain Tipping Etiquette Guide
DESCRIPTION: Learn marbella skipper tipping etiquette for your boat charter. Our guide covers how much to tip (€50-€200), when, and who to tip. Plan your day now.

Home › Guide › Marbella Skipper & Captain Tipping Etiquette Guide By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Planning a boat charter in Marbella? Understanding marbella skipper tipping etiquette ensures you enjoy a smooth, respectful experience on the water. Whether you're renting a luxury yacht from Puerto Banús or a speedboat for a day trip, knowing how much to tip, when, and who to tip is key. This guide covers all the numbers and local customs you need. Why tipping your skipper matters Your skipper is responsible for navigation, safety, and making your day unforgettable—from anchoring at Cala del Faro to spotting dolphins off Cabopino. A tip shows appreciation for their expertise and effort. It's not mandatory, but it's a standard courtesy in the yachting world. Most skippers work on a base salary plus tips, so your gesture directly supports their livelihood. How much to tip: concrete numbers A good rule is 10-15% of the charter cost. Here's what that looks like for our boats: Tier A boats (e.g., Astondoa 40, Azimut 39): €749 for 2 hours → tip €75-€110. For a full day (€2,299), tip €200-€300. Tier B boats (e.g., Mangusta 80, white or grey): €4,719 for 4 hours → tip €400-€700. This includes a free jet ski, so tip higher if you use it. Other boats (e.g., Ferretti 94, Pershing 46): pricing on request, but tip 10-15% of the total. For shorter trips (2 hours), €50-€80 is fine. For full-day charters (8 hours), €150-€300 is typical. Who to tip and how On most charters, you only tip the skipper. If there's a deckhand or stewardess (common on larger yachts like the Canados 86 or Ferretti 94), tip them separately. A fair split: 70% to skipper, 30% to crew. Hand cash directly to each person at the end of the trip. Avoid leaving tips on the table or in an envelope—it's more personal this way. Cash is king: what to bring Always bring euros in cash. Most skippers don't carry card machines, and ATMs in Puerto Banús can have long queues. If you're on a Mangusta 80 and want to tip for the jet ski, bring extra. For a group of 10-12 people, pooling cash beforehand makes it easy. A typical envelope of €200-€300 works for a half-day charter. When not to tip If the skipper is late, rude, or doesn't follow the agreed itinerary (e.g., skipping Cala Cortés without reason), you're not obliged to tip. You can also mention any issues to us—we take feedback seriously. Poor service is rare, but it happens. Trust your instincts. Cultural nuances in Marbella Marbella is international, but Spanish tipping culture is relaxed. 10% is considered generous. Unlike the US, you won't be chased for a tip. A friendly 'gracias' and a handshake are always appreciated. If you're unsure, ask the skipper discreetly—they'll likely say 'whatever you think is fair'. That's a polite cue to tip the standard amount. Frequently asked questions How much should I tip the skipper on a Marbella boat charter? A standard tip is 10-15% of the charter cost. For a half-day (4 hours) on a Tier A boat like the Azimut 39 (€749/2h or €2,299/8h), a €50-€100 tip is common. For a full-day on a Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h), consider €200-€300. Always adjust based on service quality. Is tipping expected on boat charters in Marbella? Tipping is not mandatory but is highly appreciated. Skippers and crew work hard to ensure a safe, enjoyable trip. Many guests tip as a gesture of thanks, especially if the skipper goes above and beyond—e.g., finding secluded coves or providing great local knowledge. Should I tip in cash or by card? Cash is always best. Most skippers prefer euros, but some may accept other currencies. Card tips are rarely possible on board. Bring enough cash for the tip plus any extras you might want (e.g., jet ski hire on the Mangusta 80). Do I tip the skipper and the crew separately? Yes, if you have a separate deckhand or stewardess, tip them individually. A common split is 70% to the skipper, 30% to the crew. For example, on a €2,000 charter, €140 to skipper, €60 to crew. Hand each person their tip directly. What if the service is poor—should I still tip? No, you are never obliged to tip for poor service. If the skipper was rude, unsafe, or didn't deliver the promised itinerary, skip the tip. You can also mention any issues to your booking contact; we take feedback seriously. When is the right time to give the tip? At the end of the charter, after you've docked and before you leave the boat. Hand the cash to the skipper with a handshake and a thank you. This is a polite, professional moment. Avoid tipping during the trip as it can feel awkward. Does the charter price include gratuity? No, our prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT, but not tips. The price you see (e.g., €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40) is the total; tips are extra. This is standard across Marbella charters. Are there any cultural tips for tipping in Marbella? Marbella is a cosmopolitan area, but Spanish tipping culture is modest. Unlike the US, 10% is generous. Don't feel pressured to overtip. A simple 'gracias' with a smile goes a long way. If you're unsure, ask the skipper discreetly—they'll appreciate the thought. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: Marbella Boat Rental for Dolphin Watching: Best Times and Spots
DESCRIPTION: Discover the best marbella boat rental dolphin watching season. Spot striped dolphins off Puerto Banús from April to October. Book your charter today.

Home › Guide › Marbella Boat Rental for Dolphin Watching: Best Times and Spots By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella's coastline is a natural highway for dolphins, and a private boat rental from Puerto Banús is the best way to see them up close. The marbella boat rental dolphin watching season runs from April to October, with peak sightings in the warm summer months. Whether you choose a 2-hour sprint or a full-day charter, our skippers know exactly where to find the pods. This guide covers the best times, top spots, and what to expect on the water. When Is the Dolphin Watching Season in Marbella? The prime season for dolphin watching off the Costa del Sol stretches from April through October. Water temperatures rise above 18°C from May, drawing baitfish and the dolphins that feed on them. June and July are the peak months, with sightings reported on nearly every trip. Even in April and October, you have a strong chance of encountering striped dolphins or common dolphins. Winter months (November to March) see fewer sightings, but pods still pass through—just less predictably. Best Time of Day for Dolphin Sightings Early morning (9–11 AM) offers the calmest seas—wind speeds are lower and the water surface is glassy, making it easier to spot dorsal fins. Afternoon trips (2–4 PM) can be productive too, but the sea breeze often picks up, creating chop that hides the dolphins. Late afternoon (5–7 PM) combines good sighting conditions with a golden sunset over La Concha mountain. For the highest probability, book a 2-hour morning charter on our boat rental Marbella fleet. Where Do Dolphins Gather Near Marbella? Dolphins are frequently sighted within 3 nautical miles of Puerto Banús. The key zones are: Cabopino area – 2 NM west of Puerto Banús, near the Cabopino dunes. Cala del Faro – 3 NM east, close to the Marbella Marina. Río Verde mouth – 1.5 NM south, where freshwater meets the sea, attracting fish. Estepona waters – 8 NM west, often visited on longer charters. Our skippers monitor real-time reports and adjust course to where pods are feeding. On a 4-hour charter aboard the Mangusta 80 (€4,719, includes a jet ski), you can cover multiple zones comfortably. What Dolphins Species Can You See? Three species are common in the Alboran Sea: Striped dolphin – the most frequent, recognisable by the dark stripe along its side. Common dolphin – with a distinctive hourglass pattern on its flanks. Bottlenose dolphin – larger, often seen in smaller pods of 5–15. Pilot whales and even orcas pass through occasionally, though sightings are rare. Dolphins often approach boats to ride the bow wave—keep your camera ready at the front of the vessel. Which Boat Is Best for Dolphin Watching? For a small group, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are perfect—€749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, and snacks. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers a flybridge with 360° views. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) has a spacious bow area ideal for spotting. All charters depart from Puerto Banús and include fuel, insurance, and IVA. Tips for a Successful Dolphin Watching Trip Book a morning slot for calm seas and better visibility. Bring polarised sunglasses to reduce glare and spot fins. Wear a light jacket—sea breezes can be cool even in summer. Stay quiet near the bow to avoid startling the dolphins. Respect the animals: maintain distance, no feeding, no loud music. Our skippers follow responsible wildlife guidelines. You'll get close enough for photos without disturbing the pods. Combine Dolphin Watching with Other Activities A longer charter lets you pair dolphin spotting with swimming, snorkelling, or lunch at a chiringuito. On an 8-hour trip (€2,299 on Tier A boats), you can head to Cala Cortés for a swim, then cruise back along the Golden Mile. The Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) includes a flybridge lounge for relaxing between sightings. For a unique twist, the Mangusta 80's free jet ski lets you zip out for a closer look (under supervision). Frequently Asked Questions When is the best time of year for dolphin watching in Marbella? The prime marbella boat rental dolphin watching season runs from April to October. Sightings peak during June and July when waters are warmest and dolphin activity is highest. Even in spring and autumn, you have an excellent chance of seeing pods. What types of dolphins can I see off Marbella? The most common species are striped dolphins and common dolphins. You may also spot bottlenose dolphins and, occasionally, pilot whales. They often travel in pods of 10 to 50 individuals, sometimes approaching the boat to ride the bow wave. Which departure marina is best for dolphin watching? Puerto Banús is the ideal starting point. Dolphins are frequently sighted within 2–3 nautical miles of the marina, especially near the Cabopino area and Cala del Faro. Our fleet departs directly from Puerto Banús, saving you travel time. How long does a dolphin watching charter take? Most dolphin-watching trips last 2 to 4 hours. A 2-hour charter (€749 on Tier A boats) is enough to reach the feeding grounds and enjoy sightings. For a more relaxed day, an 8-hour charter allows you to combine dolphin watching with swimming and lunch stops. What is the best time of day to see dolphins? Early morning (9–11 AM) offers the calmest sea conditions, making dolphins easier to spot. Late afternoon (5–7 PM) is also productive and pairs well with sunset views. Midday heat can reduce surface activity. Are dolphin sightings guaranteed on a private boat rental? No operator can guarantee wild animal sightings, but our skippers know the local hotspots and monitor marine traffic reports. Sightings occur on over 90% of trips during the season. If you don't see dolphins, you still enjoy the coast, swimming, and onboard amenities. What is included in the charter price for dolphin watching? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. Can I bring children on a dolphin watching boat? Yes, children are welcome on all our boats. Life jackets are available for all ages. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) and Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offer plenty of deck space for families. Always supervise young children near the rails. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/blog/marbella-boat-rental-what-to-bring/
TITLE: Marbella Boat Rental: What to Bring for a Perfect Day at Sea
DESCRIPTION: Plan your marbella boat rental with our essential packing list. From sun protection to snacks, discover what to bring for a perfect day at sea in Marbella.

Home › Guide › Marbella Boat Rental: What to Bring for a Perfect Day at Sea By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers A marbella boat rental promises a day of sun, sea, and stunning coastline – but what you pack can make or break the experience. Whether you're heading out on a Mangusta 80 or a cosy Speedboat , being prepared means more time relaxing and less time worrying. Here's your no-fluff packing list for a perfect day at sea from Puerto Banús. 1. Sun Protection: Your Top Priority The Mediterranean sun is intense. UV rays reflect off the water, increasing exposure by up to 40%. Pack: High-SPF sunscreen (SPF 50+, water-resistant) – apply before boarding and reapply every 2 hours. Lip balm with SPF – lips burn easily. Sunglasses with polarised lenses – reduce glare and protect your eyes. A wide-brimmed hat or cap – shade for your face and neck. UV-protective clothing – a lightweight rash guard or long-sleeved shirt. 2. Swimwear and Towels Every charter includes a swimming stop. Our skippers know the best coves – Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or the clear waters off Cabopino. Bring: Swimsuit (or two – one to wear, one dry). Quick-dry towel – microfibre towels take less space and dry fast. Change of clothes – a light dress, shorts, or a dry set for the ride back. 3. Footwear: Safety First Boat decks get hot and slippery. Avoid: Heels or hard soles – they damage teak and are dangerous. Open-toed sandals without grip. Best options: non-slip boat shoes, flip-flops with a rubber sole, or going barefoot on deck (watch for hot surfaces). 4. Layers: The Sea Breeze Factor Even on a 30°C day, the wind at 20+ knots can feel chilly. Pack a light jacket, fleece, or windbreaker . The return leg at 5–6 PM often brings cooler air. A thin jumper or hoodie is a lifesaver. 5. Snacks and Drinks – What's Already Included All our charters include: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and ice. No need to bring these – but you are welcome to bring extra: Your favourite spirits or mixers. A picnic lunch – the skipper will provide a cooler with ice. Fruit, crisps, or sweets. If you're on a Tier A boat (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39), drinks and snacks are included in the price. For larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 , you can arrange a catering upgrade via WhatsApp. 6. Tech and Entertainment Most boats have a Bluetooth speaker – bring your playlist. For photos and videos: Waterproof phone case or dry bag. Lanyard for your phone – prevents drops overboard. Action camera (GoPro or similar) – great for swimming stops. Note: signal can be patchy offshore. Download your playlist beforehand. 7. Seasickness Prevention The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but if you're sensitive: Take a non-drowsy travel tablet 30 minutes before departure. Ginger sweets or crystallised ginger help settle the stomach. Acupressure bands (Sea-Bands) are a drug-free option. Stay on deck, focus on the horizon, and avoid heavy meals beforehand. 8. Documents and Valuables You only need your phone and perhaps a credit card for extra purchases. Leave passports, jewellery, and valuables at your hotel or villa. The boat has dry storage, but salt spray and splashes happen. 9. What NOT to Bring Glass bottles – they break and are dangerous on deck. Use plastic or metal cups (we provide them). Large coolers – our boats have built-in coolers. Heavy luggage – space is limited. A small backpack or tote is perfect. Frequently asked questions What is included in a marbella boat rental? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You only need to bring personal items and any extra food or drinks you prefer. Should I bring my own food and drinks? You're welcome to bring your own food and drinks. The boat has a cooler with ice. Many guests bring a picnic, but we already provide soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, and snacks. If you want spirits or special dietary items, bring them along. What type of sunscreen is best for a boat day? Use a high-SPF, water-resistant sunscreen (SPF 50+ recommended). Apply 20 minutes before boarding and reapply every two hours. The sun's reflection off the sea increases exposure, so don't forget ears, lips, and the back of your neck. Do I need a towel and swimwear? Yes, absolutely. We anchor at spots like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for swimming stops. Bring a towel, swimsuit, and a change of dry clothes if you want. The boat has a freshwater shower to rinse off after a dip. What footwear is suitable on a boat? Non-slip shoes or flip-flops are best. Avoid high heels or hard-soled shoes – they can damage the teak deck and are slippery when wet. Barefoot is fine on deck but watch for hot surfaces in direct sun. Is a jacket necessary in summer? Even on a 30°C day, the sea breeze can feel cool once the boat gets moving. A light windbreaker or fleece is a good idea, especially for the return leg in the late afternoon. Many guests are surprised how chilly it gets offshore. Can I bring my phone or camera? Yes, but bring a waterproof case or dry bag. Splashes and sea spray are common. A lanyard for your phone is also handy. The skipper can take photos of your group if you ask. What about seasickness – should I take medication? If you're prone to motion sickness, take a non-drowsy travel tablet 30 minutes before departure. The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but early morning or afternoon breezes can create slight chop. Ginger sweets or acupressure bands also help. Ready to book your marbella boat rental ? Check our full fleet and choose your perfect vessel. TITLE: Best Time of Day for Dolphin Watching in Marbella
DESCRIPTION: Discover the best time for dolphin watching Marbella. Morning or sunset? We share concrete tips, boat options, and departure info from Puerto Banús.

Home › Guide › Best Time of Day for Dolphin Watching in Marbella By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella's coastline is one of the best places in the Mediterranean to spot dolphins in the wild. But the question we hear most often is: what is the best time for dolphin watching in Marbella? The short answer is early morning, but sunset runs are a close second. In this guide, we break down the pros and cons of each time slot, share concrete tips, and show you which boats from our fleet are best for the job. Why Morning Trips (8–10 AM) Are the Best Time for Dolphin Watching in Marbella Dolphins are most active during the early hours. They feed in the morning, and the sea is usually calmer before the afternoon wind picks up. Calm water means better visibility and a smoother ride. From Puerto Banús, most dolphin zones are just 20–30 minutes away, so a 2-hour charter is plenty. Our Boat Rental Marbella fleet includes the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 (both Tier A, €749 for 2 hours) — ideal for a quick, productive morning trip. Sunset Dolphin Watching (6–8 PM): The Photographer's Choice Sunset trips are popular for a reason: golden light, cooler temperatures, and a relaxed vibe. From June to September, the sun sets around 9 PM, so a 6–8 PM slot gives you both good light and a higher chance of sightings. Dolphins are still active, though slightly less than in the morning. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is a fantastic option for a sunset charter, offering plenty of deck space and a free jet ski. Rates start at €4,719 for 4 hours. Afternoon Trips: Possible but Less Reliable Between 12 PM and 4 PM, the sea is often choppier, and boat traffic is at its peak. Dolphin sightings are still possible — especially if you head further out — but the encounter rate drops. If you're set on an afternoon trip, stick to a 2–3 hour window and book a boat with a flybridge for better visibility, like the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax). Pricing is on request via WhatsApp. Best Months for Dolphin Watching in Marbella While dolphins are present year-round, the best months are May through October . During this period, calm sea days are more frequent, and encounter rates reach 85–90%. Winter months (November–February) see fewer sightings due to rougher seas and shorter daylight hours. For a full overview of what to expect, read our Marbella dolphin watching guide . Which Boats Are Best for Dolphin Watching? Boat Length Pax Best For Price (2h) Astondoa 40 12.5m 9 Morning trips €749 Azimut 39 12.5m 11 Morning trips €749 Mangusta 80 24m 12 Sunset charters €4,719 (4h) Azimut 58 Flybridge 17.6m 12 Afternoon visibility On request All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. Departure is from Puerto Banús. Tips for a Successful Dolphin-Watching Trip Book early — morning slots are popular, especially in July and August. Check the weather — calm seas (Beaufort scale 0–2) give the best odds. Bring a camera — sunrise and sunset light make for stunning shots. Wear a light jacket — even in summer, the sea breeze can be cool at speed. Respect the animals — our skippers follow strict guidelines to avoid disturbing pods. For real-time availability and weather advice, contact us via WhatsApp. We'll help you pick the best time slot for your group. Frequently Asked Questions What is the best time of day for dolphin watching in Marbella? Early morning (8–10 AM) is generally the best time for dolphin watching in Marbella. The sea is calmest, dolphins are actively feeding, and there is less boat traffic. Sunset trips (6–8 PM) are a close second, offering better lighting and cooler conditions, especially in summer. Can you see dolphins in the afternoon in Marbella? Yes, but afternoon sightings are less reliable due to increased boat traffic and choppier waters, particularly from May to September. If you go, choose a 2–3 hour window between 2 PM and 5 PM. Our Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 are available for afternoon charters. Which months are best for dolphin watching in Marbella? May through October offer the highest encounter rates, often 85–90% on calm days. Dolphins are present year-round, but winter months (November–February) see fewer sightings due to rougher seas and shorter daylight hours. How long does a dolphin-watching trip from Puerto Banús take? Most dolphin-watching trips last 2 to 4 hours. The dolphin zones are typically 20–30 minutes from Puerto Banús, leaving plenty of time for observation. Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) are ideal for 2-hour trips starting at €749. What types of dolphins can I see in Marbella? The most common species are bottlenose dolphins and striped dolphins. Occasionally, you may spot short-beaked common dolphins. They are often seen in pods of 5–20 individuals, sometimes larger. Is it better to go dolphin watching in the morning or at sunset? Morning trips (8–10 AM) offer the highest probability of sightings due to calm seas and active feeding. Sunset trips (6–8 PM) provide better light for photography and a more relaxed atmosphere. Both are excellent options. What should I bring for a dolphin-watching trip in Marbella? Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, a light jacket (especially for sunset), and a camera. Our charters include water, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, and light snacks, so you don't need to bring food or drinks. Can I book a private dolphin-watching charter in Marbella? Yes, all our boats are available for private charters. You can book via WhatsApp for real-time availability. Home › Guide › Marbella Feria San Bernabé: How to Combine the Casetas With a Daytime Cruise By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella feria San Bernabé is the city’s biggest summer party — six days of casetas, fairground rides, and live music from 11 to 16 June. But you don’t have to spend every hour on dry land. A daytime yacht charter from Puerto Banús lets you escape the midday heat, cruise the Costa del Sol, and still make it back for the evening festivities. Here’s how to combine both without rushing. Why Add a Yacht to Your Feria San Bernabé Plan? The casetas open from lunchtime, but the real buzz starts after 20:00. That leaves you a solid window for a morning or early afternoon cruise. The Mediterranean in June averages 24°C water temperature and 28°C air — perfect for swimming, sunbathing, and sipping cava offshore. A boat also gives you a different perspective of Marbella’s coastline, from the Golden Mile to Cabopino. Plus, anchoring off Marbella’s main beach puts you right opposite the fairground. Shower, rest, or explore Marbella’s old town. The casetas start serving food and drink from midday, so you can grab lunch at the fair if you prefer. 20:00–late — Head to the feria. Wear white and red (the feria colours). Dance sevillanas, try churros, and ride the Ferris wheel. Taxi back to Puerto Banús or your hotel. Alternative: book a 12:00–16:00 slot if you prefer a later start. Where to Anchor Near the Feria The best spot is off Playa de la Venus or Playa del Faro, directly in front of the fairground. Depth is 5–8 metres, sandy bottom. You can drop anchor and swim ashore (the beach is public) or stay on board and watch the action from the water. The sunset view over La Concha mountain is spectacular. For a quieter swim, head west to Cala Cortés or east to Cabopino — both 15–20 minutes from Puerto Banús. What to Bring and Wear Pack a small bag: swimsuit, towel, sunglasses, sunscreen (SPF 50+), a light jacket for the evening, and your phone for photos. The boat provides cold drinks and snacks, but you can bring your own food. Remember cash for the casetas — some stalls don’t accept cards. For the feria, women often wear flamenco dresses (trajes de gitana), men wear white shirts and red scarves. But casual summer wear is fine too. TITLE: Marbella Fishing Charter License Rules 2026: What You Need to Know
DESCRIPTION: Essential Marbella fishing charter license rules 2026: who needs a license, penalties, and how our skippered trips stay legal. Book with confidence today.

Home › Guide › Marbella Fishing Charter License Rules 2026: What You Need to Know By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Planning a Marbella fishing charter in 2026? The rules around fishing licenses in Andalucía are straightforward – but getting them wrong can cost you. This guide explains exactly who needs a license, what the 2026 regulations say, and how booking a skippered charter with Boat Rental Marbella keeps you fully legal while you focus on catching fish. Who Needs a Fishing License in Marbella? In Andalucía, anyone aged 14 or over fishing from the shore or a private boat must hold a valid recreational fishing license. Licenses cost roughly €7–€25 per year and are issued by the Junta de Andalucía . However, if you fish from a licensed charter boat with a professional skipper – like all our vessels – you do not need an individual license. The boat’s commercial charter license covers everyone on board. 2026 Rule Changes: What’s New? The 2026 regulations reinforce existing limits with stricter enforcement. Key updates include: Daily bag limit: 5 kg of fish per person, max 5 fish of any one species (excluding tuna). Tuna fishing: A specific tuna license is now mandatory; fishing is banned June–August in some zones. Minimum sizes: Sea bass (36 cm), bream (23 cm), mackerel (20 cm) – your skipper will measure and release undersized fish. Protected species: All catches of protected species (e.g., some sharks) must be released immediately. Our skippers are fully up to date with these rules, so you never have to worry. Penalties for Unlicensed Fishing Fines for fishing without a license in Marbella start at €150 for a first minor offence. Serious violations – such as exceeding bag limits, fishing in protected marine areas, or catching protected species – can result in fines up to €3,000 . Booking a skippered charter eliminates this risk entirely. Our Fleet: Legal and Ready for Action Every boat in our fleet – from the Mangusta 80 to the Astondoa 40 – is fully licensed for recreational fishing charters. All depart from Puerto Banús , giving you quick access to prime fishing grounds off the Costa del Sol. Our skippers know the best spots for sea bass, bream, mackerel, and seasonal tuna. What’s Included in Your Fishing Charter All our charters include: Licensed skipper (who handles all legal compliance) Fuel and insurance Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava Light snacks Basic fishing tackle and bait (on request) Spanish IVA (21% VAT) You just bring your own rod and reel if you prefer – our boats have plenty of deck space for serious anglers. Best Times and Spots for Fishing in Marbella The fishing season runs year-round, but the best catches happen May to October for tuna and March to November for sea bass and bream. Popular fishing areas near Marbella include Cala del Faro , Cala Cortés , and the waters off Estepona . Our skippers will choose the best location based on weather and season. Book Your Legal Marbella Fishing Charter Ready to fish without the paperwork? Browse our full fleet and pick your ideal boat. For pricing on boats like the Ferretti 94 or Pershing 46 , message us on WhatsApp. For our most popular options – the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 – prices start at €749 for 2 hours (all inclusive). Frequently Asked Questions Do I need a fishing license for a Marbella fishing charter? No. When you book a skippered charter with us, the boat’s commercial license covers all passengers. You can fish without an individual license. This applies to all our boats, from the Azimut 39 to the Mangusta 80. What are the 2026 fishing license rules for private boats in Marbella? If you bring your own boat or fish from the shore, you must hold a valid recreational fishing license issued by the Junta de Andalucía. Licenses cost around €7–€25 per year and are available online. The 2026 rules also enforce a 5 kg daily bag limit per person. Can I catch tuna on a Marbella fishing charter? Tuna fishing is heavily regulated. In 2026, a specific tuna license is required, and fishing is banned from June to August in certain areas. Our skippers know the zones and seasons – we only offer tuna trips when fully legal and with the correct permits. What are the penalties for fishing without a license in Marbella? Fines start at €150 for a first minor offence and can escalate to €3,000 for serious violations, such as fishing in protected areas or exceeding bag limits. Our charters eliminate this risk entirely. What fish can I catch on a Marbella fishing charter? Common catches include sea bass, bream, mackerel, and sometimes amberjack. From May to October, you may also catch bluefin tuna (with the right license). Our skippers target species based on season and conditions. Do your boats provide fishing gear? We provide basic tackle and bait on request, but serious anglers often bring their own rods and reels. All our boats, including the Ferretti 94 and Pershing 46, have ample deck space and rod holders. Are there size or bag limits for fish in Marbella? Yes. The 2026 rules set a daily bag limit of 5 kg per person, with a maximum of 5 fish per species (excluding tuna, which has separate quotas). Minimum sizes vary by species – your skipper will advise and ensure compliance. Can I keep the fish I catch? Yes, you can keep your catch within legal limits. Many local restaurants in Puerto Banús will also cook your fish for you. Just check with the skipper first to ensure you’re within the bag and size limits. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/blog/marbella-fishing-charter-tuna-season/
TITLE: Marbella Tuna Fishing Season: When to Book Your Charter
DESCRIPTION: Plan your Marbella tuna fishing season charter with expert timing tips. Bluefin run June–October. Book your boat from Puerto Banús today.

Home › Guide › Marbella Tuna Fishing Season: When to Book Your Charter By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella's tuna fishing season offers some of the best bluefin action in the Mediterranean. From June to October, the warm waters off Puerto Banús attract trophy-sized Atlantic bluefin tuna, drawing anglers from across Europe. Whether you're a seasoned fisherman or a first-timer, timing your charter right makes all the difference. This guide covers peak months, boat options, and everything you need to book your Marbella tuna fishing season charter. When Is the Marbella Tuna Fishing Season? The tuna fishing season in Marbella runs from June to October, with the peak in August and September. During these months, sea temperatures range from 20°C to 26°C, creating ideal conditions for bluefin tuna to feed near the coast. Early morning trips, departing around 7–8 AM, often see the most activity. Our fleet of 18 boats, all based in Puerto Banús, offers quick access to prime fishing grounds just 5–10 nautical miles offshore. Best Boats for Tuna Fishing Charters For tuna fishing, you want a boat with range, comfort, and deck space. Our top picks include the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) for its stable platform and flybridge visibility, and the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) for luxury and speed. The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is a more budget-friendly option at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for a full day. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance—no hidden extras. Where You'll Fish: Puerto Banús and Beyond All our charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's glamorous marina. From here, you can reach fishing spots like the waters off Cabopino, Cala del Faro, or further south towards Estepona. The continental shelf drops sharply just a few miles out, creating a natural feeding ground for tuna. Your skipper will navigate based on real-time conditions, ensuring you're where the fish are. What to Expect on a Tuna Fishing Charter A typical tuna fishing charter lasts 4 to 8 hours. You'll find rod holders, a fighting chair on larger boats, and plenty of shade. Our skippers are experienced in bluefin techniques, including trolling with lures and live bait. All safety equipment and insurance are included. You can also arrange for catch cleaning and packing. For the full experience, consider a full-day charter—you'll have time to explore multiple spots and enjoy lunch on the water. Pricing and Booking Tips Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, all inclusive. Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 require a minimum 4-hour charter at €4,719, which includes a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request. For peak season (August–September), book at least 2–4 weeks in advance. Contact us via WhatsApp to check availability and customise your trip. Browse our full fleet to see all options. Local Tips for a Successful Trip Bring sun protection, a hat, and polarised sunglasses—the Mediterranean sun is intense. Wear non-slip shoes, and pack a light jacket for early mornings. Most charters provide drinks and snacks, but you can bring your own food. If you're prone to seasickness, take medication an hour before departure. The waters off Marbella are generally calm in summer, with swells rarely exceeding 1 metre. Frequently Asked Questions When is the best time for tuna fishing in Marbella? The peak Marbella tuna fishing season runs from June through October. Bluefin tuna are most active in August and September when water temperatures are highest and baitfish are abundant. Early morning charters often yield the best catches. What types of tuna can I catch off Marbella? The primary target is Atlantic bluefin tuna, which can weigh over 200 kg. You may also encounter yellowfin and skipjack tuna, especially later in the season. Our experienced skippers know the best spots for each species. How long is a typical tuna fishing charter? Most tuna fishing charters from Puerto Banús last 4 to 8 hours. Half-day trips (4 hours) are popular for groups, while full-day charters (8 hours) allow more time to reach deeper grounds. All boats include a licensed skipper and full amenities. Do I need a fishing license? No, you don't need a personal license. Our charters include a licensed skipper who handles all permits and regulatory requirements. Just bring your enthusiasm and sun protection. What boats are available for tuna fishing? Fishing gear can be arranged on request. Can I keep the fish I catch? Yes, within legal limits. Our skipper will advise on size and bag limits for bluefin tuna. Many charters offer to clean and pack your catch for transport. Check local regulations for specific limits. How do I book a tuna fishing charter? URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/blog/marbella-half-day-morning-vs-afternoon/
TITLE: Half-Day Charter Marbella: Morning vs Afternoon
DESCRIPTION: Deciding between a Marbella half day morning vs afternoon charter? Compare sea conditions, light, dining, and sunset options for your 4-hour boat trip from Puerto Banús.

Home › Guide › Half-Day Charter Marbella: Morning vs Afternoon By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Choosing between a morning and afternoon half-day charter in Marbella can shape your entire experience on the water. Both slots offer distinct advantages—from calm seas and cooler air in the morning to golden light and warmer water in the afternoon. Whether you're planning a family outing, a group celebration, or a romantic escape, knowing the differences helps you book the right 4-hour window. Below, we break down the key factors to consider when deciding on your Marbella half day morning vs afternoon charter. Morning Half-Day Charter (10:00–14:00) Morning charters depart from Puerto Banús at 10:00 and return by 14:00. The sea is typically flat, the wind is light, and the coastline is quieter. This slot is ideal for guests who want a smooth ride, cooler temperatures, and the option to anchor for a swim or a picnic lunch at a cala like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés . You'll have the coves mostly to yourself, and the light is excellent for photography without the harsh midday glare. A morning charter also leaves your afternoon free for other activities—shopping on the Golden Mile or lunch at a chiringuito in Cabopino . Afternoon Half-Day Charter (15:00–19:00) An afternoon half-day starts at 15:00 and finishes around 19:00, just as the sun begins to set. The water is warmer, making swimming more comfortable, and the later hours offer the famous Marbella sunset behind La Concha mountain. This slot is popular for groups wanting a lively atmosphere, with more boats anchored near beach clubs and coves. You can anchor off Estepona or Sotogrande for a swim, then enjoy cava and snacks as the sky turns orange. The return to Puerto Banús is often the highlight, with the marina lights reflecting on the water. Sea Conditions and Weather Mornings in Marbella are usually calm, with sea breezes picking up after noon. If you or your guests are prone to seasickness, a morning charter is the safer bet. Afternoons can have a light chop, but it's rarely uncomfortable and often welcome for cooling off. Summer temperatures range from 25°C in the morning to 30°C+ in the afternoon—bring sunscreen regardless. Swimming and Water Activities Water temperature is warmer in the afternoon (often 24–26°C in summer) compared to morning (22–24°C). If swimming is a priority, book the afternoon. Mornings are still perfectly swimmable, but the cooler water can be a shock. Both slots allow time for snorkelling, paddleboarding, or using the jet ski included on the Mangusta 80 (Tier B). Dining Options: Onboard or Ashore On a morning charter, you can anchor for a picnic lunch or dock at a beachfront restaurant in Cabopino or Marbella Marina . Afternoon charters work well for a late lunch onboard (15:00–16:00) followed by a sunset drink. Both include drinks and snacks—beer, cava, white wine, soft drinks, and water—so you can focus on the experience. Sunset and Photography If sunset is your priority, choose the afternoon slot. The golden hour from 18:00 to 19:00 provides stunning light for photos, especially with La Concha or the Strait of Gibraltar in the background. Morning light is crisp and clean, ideal for capturing the coastline without haze. Which Boats Are Available? Our fleet offers several options for half-day charters, all departing from Puerto Banús . Tier A boats— Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax)—cost €749/2h or €2,299/8h. Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) start at €4,719/4h and include a jet ski. For other boats (e.g., Ferretti 94 , Canados 86 , Pershing 46 ), pricing is on request via WhatsApp. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT. Frequently asked questions What is the difference between a morning and afternoon half-day charter in Marbella? A morning half-day runs from 10:00 to 14:00, offering calm seas, cooler temperatures, and a quieter coastline. An afternoon half-day runs from 15:00 to 19:00, featuring warmer water for swimming, golden light, and the chance to watch the sunset over the Mediterranean. Which half-day is better for swimming: morning or afternoon? The afternoon is better for swimming because the sea has warmed up from the morning sun, and the air temperature is at its peak. Morning water is cooler, but still pleasant in summer (typically 22–24°C). Can I have lunch on a morning charter? Yes, you can bring your own picnic or arrange a catered lunch. Many guests anchor at Cala del Faro or Cabopino and enjoy a relaxed onboard meal. Alternatively, you can dock at a chiringuito for a sit-down lunch. Is the afternoon charter better for sunset views? Absolutely. An afternoon charter ending around 19:00 in summer lets you watch the sun dip behind La Concha mountain and the Strait of Gibraltar. It’s a popular time for photos and a glass of cava on the bow. Are sea conditions different between morning and afternoon? Yes. Mornings typically have flat seas and light winds, ideal for smooth cruising. Afternoons can see a light thermal breeze and slight chop, but this is rarely uncomfortable and often welcomed for cooling off. Which time slot is less crowded on the water? Morning charters are quieter, with fewer boats and less activity in coves like Cala Cortés. Afternoons are busier, especially near popular anchorages and beach clubs, but the lively atmosphere is part of the appeal. Can I combine a half-day charter with a beach club visit? Yes, especially in the afternoon. You can anchor near a beach club like Ocean Club Marbella or Playa Padre, use the tender to go ashore, and return to your boat for a drink. Morning charters are better for a quiet swim and snorkel. What boats are available for a half-day charter? Our fleet includes the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) from €749/2h, and the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) from €4,719/4h. All depart from Puerto Banús. For other boats, pricing is on request via WhatsApp. Still unsure which slot suits you? Browse our full boat rental Marbella guide for more tips, or contact us via WhatsApp for personalised advice. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

Home › Guide › Half Day vs Full Day Yacht Charter Marbella: Which Wins? By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Deciding between a half day vs full day yacht charter Marbella comes down to how you want to spend your time on the Costa del Sol. Half-day trips (2–4 hours) are perfect for a quick celebration, sunset cruise, or a taster session. Full-day charters (6–8 hours) unlock the coastline all the way to Estepona or Sotogrande, with multiple swim stops and a leisurely lunch. This guide breaks down the costs, itineraries, and value of each option so you can choose what fits your group best. Cost Comparison: Half Day vs Full Day Our Tier A boats — the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) — offer the clearest price difference. A 2-hour half-day charter costs €749 , while a full 8-hour day is €2,299 . That works out to €374/hour for the short trip versus €287/hour for the full day — a 23% saving per hour. For Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), the minimum charter is 4 hours at €4,719 , which includes a free jet ski. A full-day on the Mangusta is priced on request, but the per-hour rate drops significantly. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT. No hidden extras. Itinerary Reach: How Far Can You Go? From our base at Puerto Banús , a half-day charter typically stays within 5–8 nautical miles. You can cruise past the Golden Mile , stop at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés , and enjoy a swim or two. A full-day charter (6–8 hours) gives you enough fuel and time to head west to Estepona (10 NM), anchor for lunch, and even push to Sotogrande (20 NM) if you choose the 8-hour option. Eastbound, you can reach Cabopino and the dunes of Artola . Which Group Size Fits Which Duration? Half-day (2–4h): Best for groups of 2–9 people on Tier A boats, or up to 12 on the Mangusta 80. Ideal for couples, small families, or a quick corporate event. Full-day (6–8h): Suits groups of 6–12 who want a proper day out. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) and Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) are popular for full-day charters with multiple stops. Weather and Season Considerations In summer (June–September), sea temperatures average 24–26°C and winds are light in the morning. A half-day from 10:00 to 14:00 avoids the afternoon heat. A full-day from 10:00 to 18:00 lets you enjoy the calm morning, a lunch stop, and an afternoon swim. In spring or autumn, a half-day is often enough — the sun sets earlier and the water is cooler (18–22°C). What’s Included in Every Charter? Inclusion Details Licensed skipper Experienced, English-speaking Fuel Unlimited for agreed itinerary Drinks Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Snacks Light snacks (nachos, olives, crisps) Insurance Full liability and passenger cover VAT (21%) Included in all prices When to Choose Half Day Choose a half-day if you want a sunset cruise (19:00–21:00 is popular), a short celebration like an anniversary, or a taster before booking a longer trip. It’s also the budget-friendly option — from €749 for 2 hours on a Tier A boat. Half-day is also ideal if you have other plans for the day, like a round of golf at Río Verde or shopping in Puerto Banús. When to Choose Full Day Choose a full-day if you want to explore beyond Marbella , have a long lunch at anchor, or enjoy multiple swim stops. It’s the best value per hour and gives you a proper Mediterranean experience. Full-day charters are also recommended for larger groups (10–12 pax) who want space and time to relax. The Mangusta 80 with its free jet ski is a full-day favourite. How to Book Ready to decide? Browse our full fleet on the Marbella boat rental hub and pick your boat. Frequently asked questions What is the main difference between half-day and full-day charters in Marbella? The main difference is time on the water and distance covered. Half-day charters (2–4 hours) are ideal for a quick sunset cruise or a short celebration. Full-day charters (6–8 hours) let you explore further afield, visit multiple beaches, enjoy a longer lunch, and include more swim stops. How much does a half-day yacht charter cost in Marbella? For Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, a half-day (2 hours) costs €749, and a 4-hour charter is priced on request. Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 start at €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum. No. A half-day charter typically stays close to Marbella and Puerto Banús, visiting Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or the coast near the Golden Mile. Is a full-day charter worth the extra cost? If you want to truly explore the Costa del Sol coast, anchor for a long lunch, swim at multiple coves, and enjoy a relaxed pace, yes. The per-hour cost drops significantly — for Tier A boats, €2,299 for 8 hours works out to €287/hour versus €375/hour for 2 hours. Do I need to book in advance for a half-day or full-day charter? TITLE: Marbella Summer Fireworks: Best Yacht Viewing Spots
DESCRIPTION: Watch the Marbella International Fireworks from the sea. Discover the best yacht viewing spots in Puerto Banús &amp; Marbella Bay. Book your charter now.

Home › Guide › Marbella Summer Fireworks: Best Yacht Viewing Spots By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Every summer, the sky above Marbella erupts in colour during the Marbella International Fireworks Competition . While you can watch from the beach or a chiringuito, the best seats are on the water. A private yacht gives you an unobstructed view, a chilled drink in hand, and zero crowds. Here’s how to plan your evening. Where to watch the fireworks from a yacht The fireworks are launched from a barge near the coast between Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina . The prime viewing spots are: Outside Puerto Banús harbour – anchor just beyond the breakwater. You’ll be close enough to feel the boom but safe from the debris. Depths are 10–15m, good holding. Marbella Bay – a wider view, especially if you want to see the reflections on the water. Approximately 1 NM from the launch point. Cabopino – quieter, but further (3 NM). Only recommended if you want a longer cruise before the show. All our boats depart from Puerto Banús , so you can be at the best spot in under 10 minutes. Which yacht to choose For a fireworks charter, you don’t need a full-day boat. A 2-hour evening cruise is perfect. Dress for the evening – August evenings on the water can be breezy. A light jacket or jumper is wise. Arrive on time – boarding is at Puerto Banús. Allow 15 minutes to park and find the boat. No glass on the water – we use plastic cups for safety. Don’t worry, your cava still tastes great. Check the weather – if high winds or thunderstorms are forecast, the event may be postponed. We’ll notify you. Other summer events in Marbella If you’re visiting in August, the fireworks are just one highlight. Other water-friendly events include the Marbella Summer Festival concerts at the Auditorio de Marbella, and the Puerto Banús Boat Show in September. A yacht charter lets you combine any of these with a day on the sea. Frequently asked questions When are the Marbella International Fireworks? The Marbella International Fireworks Competition typically runs on selected Saturdays in August. Exact dates vary each year, so check the official Marbella events calendar or contact us for the current schedule. Can I watch the fireworks from a yacht in Puerto Banús? Yes. The best view is from just outside Puerto Banús harbour, where you can anchor or drift. Our boats depart from Puerto Banús, so you can be on the water within minutes. We recommend arriving 30–45 minutes before the display starts. Which yacht is best for a fireworks charter? For small groups, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (9–11 guests, €749 for 2 hours) are ideal. Each display lasts roughly 20–30 minutes. However, we recommend a 2-hour charter to allow time to cruise out, find a good spot, and enjoy the show without rushing back. Is it safe to watch fireworks from a yacht? Yes. Our licensed skippers know the safe zones and keep a respectful distance from the firing area. All boats have safety equipment and insurance. You'll have a clear, unobstructed view from the water. What is included in the charter price? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). No hidden costs. For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge are popular choices. Contact us via WhatsApp for availability. What happens if the fireworks are cancelled due to weather? If the event is cancelled by the organisers, we offer a full refund or the option to reschedule. A combo package means you don't have to choose between relaxation and adrenaline. You can sunbathe on the flybridge, sip cava, then zip across the waves on a jet ski. Once you reach a calm spot — often near Cabopino or Cala del Faro — the skipper will brief you on safety and operation. You'll get 30–60 minutes of riding time, split between guests. Life jackets are mandatory and provided. The skipper stays nearby for assistance. If you have a valid Spanish jet ski licence (PER or PNB), you can ride solo; otherwise, the skipper can drive while you ride as a passenger. Best Routes for Jet Skiing from Puerto Banús From Puerto Banús, head east towards Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile, where the water is usually flat in the morning. When to Go: Best Season for Jet Skiing The Mediterranean water temperature in Marbella ranges from 18°C in May to 26°C in August. The best months for jet skiing are June to September , when the sea is calm and the sun is strong. May and October are also good, with fewer crowds. Avoid winter (November–March) as the water is cold and winds can be strong. Our charters run year-round, but jet ski availability is weather-dependent. Safety & Licensing Requirements Spanish law requires a jet ski licence (PER or PNB with jet ski endorsement) to ride independently. If no one in your group has a licence, the skipper can operate the jet ski while you ride as a passenger — this is allowed and common. We provide life jackets and a safety briefing before departure. The jet ski is limited to 2 persons at a time. Children under 16 can ride as passengers with an adult. How to Book Your Combo Package Ready to combine a luxury boat charter with a jet ski adventure?
