TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter VAT &amp; Tax: What You&#x27;ll Pay
DESCRIPTION: Understand marbella yacht charter VAT tax — Spanish IVA is always included in our prices. No hidden fees, 21% tax covered. Book with total transparency.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter VAT & Tax: What You'll Pay By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers When you're planning a Marbella yacht charter , the last thing you want is a surprise tax bill at the end. That's why every price on our site includes Spanish IVA (VAT) at 21% — no hidden fees, no asterisks. Whether you're booking the €749/2h Astondoa 40 or the €4,719/4h Mangusta 80, what you see is exactly what you pay. This page explains how VAT works on yacht charters in Marbella, so you can book with total confidence. Spanish IVA on Yacht Charters: The Basics In Spain, all recreational boat charters are subject to a 21% Value Added Tax (IVA). This applies to any private charter departing from a Spanish port, including Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and Cabopino. The tax is calculated on the total charter fee, including the boat, skipper, fuel, and any included extras like drinks and snacks. Our prices are always quoted inclusive of IVA, so you don't need to do any mental arithmetic. For example, the €749/2h rate for the Azimut 39 already has the 21% tax baked in. This transparency is part of our commitment to honest, no-fluff service. What's Included in Our VAT-Inclusive Prices Every charter with us includes the following, all covered by the 21% IVA: Licensed skipper Fuel for the entire trip Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava Light snacks Insurance and safety equipment Spanish IVA (21%) — already included There are no additional port fees, cleaning charges, or tourist taxes. The price you see on our Marbella boat rental page is the final price. How IVA Affects Different Boat Tiers Our fleet is divided into pricing tiers, and IVA is included across all of them: Boat Price (IVA included) Duration Astondoa 40 €749 2 hours Azimut 39 €749 2 hours Mangusta 80 (any variant) €4,719 4 hours (min.) Other boats (e.g., Pershing 46, Ferretti 94) On request On request For boats like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86, pricing is customised based on your itinerary. We'll provide a full quote via WhatsApp that includes IVA, so there are no surprises. Can You Reclaim IVA on a Yacht Charter? If you're an EU business with a valid VAT number, you may be able to reclaim the 21% IVA under Spanish reverse-charge rules. This typically applies to charters used for corporate entertainment or client meetings. Non-EU residents generally cannot reclaim IVA, as it's a consumption tax. We recommend speaking with your tax advisor to confirm eligibility. If you need an invoice with your VAT number, just let us know when booking via WhatsApp — we'll provide a proper tax receipt. No Tourist Tax or Hidden Surcharges Unlike some holiday destinations (e.g., the Balearic Islands), Marbella and the Costa del Sol do not impose a tourist tax on yacht charters. There are no municipal surcharges, port entry fees, or environmental taxes added to our prices. The 21% IVA is the only tax you'll pay. This makes chartering from Puerto Banús particularly straightforward. You can focus on enjoying the coastline — from the Golden Mile to Cala del Faro — without worrying about extra costs. What About Charters for Special Events? Whether you're booking a Marbella yacht charter for a birthday party, wedding reception, or corporate event, the same IVA rules apply. Our all-inclusive pricing covers everything, and we can tailor the catering, decorations, or entertainment to your needs. Just ask for a custom quote — it will always include IVA. For example, a 4-hour charter on the Mangusta 80 with a jet ski included costs €4,719, all taxes paid. No extra line items. Why We Include IVA in Our Prices We've been operating in Marbella long enough to know that hidden fees erode trust. By including IVA in every advertised price, we make budgeting simple. You don't need to calculate 21% on top of the quote — it's already there. This approach is consistent across our entire fleet, from the compact Dubhe (€ on request) to the 29m Ferretti 94. It's part of our ethos: honest, direct, and transparent. If you have any questions about taxes, just contact us — we're happy to explain. Frequently asked questions Is VAT included in your yacht charter prices? Yes, 100%. All prices listed on our site — from the €749/2h Astondoa 40 to the €4,719/4h Mangusta 80 — include Spanish IVA at 21%. There are no hidden taxes added at checkout. What is the VAT rate for yacht charters in Marbella? The standard Spanish IVA rate for recreational boat charters is 21%. This applies to all private charters departing from Puerto Banús and other Marbella marinas. Can I reclaim the VAT on my yacht charter? If you are an EU business with a valid VAT number, you may be able to reclaim the IVA under Spanish reverse-charge rules. Non-EU residents generally cannot reclaim. Contact us via WhatsApp for guidance. Are there any other taxes or fees I should expect? No. There is no tourist tax, municipal surcharge, or port fee added to our charters. The price you see covers the boat, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA. Do you charge extra for fuel or the skipper? No. Fuel and a licensed skipper are included in every charter, and both are covered by the 21% IVA. You won't pay a cent more than the advertised price. Is IVA different for longer charters or overnight trips? For standard day charters (up to 8 hours), the 21% IVA applies. Overnight or multi-day charters may have different tax treatment. Contact us for a custom quote. Why is IVA included rather than added at the end? We believe in total transparency. By including IVA in our upfront prices, you know exactly what you'll pay — no surprises. It's the honest way to do business. What about charters for corporate events or celebrations? The same 21% IVA applies to all private charters, including corporate events, birthdays, and weddings. Our prices are all-inclusive, so you can budget with confidence. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: Cala de Mijas Music Festival: Yacht Charter from Marbella
DESCRIPTION: Cala de Mijas Music Festival 2026 by yacht from Marbella: 22 NM run, anchorage tips, fleet from €749/2h. Book via WhatsApp now.

Home › Guide › Cala de Mijas Music Festival: Yacht Charter from Marbella By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Cala de Mijas Music Festival turns a quiet stretch of Costa del Sol beach into one of the busiest weekends of the Andalucian summer — and the easiest way to attend without traffic, parking battles or queues is to arrive by sea. From Puerto Banús the run is roughly 22 nautical miles east, an hour at cruising speed, and you finish the day anchored offshore with the headline act drifting across the water. This guide explains how the trip actually works on our fleet, what it costs, and which boats suit the festival best. For the full boat list and pricing, see the main page . Why arrive at Cala de Mijas by yacht The festival site sits on the beachfront just east of La Cala de Mijas marina. On festival weekend the AP-7 exit backs up from late afternoon, taxis triple their fares and parking within 2 km is gone by 18:00. By yacht you bypass all of it: you anchor 200–300 metres off the beach, tender in when you want and tender out when you've had enough. You keep your own food, drinks and bathroom on board, and the return trip to Puerto Banús in flat night seas is genuinely one of the better parts of the day. Practically speaking, this is a charter that suits groups of 6–12 who want a proper day out rather than just transport. The boat is the venue; the festival is a 90-minute interval in the middle. The route from Puerto Banús All our boats depart Puerto Banús. The eastbound route runs past Marbella Marina, the Golden Mile, Río Verde, Cabopino and Calahonda before reaching La Cala de Mijas. Total distance is around 22 NM. A typical timeline on the Azimut 39 or Astondoa 40 at 22 knots: Time Location Notes 14:00 Depart Puerto Banús Welcome drinks, briefing 14:45 Cabopino Swim stop, 30–45 min 16:30 Arrive Cala de Mijas Anchor, tender check 17:00–22:00 Festival Tender shuttles ashore 22:30 Depart for Banús Dinner on deck 00:00 Back in Banús Late return possible The Mangusta 80 and Pershing 46 run the route noticeably faster, useful if you want a longer Cabopino stop. The Lagoon 380 catamaran is slower but the most stable platform at anchor off an open beach. Boats that suit the festival For groups of 8–11 the sweet spot is the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 — Tier A pricing, full inclusions, and enough deck space to actually enjoy the day. Both are 12.5m, both seat 9–11 guests, and both come in at €749 for 2 hours rising to €2,299 for 8 hours with skipper, fuel, drinks and 21% IVA included. For a statement event or a 12-guest party, the Mangusta 80 (white or grey) is the obvious upgrade: 24 metres, free jet ski, €4,719 minimum 4 hours. The Azimut 58 Flybridge, Pershing 46 and Ferretti 94 are priced on request via WhatsApp — useful if you want a flybridge for the sound system or extra cabins for guests staying late. For groups that prioritise stability over speed, the Lagoon 380 catamaran (11.6m, 10 pax) sits flat at anchor even in 1-metre swell. That matters at Cala de Mijas, which is an open roadstead. Anchoring and tender logistics The bay east of La Cala marina has a sand seabed in 6–10 metres, holding well in settled weather. We typically anchor 200–300 metres offshore — close enough for a 5-minute tender run, far enough to stay out of the swimming zone. The skipper runs a fixed shuttle: confirmed drop times (e.g. 17:00 and 19:30) and confirmed pickup times (e.g. 22:00 and midnight). You don't need to call — you just turn up at the agreed spot. Conditions matter. Westerly poniente winds above 15 knots make the anchorage uncomfortable; in that case the skipper will hold the boat closer to Cabopino, which is 6 NM back west and far more sheltered. We monitor the forecast 48 hours out and offer free date changes if conditions look unsuitable. What's included on every charter Licensed Spanish skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (cured ham, cheese, olives, crisps) Towels, Bluetooth sound system, swim ladder 21% Spanish IVA — the quoted price is the final price Not included: festival entry tickets (buy on the official festival site), any restaurants ashore, and jet ski hire on Tier A boats. The Mangusta 80 includes the jet ski free. For full inclusion details see the fleet page . Festival-day food on board Most groups skip the food trucks ashore and eat on deck. Standard catering is built into the price — light snacks for grazing through the afternoon. For a proper dinner we can add a chef-prepared menu (paella, grilled fish, salads) for €45–65 per person, ordered 72 hours in advance via WhatsApp. The 8-hour Tier A charter at €2,299 leaves comfortable headroom in most budgets for catering, festival tickets and a round of cocktails ashore. Booking timeline and deposits Cala de Mijas Music Festival weekend is the busiest non-July date for our fleet. Tier A boats and the Lagoon 380 sell out 4–6 weeks ahead. To book, message us on WhatsApp with date, group size and boat preference. We confirm within the hour during the working day, hold the slot for 24 hours, and send a deposit link — typically 30% to confirm, balance on the charter day. Cancellation for weather is free up to 48 hours before departure. The main page has the full fleet, current pricing and the WhatsApp button. Two scrolls and you're talking to the skipper. Frequently asked questions Can I attend Cala de Mijas Music Festival by yacht from Marbella? Yes. We depart Puerto Banús and cruise roughly 22 NM east to anchor off La Cala de Mijas beach, within tender distance of the festival site. The run takes 50–70 minutes on a planing boat such as the Pershing 46 or Fairline Targa 12m, longer on a displacement cruiser. You stay on board for food, drinks and music drifting over the water, then tender ashore for the headline sets. The skipper coordinates timings so you don't miss the acts you care about. How far is Cala de Mijas from Puerto Banús by sea? About 22 nautical miles east along the coast, passing Cabopino and Calahonda. At 22 knots — a comfortable cruise for our Azimut 39 or Astondoa 40 — that's roughly an hour. The Mangusta 80 and other Tier B boats will do it noticeably faster, but most guests prefer to slow down, swim at Cabopino on the way and arrive relaxed. Plan the return for after sunset; the run back in flat evening seas is part of the experience. Which boats in your fleet work best for the festival? For groups of 8–11, the Azimut 39 and Astondoa 40 hit the sweet spot — Tier A pricing from €749/2h, full inclusions, comfortable for a long day. For larger parties or a statement event, the Mangusta 80, Azimut 58 Flybridge or Pershing 46 give you more deck space and a proper sound system. The Lagoon 380 catamaran is the most stable option at anchor off an open beach, which matters if conditions get lumpy. Is it safe to anchor off Cala de Mijas? In settled summer weather, yes — the seabed is sand and holds well in 6–10 metres. The bay is an open roadstead though, so westerly poniente winds above 15 knots make it uncomfortable and the skipper may move the boat to a more sheltered spot near Cabopino. We monitor the forecast in the 48 hours before departure and will rebook your charter date free of charge if conditions look unsuitable. Safety always overrides the festival schedule. What's included in the charter price? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment and Spanish IVA at 21%. On board you'll find water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava and light snacks. Towels and a Bluetooth sound system are standard. What's not included: festival tickets (buy separately on the official site), restaurant lunches ashore, and any extras like jet ski hire on Tier A boats. If you're planning a weekend around the tournament, adding a yacht charter from Puerto Banús transforms the experience into something truly memorable. This guide covers how to structure your weekend, which boats suit your group, and what you can expect from a day on the water alongside the padel action. Why combine padel with a yacht weekend? Marbella offers a rare blend of world-class sport and Mediterranean luxury. The tournament venues sit minutes from Puerto Banús, meaning you can watch morning quarter-finals, walk to the marina, and be cruising past the Golden Mile within 30 minutes. A yacht provides a private, air-conditioned retreat between matches — no crowded beach clubs, no queues. You can anchor off Cala del Faro, dive into 22°C water, and return for the evening finals refreshed. Suggested weekend itinerary A balanced weekend might look like this: Friday evening: Arrive in Marbella, check into your hotel or villa near the Golden Mile. Dinner at a Puerto Banús beachfront restaurant. Saturday morning: Watch early matches at the tournament. Break for a 4-hour yacht charter (e.g., 12:00–16:00) — cruise to Cabopino, swim, enjoy the onboard bar. Return for semi-finals. Sunday: Full-day charter (8 hours) on a Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge. Head west to Estepona or east to Cala Cortés. Jet ski included on the Mangusta. Evening finals from the flybridge with a glass of cava. Monday: Depart or extend with a laid-back half-day cruise to Sotogrande. This structure gives you two distinct on-water experiences without missing any top-tier padel. Best boats for a padel weekend group Groups attending the tournament typically range from 8 to 12 people. Here are our recommended options: Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax): €749/2h or €2,299/8h. Ideal for a smaller group wanting a cost-effective afternoon. Departs Puerto Banús. Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax): Same pricing as the Astondoa. Slightly more passenger capacity. Good for mixing padel fans and partners. Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax): From €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a free jet ski. The ultimate statement boat for a celebration weekend. Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax): Pricing on request. Flybridge offers panoramic views — perfect for watching the sunset after a match day. All boats include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance. No hidden extras. Where to cruise during the tournament From Puerto Banús, you have several excellent cruising options within a 30-minute to 1-hour trip: Cala del Faro: A quiet cove 15 minutes east, with clear water and a small beach. Great for a quick swim between matches. Cala Cortés: 20 minutes west, slightly larger, with calm waters. Popular for anchoring and paddleboarding. Estepona: 45 minutes west. The old town and marina offer a charming lunch stop. The Mangusta 80 can make this run in under 30 minutes. Cabopino: 20 minutes east. A small marina with a sandy beach and chiringuitos — ideal for a casual bite. Your skipper will tailor the route to your group’s preferences and the day’s conditions. Home › Guide › Marbella Polo Cup: Sotogrande Polo + Same-Day Yacht Charter Itinerary By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers The Marbella Polo Cup at the Santa María Polo Club in Sotogrande is one of the summer’s most glamorous sporting events. With a bit of planning, you can combine a morning yacht charter from Puerto Banús with an afternoon watching world-class polo – then return for a sunset cruise along the Costa del Sol. Here’s a practical same-day itinerary using our fleet. How the Itinerary Works The Santa María Polo Club is 30 NM south of Marbella, about a 45-minute drive from Puerto Banús. Matches typically kick off at 16:00 and finish by 18:00. That leaves the morning free for a 4-hour yacht charter (10:00–14:00) or a shorter 2-hour trip (10:00–12:00). After the match, drive back to Puerto Banús for an evening cruise along the Golden Mile. All our charters depart from Puerto Banús Marina – see our full boat list . If you want a longer day on the water, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) costs from €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT. This works with a 2-hour morning charter and a 2-hour evening charter, or one 4-hour charter split around the polo. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange timing. Tickets are sold by the club – we don’t sell them. The atmosphere is relaxed: think champagne, summer dresses, and linen suits. The club has a restaurant and bar. After the match, head back to the coast for a late dip at Cala Cortés or a drink at Puerto Banús. Evening Cruise Options After the Polo If you’ve booked a full-day charter, your skipper can take you from Puerto Banús to Estepona (10 NM west) or along the Golden Mile for sunset. Frequently Asked Questions How far is Sotogrande from Puerto Banús? Sotogrande is about 30 nautical miles south of Marbella, or a 40–50 minute drive by car. The Santa María Polo Club is roughly 45 minutes from Puerto Banús Marina. What time do Marbella Polo Cup matches start? Matches at the Santa María Polo Club usually start at 16:00 and finish around 18:00, though times can vary by day. Check the official schedule before booking. Do I need to book the yacht and polo separately? Yes. We only arrange the yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Polo tickets and transport to Sotogrande are your responsibility. Contact us via WhatsApp for availability. Can I watch the polo from the yacht? No – the Santa María Polo Club is inland, not on the coast. The yacht charter is for the morning or evening; you watch the match from the club’s grandstands. What’s the sunset time in Marbella during polo season? During summer (June–August), sunset is around 21:30. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/blog/marbella-restaurants-by-boat/
TITLE: Marbella Restaurants You Can Arrive at by Boat
DESCRIPTION: Marbella restaurants by boat: 9 beach clubs and chiringuitos with mooring or tender drop, plus 4h charter from €749. Book via WhatsApp today.

Home › Guide › Marbella Restaurants You Can Arrive at by Boat By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers The fastest table in Marbella is the one you reach by sea. Skip the N-340 traffic, the €25 valet and the 20-minute walk from the car park: arrive directly at the chiringuito by tender, or moor stern-to at a Puerto Banús quayside terrace. This guide covers nine restaurants between Estepona and Cabopino that work for a lunch stop on a half-day charter, the practicalities of getting ashore, and how to pair the meal with a swim at Cala del Faro or Río Verde . Puerto Banús quayside: mooring stern-to for lunch Five venues inside the marina take walk-up boat traffic from 12:00 to 16:00. Cipriani (Italian, terrace right on the Levante quay), Astral (Mediterranean fish, opposite the superyacht line), La Sala by the Sea (British-leaning brasserie, easy with kids), Pangea (steaks, lounge atmosphere) and Sumosan (Japanese, end of the harbour). All five sit within a 50m walk of the visitor berths. Our skipper calls Puerto Banús harbour control on VHF 09 about 30 minutes before arrival and you usually have a berth allocated by the time you tie up. Quayside rates for a 2-3 hour lunch run €40-€90 depending on overall length; for boats over 20m we pre-book the day before. None of these venues charge a corkage if you arrive by water — you simply order from their drinks list as usual. Trocadero Arena and the Río Verde anchorage Trocadero Arena sits 1.5NM east of Puerto Banús on the sand at Río Verde. It is the closest serious chiringuito to the marina and the easiest first stop on a 4h Tier A charter at €749. We anchor in 4-5m of water about 100m off the beach, drop the tender, and you step ashore at the lifeguard tower. The kitchen runs a Mediterranean menu: grilled sea bass €32, ibérico croquettes €18, rice dishes from €28 for two. Sunbeds on their stretch of sand cost €40 a pair including a parasol. Service from 13:30; reserve the morning of your charter on +34 952 779 365. From the anchorage you see La Concha and the Golden Mile framing the coast — one of the best lunch views in Marbella. Nikki Beach: the dressy option Nikki Beach Marbella sits 3NM east of Puerto Banús, in front of the Don Carlos hotel. The anchorage is exposed to the east wind, so we check the forecast and only commit if Levante stays under 12 knots. Tender ashore is a 4-minute run. The dress code matters here: no swimwear at the table after 14:00, so guests change on the boat into linen or a dress before the tender shuttle. A two-course lunch with a bottle of rosé runs €70-€110 a head; sunbeds €60 each. Sunday brunches are theatrical and pricey but a good reason to take the 8h charter at €2,299 and stay for the dancing-on-tables 16:00 set. Cabopino chiringuitos: low-key and fish-led Push another 5NM east of Nikki Beach and you reach the dune system at Cabopino. Three chiringuitos work here for a boat lunch: Bono Beach , Andy's Beach and El Oasis . All three are sand-floored, swimwear-OK, with main courses €18-€28 and a kids' menu around €14. Anchorage is sheltered from westerlies and the holding is good in 4m of sand. We drop the hook 120m off Bono Beach's lifeguard post and shuttle in by tender. This is the best stop if you want a quiet lunch without the Puerto Banús showboat energy; it also pairs nicely with a return swim at Cala Cortés on the way back . How the catering-onboard option works If you would rather not go ashore, every charter already includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava and light snacks — that is enough for a 2-3 hour trip. For a proper meal onboard we coordinate with you the day before: tapas platters from €18 per person, sushi boxes around €35, paella for the table at €45 per head. We collect the order from Puerto Banús before you board and serve it at anchor in your chosen cove. Drinks remain included on top. This is a good move on August weekends when the popular chiringuitos run two-hour waits for walk-ins. Recommended routes by charter length Charter length Route Lunch stop Price (Tier A) 2h Puerto Banús quay loop Cipriani or Astral, stern-to €749 4h Puerto Banús → Río Verde → return Trocadero Arena, tender ashore €1,499 6h Puerto Banús → Nikki Beach → Cala del Faro swim Nikki Beach (smart-casual) €1,899 8h Puerto Banús → Cabopino → sunset return Bono Beach, swimwear €2,299 All four routes run on Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, both 12.5m, up to 9-11 guests). Upgrading to a Mangusta 80 for the day starts at €4,719 for 4h and adds a free jet ski, which makes the swim stop between courses considerably more entertaining. Booking sequence for a lunch charter Pick a route from the table above and message us the date on WhatsApp via the homepage . We hold the boat for 24 hours while you reserve the restaurant directly. Send us the reservation confirmation; we plan timings around the table slot. Pay a 30% deposit to confirm; balance settles on the day in Puerto Banús. Arrive 15 minutes before departure at the Levante quay; the skipper greets you with the drinks already on ice. Frequently asked questions Which Marbella restaurants can I actually arrive at by boat? You can reach Puerto Banús quayside venues (Cipriani, La Sala by the Sea, Astral) directly by mooring stern-to. For beach clubs like Nikki Beach, Trocadero Arena, Bono Beach and the Cabopino chiringuitos, you anchor 100m off the sand and our skipper runs you in by tender. Plan a 1-2NM hop from Puerto Banús — most stops are 10-20 minutes away. Do I need to book a berth in Puerto Banús for lunch? For our fleet under 15m, the skipper handles same-day berthing via VHF 09 on arrival; usually you get a slot within 20 minutes. For yachts over 20m (Mangusta 80, Canados 86, Ferretti 94) we pre-book the day before. Lunch-only stays of 2-3 hours are charged at the short-stay quayside rate, typically €40-€90 depending on length overall. How does a tender drop-off at a beach club work? Your skipper anchors 80-150m off the beach in 4-6m of water, then ferries guests ashore in the tender, two or three at a time. You step off in knee-deep water onto the sand and walk 30 seconds to the chiringuito. Bags, phones and shoes ride in a dry bag. The skipper waits aboard and collects you when you signal by phone or VHF. What is the dress code at Marbella beachfront restaurants? Chiringuitos like Trocadero Arena, Bono Beach and the Cabopino huts are swimwear-friendly at lunch — sarong or shirt over your costume is fine. Puerto Banús quayside venues (Cipriani, Astral) expect smart-casual: linen shirts, dresses, dry shoes. Nikki Beach enforces no swimwear at the table after 14:00, so pack a change of clothes if that is your stop. Can we eat onboard instead of going ashore? Yes — every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava and light snacks. For a full meal we can pre-order catering: tapas platters from €18 per person, sushi boxes around €35, or a paella delivered to the boat for €45 per head. Tell us the day before and we collect the order from Puerto Banús before departure. Drinks remain included on top. What is the best route for a half-day lunch charter? From Puerto Banús, head east to Río Verde for a quick swim, anchor off Trocadero Arena (1.5NM, 12 minutes), eat lunch, then continue to Cabopino dunes (another 6NM east) for an afternoon swim before returning. Total distance about 16NM round-trip, comfortably done in a 4h Tier A charter at €749 or in 6h for a more relaxed pace. Are children welcome at these restaurants? Most chiringuitos are family-friendly — Bono Beach and the Cabopino huts have shaded play areas and kids' menus around €12-€16. Trocadero Arena and Nikki Beach accept children at lunchtime but lean adult. Puerto Banús venues like Cipriani are fine for over-eights but not really set up for toddlers. Our boats carry life jackets in every child size from 10kg upwards. Can I combine a boat lunch with a sunset return

TITLE: 5 Best Snorkeling Spots in Marbella Accessible Only by Boat
DESCRIPTION: Discover the 5 best snorkeling spots in Marbella accessible only by boat. Crystal-clear coves, marine life, and 12+ charter boats from Puerto Banús. Book now.

Home › Guide › 5 Best Snorkeling Spots in Marbella Accessible Only by Boat By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella's coastline hides some of the Costa del Sol's most pristine snorkeling spots — but you can't reach them by foot. These secluded coves, with crystal-clear water and abundant marine life, are accessible only by private boat. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced snorkeler, our fleet of 18+ boats from Puerto Banús offers the perfect way to explore them. This guide covers the 5 best snorkeling spots in Marbella by boat, plus practical tips for your day on the water. 1. Cala del Faro – Best for Beginners Location: 2.5 NM west of Puerto Banús (10 minutes by powerboat) Depth: 2–6 metres Marine life: Saddled seabream, ornate wrasse, occasional octopus Cala del Faro is a small, sheltered cove tucked beneath the lighthouse at Punta de la Mona. Its gentle slope and sandy bottom make it ideal for inexperienced snorkelers. The water is usually calm, with visibility reaching 12 metres on a good day. Drop anchor from any of our boats — the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is a popular choice for small groups. Bring a mask and float effortlessly among the rocky edges where bream and wrasse gather. 2. Cala Cortés – Most Secluded Location: 6 NM west of Puerto Banús (25–30 minutes) Depth: 3–10 metres Marine life: Damselfish, painted comber, sea stars Cala Cortés is a narrow inlet framed by steep cliffs, accessible only by boat. There is no beach — just rocks and deep, clear water. This means fewer crowds and a more immersive experience. The rocky seabed is home to colourful damselfish and the occasional painted comber. For a comfortable day out, charter the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) which includes a free jet ski — perfect for zipping between this cove and the next. Water temperature here is typically 1–2°C cooler than the open sea, so a thin wetsuit is advisable in spring. 3. Cabo de Plata Marine Reserve – Best for Biodiversity Location: 4 NM east of Estepona (20 minutes from Puerto Banús) Depth: 5–15 metres Marine life: Red mullet, two-banded seabream, moray eels (in crevices) This protected marine area stretches along the coast between Estepona and Marbella. Fishing is prohibited here, so fish populations are denser and less wary. Snorkel along the rocky reef where red mullet and two-banded seabream feed in the shallows. The deeper sections (10–15 metres) are better for experienced snorkelers; stick to the 5–8 metre range for the best balance of light and life. Our Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a stable platform with a large swim platform for easy entry and exit. 4. Punta de la Mona – Best for Advanced Snorkelers Location: 3 NM west of Puerto Banús (15 minutes) Depth: 6–18 metres Marine life: Barracuda, damselfish, occasional stingray Punta de la Mona is the rocky headland that separates Marbella from the western coast. The underwater terrain drops off quickly, creating a wall-like environment where larger pelagic fish patrol. Barracuda are commonly seen in small schools, and stingrays rest on sandy patches at 12 metres. This spot requires good buoyancy control and confidence in deeper water. A Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) is a fast and agile choice to reach this spot quickly and spend more time in the water. 5. La Cala de Mijas – Best for Families Location: 5 NM east of Marbella Marina (25 minutes) Depth: 1–5 metres Marine life: Small bream, crabs, sea urchins (avoid) La Cala de Mijas is a wide, sandy bay with very shallow water close to shore. The gentle gradient makes it perfect for children or less confident swimmers. The seabed is mostly sand with scattered rocks, where small bream and crabs hide. Anchor the Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) or Lagoon 380 (11.6m, 10 pax) — both have shallow drafts and can get close to the beach. The water here warms up fastest in summer, often reaching 26°C by August. Snorkeling Charter Tips for Marbella Best time to go: May to October, with July and August offering peak visibility and water temperature (up to 26°C). Mornings (9 AM – 12 PM) are calmest with the least boat traffic. What to bring: Sunscreen (reef-safe, SPF 50+), a towel, a hat, and a thin wetsuit if you visit outside summer. We provide masks, fins, and life jackets on all charters. Boat recommendations: Small group (up to 9 pax): Astondoa 40 from €749/2h Medium group (up to 11 pax): Azimut 39 from €749/2h Large group with jet ski (up to 12 pax): Mangusta 80 from €4,719/4h (free jet ski included) All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees. Book your snorkeling adventure: Contact us via WhatsApp for any boat. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can book directly online. Explore all options on our main page . Frequently asked questions Do I need my own snorkeling gear? No. All our charters include snorkel masks, fins, and life jackets at no extra cost. Just bring your own towel and sunscreen. Which boat is best for a snorkeling trip with a group? For up to 12 guests, the Mangusta 80 (24m) or Ferretti 94 (29m) offer ample deck space and a free jet ski. For smaller groups, the Azimut 39 (11 pax) is a great value option. How long does it take to reach the snorkeling spots? Most coves are 15–30 minutes from Puerto Banús by powerboat. Cala del Faro is the closest at about 10 minutes; Cala Cortés is the furthest at around 30 minutes. Is swimming experience required? Yes, basic swimming ability is recommended. Life jackets are provided, but these are open-water coves without lifeguards. What is the best time of year for snorkeling in Marbella? May to October offers the warmest water (up to 26°C) and calmest seas. July and August have peak visibility, but mornings are less crowded. Can we combine snorkeling with other activities? Absolutely. Our boats have sun decks, a BBQ option (on select yachts), and a free jet ski on Mangusta 80 charters. Many guests stop for lunch at a chiringuito in Cabopino or Estepona. Are there any marine life restrictions in these coves? No fishing or spearfishing is allowed in the Cabo de Plata marine area. Touching or removing marine life is prohibited. We provide eco-friendly sunscreen recommendations. How do I book a snorkeling charter? Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can book directly online at €749/2h. TITLE: Marbella Snorkeling Tour by Boat: Best Coves and Gear Tips

Home › Guide › Marbella Snorkeling Tour by Boat: Best Coves and Gear Tips By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella's coastline hides some of the Costa del Sol's best snorkeling spots, and the best way to reach them is by private boat. A marbella snorkeling tour by boat lets you access secluded coves that are otherwise unreachable by land, with crystal-clear water, rocky reefs, and abundant marine life. Whether you're a first-timer or a seasoned snorkeller, this guide covers the top locations, what gear to bring, and how to book your trip from Puerto Banús. Why Choose a Boat for Snorkeling in Marbella? The beaches around Marbella are mostly sandy and shallow, but the best snorkeling happens near rocky headlands and coves. A private boat from Puerto Banús gives you direct access to spots like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, where visibility often reaches 15–20 metres on calm days. You avoid crowded shorelines, and your skipper anchors close to the rocks where fish gather. All our charters include a licensed skipper who knows the best spots based on wind and swell conditions. Best Coves for Snorkeling on a Marbella Boat Tour Here are the top coves our skippers recommend: Cala del Faro – Near Cabopino, this rocky cove has a small sandy beach and clear water with plenty of fish around the rocks. Depth ranges from 2 to 8 metres. Cala Cortés – West of Marbella, this sheltered cove offers calm water and a mix of sand and rock seabeds. Good for beginners. Punta de la Mona – A rocky headland with deeper water (up to 12 metres) and occasional sightings of stingrays and octopus. Cabopino Reef – Just off the Cabopino marina, this artificial reef attracts bream, wrasse, and small groupers. All these spots are within 30 minutes of Puerto Banús. Our skippers adjust the itinerary based on sea conditions and your preferences. Snorkeling Gear: What's Included and What to Bring We provide basic snorkeling masks and fins on every charter. For a more comfortable experience, we recommend bringing your own gear – especially if you need prescription lenses or prefer a dry snorkel. If you want to capture the underwater life, a GoPro or waterproof phone case is ideal. We also suggest a rash vest or UV shirt to protect against the strong Andalusian sun (SPF 50+ reef-safe sunscreen is essential). Avoid spray sunscreens, as they can harm marine life. Our Fleet: Which Boat Suits Your Snorkeling Trip? We have boats for every group size. For a small group of up to 5 guests, the Dubhe (8m) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m) are nimble and easy to anchor in tight coves. For up to 10 guests, the Fairline Targa 12m or Lagoon 380 catamaran offer stable platforms for snorkeling. For larger groups (up to 12), the Mangusta 80 (24m) includes a free jet ski, which is great for exploring shallower areas. All boats depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. Pricing for Snorkeling Charters Our most popular options for snorkeling tours are the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 , both from €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours (includes skipper, drinks, and VAT). The Mangusta 80 (available in grey or white) starts at €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. For other boats like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 , pricing is on request via WhatsApp. All prices include 21% Spanish IVA, fuel, insurance, and a full drinks and snacks package. What to Expect During Your Snorkeling Tour Your skipper will welcome you at Puerto Banús and brief you on the day's plan. After a short cruise (10–30 minutes) to your chosen cove, the skipper anchors and you can jump in from the swim platform. Snorkeling sessions typically last 30–45 minutes per cove. Between swims, you can relax on deck, enjoy drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), and light snacks. Most 4-hour tours visit 2–3 coves. For the best experience, book a full-day (8-hour) charter to explore further afield, including spots near Estepona or Sotogrande. Best Months for Snorkeling in Marbella Water temperatures peak from June to October, reaching 24–26°C in August. Visibility is best in July and August, often exceeding 15 metres. Spring (April–May) and autumn (September–October) are also good, with fewer crowds and water temperatures around 18–22°C. Winter months (November–March) are cooler (14–17°C) but still possible with a wetsuit (we can provide one on request). Book Your Marbella Snorkeling Tour by Boat Ready to explore the underwater world of the Costa del Sol? Browse our full fleet on the Boat Rental Marbella page, or contact us via WhatsApp for a personalised recommendation. We'll help you choose the perfect boat and cove for your snorkeling adventure. Frequently asked questions What is the best time of year for a marbella snorkeling tour by boat? The best months are June through October, when water temperatures range from 20°C to 26°C. July and August offer the calmest seas and best visibility, often exceeding 15 metres. Spring and autumn can also be good, but expect cooler water and more variable conditions. Which coves are included in a marbella snorkeling tour by boat? Popular coves include Cala del Faro (near Cabopino), Cala Cortés (west of Marbella), and the rocky areas around Punta de la Mona. All are within a 30-minute cruise from Puerto Banús. Our skippers know the calmest spots based on wind and swell. Do you provide snorkeling gear on the boat? Yes, we supply basic snorkeling masks and fins on all charters. For higher-quality gear or prescription masks, we recommend bringing your own. We also have a selection of GoPro mounts available upon request. Can beginners join a marbella snorkeling tour by boat? Absolutely. Our skippers provide a quick safety briefing and choose calm, shallow coves ideal for beginners. Life jackets are available. Many of our guests have never snorkelled before and enjoy spotting fish right from the swim platform. What marine life can I expect to see? Common sightings include damselfish, wrasse, bream, and occasional octopus. In deeper areas near Cabopino, you may see small stingrays or sea turtles. The rocky seabeds around Cala del Faro are home to colourful algae and starfish. How long should I book for a snorkeling-focused charter? We recommend at least 4 hours. This allows time to cruise to two or three coves, spend 30–45 minutes snorkelling at each, and enjoy lunch or drinks onboard. Shorter 2-hour trips work for a single cove if you're short on time. What should I bring for a marbella snorkeling tour by boat? Bring a towel, reef-safe sunscreen (non-spray to avoid damaging marine life), a rash vest or UV shirt, a hat, and a waterproof phone case. A GoPro or underwater camera is great for memories. We provide drinks and snacks. Can I combine snorkeling with other activities on the tour? Yes. Many guests combine snorkeling with paddleboarding (available on request), jet skiing (free on Mangusta 80 charters), or simply sunbathing on deck. Home › Guide › Plan a Romantic Proposal on a Marbella Sunset Cruise By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Asking the most important question of your life deserves a setting that matches the moment. A Marbella sunset cruise romantic proposal combines the golden hour glow of the Costa del Sol with the intimacy of a private boat, creating a memory that will last a lifetime. Whether you choose a sleek motor yacht or a cosy speedboat, the backdrop of Puerto Banús, La Concha mountain, and the Mediterranean Sea sets the scene perfectly. Why a Sunset Cruise Works for a Proposal The psychology of a sunset proposal is simple: warm light flatters everyone, the sea breeze calms nerves, and the privacy of a boat means no interruptions. Marbella's coastline offers calm waters and stunning views, from the glamorous Puerto Banús to the quieter Cala del Faro . With over 300 sunny days a year, the odds of a perfect sunset are in your favour. Our all-inclusive charters take the stress out of planning — you just bring the ring. Choosing the Right Boat for Your Proposal Your boat choice depends on how many guests you want and the vibe you're after. For an intimate two-person proposal, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) offers a small cabin and private deck — ideal for a quiet moment. The Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) is sporty and open, perfect for couples who want to feel the sea air. If you're planning a surprise with a few close friends, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) offer more space and a flybridge for panoramic views. For the ultimate luxury, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) includes a jet ski and a dedicated crew to ensure everything runs smoothly. All boats depart from Puerto Banús . Timing Your Sunset Cruise Sunset times in Marbella vary by season. In summer (June–August), the sun sets around 21:00–21:30, so a 19:00 departure gives you two hours of cruising before the golden hour. In spring and autumn, sunset is earlier (19:00–20:30), so aim to depart 1.5 hours before. Your skipper knows the best anchor spots — often near Cala Cortés or off the Golden Mile — where the sun dips behind La Concha mountain. Always check the marine forecast, though we rarely cancel due to weather. What’s Included in Your Charter Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. Spanish IVA (21%) is included in the price. For a proposal, we can arrange premium champagne, flowers, or a custom cake on request. The skipper can also help you time the moment — they'll slow the boat and point out the best view. Prices start from €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, up to €4,719 minimum 4 hours on the Mangusta 80 (which includes a jet ski). For other boats, contact us via WhatsApp for a quote. Best Spots Along the Coast for a Proposal Your skipper will navigate to one of several stunning locations depending on sea conditions and your preference. Cala del Faro offers a rocky cove with crystal-clear water and views back to Marbella. Cala Cortés is a quiet beach backed by cliffs, ideal for anchoring and popping the question. For a more glamorous backdrop, cruise past the superyachts of Puerto Banús and stop just offshore near the Río Verde estuary. If you want a longer cruise, head west towards Estepona or Sotogrande — both offer beautiful sunset views with fewer boats. The skipper will tailor the route to your wishes. Practical Tips for a Flawless Proposal Plan ahead: book at least 2–3 weeks in advance, especially for weekends and popular boats. Bring the ring in a secure case — the skipper can store it in the cabin if you want to surprise your partner. Dress in layers: even summer evenings can get breezy on the water. A light jacket or pashmina is a good idea. Consider a photographer — we can arrange one to capture the moment from a second boat or discreetly on board. Finally, don't forget to browse our full fleet and choose the boat that feels right for you. What to Do After the Proposal Once you've said yes, the cruise continues. Enjoy the remaining sunset light, toast with cava or champagne, and take in the twinkling lights of Marbella's coastline as darkness falls. Many couples choose to extend their charter into the evening, cruising back to Puerto Banús under the stars. We can arrange a dinner booking at a waterfront restaurant on the Golden Mile or in Puerto Banús itself. Just let us know — we're happy to help with recommendations. Spring and autumn sunsets are earlier, typically 19:00–20:30. Can I bring my own decorations or champagne on board? Yes, you are welcome to bring personal decorations, flowers, or a special bottle. You simply relax, enjoy the views, and focus on the moment. The skipper will also know the best quiet coves and sunset spots along the coast. Marbella enjoys over 300 sunny days a year, so bad weather is rare, but we always monitor forecasts closely. How far in advance should I book a proposal cruise? TITLE: Marbella Tuna Fishing Season: Best Boats &amp; Months to Book
DESCRIPTION: Plan your marbella tuna fishing season charter. Best months June–October, 12.5m Azimut 39 from €749/2h. Book your trip from Puerto Banús today.

Home › Guide › Marbella Tuna Fishing Season: Best Boats & Months to Book By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella is one of the top destinations for bluefin tuna fishing in the Mediterranean, and knowing the marbella tuna fishing season charter details is key to a successful trip. The season runs from June to October, with August and September offering the highest catch rates. Whether you're an experienced angler or a first-timer, our fleet from Puerto Banús provides the perfect platform for an unforgettable day on the water. When Is Tuna Season in Marbella? The bluefin tuna migration through the Strait of Gibraltar peaks between June and October. Sea surface temperatures rise to 22–26°C, drawing large schools close to the Costa del Sol. August and September are prime months, with fish weighing 100–300 kg common. Early morning charters (departing 7–8 AM) often yield the best results, as tuna feed actively near the surface. Best Boats for Tuna Fishing from Puerto Banús Our Tier A boats — the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) — are ideal for tuna fishing charters. Both depart from Puerto Banús, are equipped with spacious decks for rod handling, and include a licensed skipper who knows local hotspots like the waters off Cabopino or the Río Verde estuary. For a 4–8 hour trip, these boats offer excellent value and comfort. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours and go up to €2,299 for 8 hours, with all inclusions (skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA). For larger groups or longer expeditions, consider our Boat Rental Marbella hub for other options. What’s Included in Your Charter? Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You don’t need to worry about hidden costs — just bring your fishing gear or arrange it with the skipper. Many guests opt for trolling with lures or live bait, which the skipper can advise on. Typical Tuna Fishing Itinerary A standard 4-hour charter departs Puerto Banús at 8 AM. After a 20-minute cruise to the fishing grounds (5–10 NM offshore), you’ll spend 2.5–3 hours fishing. The skipper monitors sonar and bird activity to locate schools. Lunch or snacks are served on board, with time for a swim at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés if desired. Return by midday or extend to 8 hours for deeper exploration. Tips for a Successful Tuna Fishing Trip Book early : August and September slots fill quickly, especially for weekends. Bring sun protection : Sunscreen, hat, and polarised sunglasses are essential. Dress in layers : Mornings can be cool, even in summer. Communicate with the skipper : Share your experience level and target species. Consider a full-day charter : 8-hour trips allow you to follow the tuna as they move. Other Boats in Our Fleet for Fishing While the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 are our top fishing picks, other boats like the Fairline Targa 12m (12m, 10 pax) or Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) can also be arranged for fishing charters. For pricing on these, contact us via WhatsApp. All depart from Puerto Banús, ensuring easy access to the best fishing grounds. Local Fishing Spots Near Marbella Key fishing areas include the waters off Cabopino , the Río Verde estuary, and the deeper drop-offs near Estepona . Tuna often congregate around underwater ridges and current lines. The skipper will adjust the route based on real-time conditions, maximising your chances of a catch. Frequently asked questions What is the best time for tuna fishing in Marbella? The best time is from June to October, with peak activity in August and September when bluefin tuna migrate through the Strait of Gibraltar. Sea temperatures are warmest then, and catches are most consistent. Which boats are best for a tuna fishing charter in Marbella? The Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) and Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) are excellent choices for tuna fishing. Both depart from Puerto Banús, offer comfortable deck space, and include a licensed skipper who knows local fishing hotspots. How much does a tuna fishing charter cost in Marbella? A 2-hour charter on Tier A boats (Azimut 39 or Astondoa 40) starts at €749, with an 8-hour option at €2,299. Prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and 21% IVA. Longer trips for serious fishing are typically 4–8 hours. What is included in the charter price? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You just need to bring your fishing gear or arrange it with the skipper. Do I need a fishing license for tuna fishing in Marbella? Yes, a recreational fishing license is required for individuals. However, on our charters, the skipper can arrange a temporary license or include it in the package. Confirm at booking to ensure compliance. What type of tuna can I catch in Marbella? Bluefin tuna (Thunnus thynnus) is the main target, with specimens often exceeding 100 kg and occasionally over 300 kg. You may also encounter yellowfin tuna and bonito, especially in late summer. Where do the tuna fishing charters depart from? All our charters depart from Puerto Banús, the luxury marina in Marbella. The fishing grounds are 5–10 nautical miles offshore, accessible within 20–30 minutes from port. Can I combine tuna fishing with other activities? Absolutely. Many charters include time for swimming, snorkelling, or coastal sightseeing. The skipper can adjust the itinerary to balance fishing with relaxing at spots like Cabopino or Cala del Faro. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter + Malaga Airport Transfer Guide
DESCRIPTION: Plan your marbella yacht charter airport transfer with ease. Puerto Banús is 45 km from Málaga AGP. Book a private taxi or our 12-pax Mangusta 80.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter + Malaga Airport Transfer Guide By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Landing at Málaga Airport (AGP) and heading straight to a Marbella yacht charter is one of the smoothest transitions on the Costa del Sol. The airport is just 45 km from Puerto Banús — roughly a 40-minute drive. This guide covers every transfer option, from private taxis to rental cars, plus how to match your arrival time with the right boat from our fleet. Málaga Airport to Puerto Banús: Distance & Time Málaga-Costa del Sol Airport (AGP) is the main gateway for the Costa del Sol. From the arrivals hall to the marina at Puerto Banús, the distance is exactly 45 km. By car, the journey takes 35–50 minutes depending on traffic. The route follows the A-7 motorway along the coast, passing through Torremolinos, Benalmádena, and Fuengirola. Peak season (June–September) can add 10–15 minutes, especially on Friday afternoons and Sunday evenings. Transfer Options: Cost & Convenience Private taxi: €65–€90 one-way. Pre-book via Welcome Pickups or local companies. Door-to-door, no waiting. Shared shuttle: €15–€25 per person. Companies like ALSA or Suntransfers. 45–60 min journey. Good for solo travellers. Rental car: From €30/day with Avis or Hertz at AGP. Parking at Puerto Banús costs €20–€30/day. Useful if you plan to explore Marbella after the charter. Train + bus: Not recommended. Cercanías train goes to Fuengirola (€6), then a bus to Marbella (€3). Total 1.5–2 hours. Best Boats for a Same-Day Arrival Charter If you land in the morning (09:00–11:00), you have time for a full-day charter. Our Tier A boats — the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) — cost €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT. For a larger group (up to 12 pax), the Mangusta 80 (24m) is our flagship. It requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719 and includes a free jet ski. Three variants are available: grey, white, and the standard model. All depart from Puerto Banús. If you arrive in the afternoon (14:00–16:00), consider a sunset charter on the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) or the Fairline Targa 12m (12m, 10 pax). Pricing for these is available on request via WhatsApp. What to Do with Your Luggage Most of our yachts have small cabins or storage compartments where you can leave bags during the charter. For large suitcases, we recommend leaving them at your hotel or using a left-luggage service near the port. Puerto Banús has several luggage storage points, including Locker Marbella on Calle Ribera, about 5 minutes from our dock. Alternatively, ask your taxi driver to drop your bags at your accommodation before heading to the marina. Local Tips for a Seamless Day Book your taxi 24–48 hours in advance during high season (July–August). Arrive at the marina 15 minutes before departure to meet your skipper and stow belongings. If you want to stock up on extra snacks or drinks, ask your driver for a short stop at a Mercadona or Carrefour . After the charter, explore the Golden Mile or dine at one of the chiringuitos on the beach — many are a short walk from Puerto Banús. Frequently asked questions How far is Málaga Airport from Puerto Banús? Málaga-Costa del Sol Airport (AGP) is 45 km west of Puerto Banús. By car or taxi the journey takes 35–50 minutes depending on traffic. The route follows the A-7 coastal highway with views of the Mediterranean. What is the best way to get from Málaga Airport to my yacht charter? A private taxi or pre-booked transfer is the most reliable option, costing €65–€90. For groups of 4+, it's often cheaper than a shared shuttle (€15–€25 per person). Rental cars are available but parking in Puerto Banús costs €20–€30 per day. Can I book a transfer that includes a stop for groceries or drinks? Yes, private taxi drivers usually accommodate a brief stop at a supermarket or local shop. Let us know in advance and we can coordinate the timing so you arrive at the marina with everything you need. Do you offer airport pickup as part of the yacht charter? We do not include airport transfers in our standard charter packages. However, we can recommend trusted local taxi companies. You can also arrange a private transfer via the WhatsApp number on our site. What is the earliest time I can start a yacht charter after landing? Most charters begin at 10:00 or 12:00 from Puerto Banús. If your flight lands by 09:00, a 40-minute transfer gets you to the marina comfortably. For afternoon arrivals, 15:00 or 17:00 departures are also available. Are there luggage storage facilities at Puerto Banús? Puerto Banús has limited luggage storage. Most yachts have small cabins or storage areas where you can leave bags during the charter. For larger suitcases, consider leaving them at your hotel or using a left-luggage service near the port. Can I charter a yacht for a full day after a late flight? Yes, we offer 8-hour charters starting as late as 14:00. For example, the Astondoa 40 (€2,299) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (pricing on request) can depart at 14:00 and return at 22:00, giving you sunset cruising time. Do I need a visa or special documentation to charter a yacht in Spain? No special documentation is required for a standard charter. A valid passport or EU ID is enough. The yacht is operated by our licensed skipper. Non-EU visitors should ensure their passport is stamped upon entry to Spain. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/blog/marbella-yacht-charter-august-peak-season/
TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter in August: Peak Season Guide
DESCRIPTION: Marbella yacht charter in August: peak-season prices from €749/2h, 30°C sea, book 14+ days ahead. WhatsApp us to lock your slot.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter in August: Peak Season Guide By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers August is the busiest charter month in Marbella, with sea temperatures peaking at 23-25°C, near-zero rain risk and Puerto Banús quay packed by mid-morning. Our fleet runs at 85-95% capacity through the month, so this guide covers what to expect on price, weather, availability and which slots disappear first. If you want the short version: book mid-week if you can, lock weekends in 14+ days ahead, and start with the main Boat Rental Marbella hub for the full fleet overview. August pricing — what stays fixed and what doesn't Our headline pricing does not surge in August. The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 stay at €749 for 2 hours and €2,299 for 8 hours, all-inclusive of skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance and 21% IVA. The Mangusta 80 (both white and grey variants) remains €4,719 for the 4-hour minimum with a jet ski included free. Pricing for other boats — Pershing 46, Fairline Targa 12m, Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Maiora 26m and the rest — stays consistent year-round and is shared on WhatsApp request. What does change in August is the mix of inventory available. Discounted last-minute windows that appear in May or October do not exist in August: every slot books at standard rates. The cheapest entry-level slot — Tier A 2-hour mornings — disappears first. See the full fleet and pricing on the homepage for comparisons. Sea, weather and wind in August August is Marbella's warmest month. Air temperatures sit at 29-32°C inland and 26-29°C on the water, with sea temperatures averaging 23-25°C and peaking around 26°C in the third week. Rain risk is under 2% on any given day. The dominant pattern is glassy water from 09:00 to roughly 13:00, then thermal winds pick up. Morning (09:00-13:00): winds under 5 knots, flat water, best for paddleboarding and first-time guests. Afternoon (14:00-18:00): Poniente (west) or Levante (east) winds at 12-18 knots, occasionally 20+. Evening (18:00-21:30): winds drop, sea calms, sunset 21:15-21:45 early August. Skippers adapt routing daily. On Levante days the fleet runs west toward Estepona and Sotogrande for shelter; on Poniente days it heads east toward Cabopino and Cala del Faro. Cancellations for weather are under 3% across the month. Which boats book out first The booking order across August is predictable. Entry-tier boats go first because of the €749/2h price point, then the headline Mangustas, then the mid-size sport boats. Boat Capacity Typical lead time (weekends) Astondoa 40 / Azimut 39 9-11 pax 14-21 days Mangusta 80 (white & grey) 12 pax 10-14 days Lagoon 380 catamaran 10 pax 7-10 days Pershing 46, Fairline Targa 12m 10 pax 5-10 days Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Maiora 26m 12 pax 3-7 days For the full list, the Mariah SX21, Speedboat and Dubhe are good fallback options for smaller groups — they have higher mid-August availability than the flagship boats. Peak dates within August Three weekends are functionally booked solid 10+ days out: 8-9 August, 15-16 August (Asunción bank holiday weekend), and 22-23 August. The 15 August public holiday in particular pulls in domestic Spanish demand on top of international visitors. If you can shift to a Tuesday, Wednesday or Thursday, availability is 20-30% easier and you get the same boats and pricing. Time slots — which to choose August demands you pay attention to time of day. The 10:00-13:00 slot is the most-booked because of flat water and cooler temperatures. The 18:00-20:00 sunset window is the second most popular, especially for couples and small groups. The 13:00-16:00 midday slot is the easiest to book but the hottest — pick a boat with proper shade if you go midday (Mangusta 80, Canados 86, Azimut 58 Flybridge). What's included — no upsells Every August charter includes: Licensed Spanish skipper familiar with the Marbella coast All fuel and harbour fees Cold drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (chips, olives, cured ham) Insurance and full safety equipment 21% Spanish IVA There are no fuel surcharges, no skipper tips required, no port fees added at the end. WhatsApp is the fastest way to confirm — see the homepage for the booking number. Routes our skippers run in August August routing prioritises shaded anchorages and calm water. The standard half-day loop is Puerto Banús → Río Verde → Golden Mile → Cala del Faro → return, with two swim stops. Full-day charters extend to Cabopino in the east or Estepona in the west. Sunset trips usually stay between Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina with a 30-minute anchor stop off the Golden Mile for cava and the light show against La Concha. See more on the main site . Booking checklist for August Decide weekday vs weekend — weekday saves you 14+ days of lead time Pick your time slot — morning, midday, or sunset Confirm headcount (our boats run 5 to 12 pax) WhatsApp us with date + boat preference for instant availability Pay deposit to lock the slot — full payment on departure day Frequently asked questions How far in advance should I book a Marbella yacht charter for August? For August weekends, book 14-21 days ahead — our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) and the Mangusta 80 fleet sell out fastest on Fridays and Saturdays. Mid-week slots (Tuesday-Thursday) usually have availability within 5-7 days. For 15 August (Asunción bank holiday), aim for 30+ days lead time. WhatsApp us to check live availability. What is the sea temperature in Marbella during August? August is the warmest month, with sea temperatures averaging 23-25°C and peaking around 26°C in the third week. Air temperatures sit at 29-32°C inland and 26-29°C on the water. The water is warm enough for hours of swimming without a wetsuit — most guests jump in at every anchorage stop along the Golden Mile or off Cala del Faro. Which boats book out first in August? The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 go first because of the €749/2h entry price. The Mangusta 80 (white and grey variants) sells out next — they are the most photographed boats in Puerto Banús. Catamarans like the Lagoon 380 are popular for groups wanting stability. Larger yachts (Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Maiora 26m) have better mid-week availability. Are prices higher in August than other months? Our headline rates stay the same year-round — Tier A starts at €749/2h, Mangusta 80 from €4,719/4h. What changes in August is availability: discounted last-minute slots disappear and the cheapest morning windows go first. Booking early locks in the standard rate. Pricing on other boats stays consistent via WhatsApp quote. What is the weather like for sailing in August? Mornings are typically glassy with winds under 5 knots until around 13:00. Afternoon Poniente or Levante winds build to 12-18 knots, occasionally gusting 20+ on Levante days. Cancellations from weather are rare in August — under 3% of bookings. Skippers route around any chop, usually staying west toward Estepona on Levante afternoons. Can we anchor and swim during an August charter? Yes — every charter includes anchor stops for swimming. Popular August spots are Río Verde (5 minutes from Puerto Banús), Cala del Faro near Marbella town, and Cala Cortés toward Cabopino. Your skipper picks the calmest bay based on wind direction that day. Most guests do 2-3 swim stops on a half-day charter. Is sunset cruising worth it in August? Sunset is the best time slot in August — temperatures drop to a comfortable 25-27°C, winds calm down after 19:30, and La Concha mountain glows orange. Sunset sits at around 21:15-21:45 in early-to-mid August. The 18:00-20:00 evening slot is our most-booked window after weekend mornings. Cava and snacks included, as on every charter. What happens if there is a heatwave during my charter? August heatwaves (35°C+) actually make charters more comfortable — the breeze offshore is 5-8°C cooler than the beach. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Cancellation Policy Explained
DESCRIPTION: Need to cancel your yacht charter in Marbella? Our clear cancellation policy covers deposits, refunds, weather, and rebooking. Book with confidence today.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter Cancellation Policy Explained By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Planning a yacht charter in Marbella comes with excitement, but it's wise to understand the yacht charter cancellation policy Marbella before you book. Our policy is designed to be transparent and fair, covering deposits, refunds, weather issues, and rebooking options. Whether you're chartering a luxury boat from Puerto Banús or a smaller speedboat, knowing these terms helps you book with confidence. Below, we break down every aspect so you can plan stress-free. Deposit and Payment Schedule To secure your booking, we require a 50% deposit at the time of reservation. The remaining balance is due 7 days before your charter date. We accept bank transfers and major credit cards. This schedule applies to all boats in our fleet, from the Astondoa 40 (€749 for 2 hours) to the Mangusta 80 (€4,719 minimum 4 hours). Standard Cancellation Timeline Our cancellation policy is straightforward: More than 14 days before charter: Full refund of all payments. Between 7 and 14 days before charter: 50% refund of the total charter cost. Within 7 days of charter: No refund. All cancellations must be confirmed in writing via WhatsApp or email. We process refunds within 5 business days. Weather-Related Cancellations Marbella enjoys over 300 sunny days a year, but weather can change. If the skipper deems conditions unsafe (high winds, storms, or rough seas), you have two options: a full refund or rebooking at no extra cost. We monitor forecasts closely and will inform you at least 24 hours in advance when possible. This policy applies to all charters, including those departing from Puerto Banús . Late Arrival and No-Show Policy If you arrive late or fail to show up, the charter is treated as a cancellation with no refund. We recommend arriving 15 minutes before departure to complete check-in. If you foresee a delay, contact us immediately; we may be able to adjust the departure time subject to availability. Rebooking and Date Changes We understand plans change. If you need to cancel due to illness, family emergency, or work commitments, we offer free rebooking to a later date (subject to availability). Proof may be required for emergency-related rebookings. Date changes are free with at least 7 days' notice. This flexibility is part of our commitment to making your Marbella boat rental experience hassle-free. Mechanical Issues or Operator Cancellation In the unlikely event of a mechanical breakdown or issue with your chosen boat, we will either substitute a similar vessel (e.g., replacing an Azimut 39 with an Astondoa 40 ) or provide a full refund. Your safety and satisfaction are paramount. We never cancel lightly, and we'll communicate any changes immediately. How to Cancel or Modify Your Booking To cancel or modify, simply send a WhatsApp message or email to our team with your booking reference. We'll confirm receipt and guide you through the process. For urgent cancellations, WhatsApp is fastest. Keep your booking confirmation handy for reference. Frequently asked questions What is your yacht charter cancellation policy in Marbella? We require a 50% deposit to secure your booking. If you cancel more than 14 days before your charter, you receive a full refund. Cancellations between 7 and 14 days get a 50% refund. Cancellations within 7 days are non-refundable. All cancellations must be confirmed in writing. Do I get a refund if the weather is bad? Yes. If the skipper deems the weather unsafe for sailing, we offer a full refund or the option to rebook for another date at no extra cost. We monitor conditions closely and will notify you as early as possible. Can I rebook if I need to cancel due to illness or emergency? Absolutely. If you cancel due to illness or a family emergency, we allow rebooking to a later date without any additional fees. We just ask for proof, such as a doctor's note or official documentation. What happens if I arrive late or don't show up? If you are late or do not show up for your charter, it is treated as a cancellation with no refund. We recommend arriving at least 15 minutes before departure to avoid delays. How do I cancel my yacht charter in Marbella? You can cancel by sending a WhatsApp message or email to our team. We will confirm receipt and process your refund according to our policy. Make sure to include your booking reference. Is the deposit refundable if I cancel within 14 days? If you cancel between 7 and 14 days before the charter, we refund 50% of the total amount, which may be less than the deposit if the deposit was 50%. Cancellations within 7 days are not refundable. What if the boat has a mechanical issue? In the rare event of a mechanical breakdown, we will either replace the boat with a similar model (subject to availability) or offer a full refund. Your safety and experience are our priority. Can I change the date of my charter instead of cancelling? Yes, date changes are free of charge as long as we receive at least 7 days' notice. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Credit Card Fees Explained
DESCRIPTION: Avoid surprise charges on your Marbella yacht charter. We explain credit card fees, deposit policies, and how to pay with zero surcharge. Book now.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter Credit Card Fees Explained By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers When booking a Marbella yacht charter , understanding how credit card fees work can save you money. Many operators add a surcharge for card payments, and we’re transparent about ours. This guide breaks down exactly what you’ll pay, how to avoid extra costs, and what’s included in every charter from Boat Rental Marbella . Our Credit Card Surcharge Policy We accept Visa, Mastercard, and American Express for all bookings. A 2.5% surcharge applies to Visa and Mastercard transactions, and 3.5% for Amex. This covers the processing fees charged by our payment gateway. To avoid this, choose bank transfer (SEPA or international) or pay in cash on arrival – both with zero surcharge. How to Pay Without Extra Fees The simplest way to avoid the surcharge is a bank transfer. We provide our IBAN upon booking confirmation. Alternatively, bring euros in cash to the marina on the day. Cash payments are accepted for deposits and balances. For large amounts (e.g., a €4,719 Mangusta 80 charter), bank transfer is more practical. Deposit and Payment Schedule To secure your Marbella yacht charter , a 30% deposit is required. The remaining 70% is due 7 days before departure. If you pay the deposit by card, the surcharge applies only to that portion. The balance can be paid via bank transfer or cash to avoid further fees. All prices include 21% Spanish IVA, so no surprises at checkout. What’s Included in Every Charter Our prices are all-inclusive: a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and VAT. For example, an Astondoa 40 costs from €749 for 2 hours to €2,299 for 8 hours – no hidden charges. The Mangusta 80 (Tier B) starts at €4,719 for 4 hours, which includes a free jet ski. Why We Charge a Card Fee Payment processors take a cut of each transaction – typically 1.5–3% for cards. Rather than absorbing this cost into our base prices (which would raise them for everyone), we pass it on only to card users. This keeps our cash and bank-transfer prices lower. It’s a common practice among yacht charter operators in Marbella and across the Costa del Sol. Comparing Payment Methods Method Surcharge Best for Bank transfer (SEPA) 0% Deposits & balances Cash (euros) 0% Balance on day Visa/Mastercard 2.5% Quick deposits American Express 3.5% Large amounts Frequently Asked Questions Do you charge a credit card fee on Marbella yacht charters? Yes, a 2.5% surcharge applies for Visa and Mastercard payments, and 3.5% for American Express. We recommend bank transfer or cash to avoid this fee entirely. How can I pay without a credit card surcharge? Pay by bank transfer (SEPA or international) or cash in euros on arrival. Both methods incur zero additional fees. What deposit is required to book a yacht in Marbella? A 30% deposit is needed to confirm your reservation. The remaining 70% is due 7 days before departure. Deposits are fully refundable if cancelled 14+ days prior. Are there any hidden fees in your pricing? No. All our prices include the licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% Spanish IVA. What you see is what you pay, minus any optional extras. Can I pay with a debit card? Yes, debit cards are accepted and treated like credit cards – the same 2.5% surcharge applies for Visa/Mastercard debit. Bank transfer avoids this. What happens if I cancel my booking? Cancel 14 or more days before your charter for a full refund. Within 7-13 days, you lose 50% of the deposit. Less than 7 days, no refund. Do you accept PayPal or other digital wallets? Currently we only accept bank transfer, cash, and credit/debit cards via secure online link. PayPal is not available. Is the credit card fee refundable if I cancel? No, card processing fees are non-refundable and charged by the payment gateway. If you cancel, we refund the charter amount minus the card fee. Understanding Marbella yacht charter credit card fees helps you budget accurately. For a stress-free booking, opt for bank transfer or cash.
