TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter: Cash, Cards &amp; Currency Tips
DESCRIPTION: Plan your Marbella yacht charter with our currency exchange tips. Cash vs card at Puerto Banús. Avoid hidden fees &amp; enjoy a smooth booking.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter: Cash, Cards & Currency Tips By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Whether you're chartering an Astondoa 40 for a sunset cruise or a Mangusta 80 for a full day on the Mediterranean, knowing how to handle money in Marbella makes the experience smoother. This guide covers cash vs card, where to exchange currency, and how to avoid fees so you can focus on the sea. Cash or card for your Marbella yacht charter? Both have their place. For the charter fee itself, card is standard — we accept Visa, Mastercard, and bank transfers. For onboard tips, dock fees, and spontaneous purchases from beach vendors, cash is more practical. ATMs in Puerto Banús are plentiful but charge €3–5 per withdrawal, so take out larger amounts less often. Currency exchange rates in Puerto Banús Exchange offices in the marina offer convenience but at a cost — typically 3–5% worse than the interbank rate. A better strategy: withdraw Euros from a bank ATM using a fee-free travel card, or order currency from your home bank before departure. Avoid airport exchange desks entirely. What's included in our charter prices All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees. For example, our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours — all inclusive. The Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. Tipping the crew: cash is king If you're happy with the service, a 10–15% tip is customary. Cash ensures the crew receives the full amount. Some guests prefer to tip at the end of the day in an envelope — simple and appreciated. Foreign transaction fees on cards When paying by card, your bank may charge a foreign transaction fee of 1–3%. Check before you travel. Some travel credit cards have zero foreign fees — those are ideal for your Marbella yacht charter . For the charter deposit, we recommend a card with good fraud protection. Budgeting for extras Beyond the charter fee, budget for: Tips: 10–15% of charter cost Dock fees: €20–50 if you stop at a marina Water toys: jet skis, paddleboards (some included on Mangusta 80) Food upgrades: private chef or premium catering (ask us) Most extras are best paid in cash on the day. Best places to get Euros in Marbella For the best rates, use a bank ATM (not a Euronet or independent machine). Banks like Santander, BBVA, and CaixaBank have branches in Marbella and Puerto Banús. If you must use an exchange office, compare rates at two or three before committing. Frequently asked questions Can I pay for a Marbella yacht charter with a credit card? Yes, we accept major credit cards for deposits and final payments. However, some smaller operators may prefer bank transfer or cash. Always confirm before booking to avoid surprises. Should I bring cash or card for my yacht charter in Marbella? Both are useful. Card is best for the charter fee and larger expenses. Cash is handy for tips, dock fees, and buying from beach vendors. ATMs in Puerto Banús are convenient but charge 3–5€ per withdrawal. What currency is used in Marbella? The Euro (EUR) is the official currency. Most businesses accept cards, but cash is still king at smaller bars, market stalls, and for tipping the crew. Are there currency exchange offices in Puerto Banús? Yes, several exchange offices operate in Puerto Banús, but their rates are typically 3–5% worse than interbank rates. Use a bank ATM or a fee-free travel card for better value. Do I need to tip the yacht crew in cash? Cash tips are appreciated and ensure the crew receives the full amount. 10–15% of the charter fee is standard if you're happy with the service. Cards can be used but cash is simpler. What is the best way to get Euros for my yacht charter? Order Euros from your home bank before travel or use a fee-free ATM in Marbella. Avoid exchange desks at airports and marinas, as their rates are poor. Are there any hidden fees when paying for a yacht charter? Our prices include VAT (21%), skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. No hidden fees. If you pay with a foreign card, your bank may charge a foreign transaction fee (typically 1–3%). Can I pay in US Dollars or British Pounds for a charter? No, all charters are priced in Euros. Some operators may accept other currencies at a poor exchange rate. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/blog/marbella-yacht-charter-deposit-rules/
TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Deposit Rules: What to Expect
DESCRIPTION: Marbella yacht charter deposit rules explained: 30% to confirm, balance 7 days before, refunds, and 1 simple WhatsApp booking flow.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter Deposit Rules: What to Expect By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Booking a yacht in Marbella should not feel like signing a mortgage. Our deposit rules are deliberately simple: 30% to confirm the date, 70% seven days before departure, and a full refund if the captain calls it off for weather . Everything below explains the small print in plain English so you can budget honestly before you message us on WhatsApp — and so there are no surprises when the balance reminder lands in your inbox. If you are still comparing options, our main charter guide covers the fleet and routes first. The 30% booking deposit, explained The booking deposit on every Marbella yacht charter we run is 30% of the total charter price . It is paid the moment you confirm the date and boat — usually within an hour of the WhatsApp conversation — and it does one thing: it locks the slot. Until the deposit lands, the date stays open to other guests, which matters in July and August when Saturdays book three weeks out. The deposit is taken by SEPA bank transfer, card link, Revolut or Wise. Cash is only accepted for in-person last-minute walk-ups at Puerto Banús. On a Tier A boat like the Astondoa 40 at €749 for two hours, that is €224.70 to confirm. On a Tier B booking like the Mangusta 80 at €4,719 for four hours, the deposit is €1,415.70. Every figure on our site is already inclusive of Spanish IVA at 21%, so the percentage applies to the headline price, not a net figure plus tax. When the balance is due The remaining 70% is due seven calendar days before departure . We send a single reminder with the same payment link or IBAN you used for the deposit, and most guests settle it within a day. If you book inside that seven-day window — common for last-minute August trips or weekend changes of plan — the full amount is paid in one go on confirmation, usually within 24 hours. Why seven days? It is the cut-off where we commit fuel, provisioning, skipper rota and dock fees for your specific date. Paying late genuinely risks the slot during peak weekends, because the waiting list for boats like the Azimut 39 and Pershing 46 fills the same morning a date frees up. Is there a separate damage deposit? For Tier A boats — Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 — there is no extra damage deposit . Comprehensive hull and third-party insurance is already included in the price you see, alongside fuel, the licensed skipper, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava and light snacks. You pay the headline number and nothing else. On larger yachts — the Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Maiora 26m and Azimut 58 Flybridge — we may ask for a refundable security hold of €1,000 to €3,000 against a card. It is a pre-authorisation, not a charge: the funds are ring-fenced on your card but never actually leave your account unless there is a documented incident. The hold is released within 48 hours of return once the boat is checked in. Deposit amounts by boat tier Tier Example boats Charter price 30% deposit Security hold A — 2h slot Astondoa 40, Azimut 39 €749 €224.70 None A — 8h day Astondoa 40, Azimut 39 €2,299 €689.70 None B — 4h minimum Mangusta 80 (white/grey) €4,719 €1,415.70 €1,000-€2,000 Premium 24m+ Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Maiora 26m On request 30% of quote €2,000-€3,000 The Tier B Mangusta 80 price includes a free jet ski tow, which is unusual at this price point on the Costa del Sol. For premium 24m+ bookings, message us via the contact page for a fixed quote — we do not list hourly rates we cannot honour. Cancellation and refund timeline The refund window mirrors the way slots resell. The earlier you cancel, the easier it is for us to fill the date, so the more we can give back: 14+ days before departure: 100% of the deposit refunded. 7-13 days before: 50% of the deposit refunded. Inside 7 days: deposit is non-refundable — the boat is off the market. Weather cancellation by the captain: 100% refund or free reschedule. The weather rule matters. If forecast winds top 20 knots or the sea state hits 4+, our captain calls the trip — not you. You can take a full refund within five working days or move the booking to any open date in the next twelve months. Detail on how we read the Levante and Poniente winds is in our Marbella weather guide . Transfers, changes and group splits Plans change. If you need to move your date and give us seven or more days notice, we transfer 100% of the deposit to a new booking within twelve months, subject to availability and any price difference for peak dates — an April midweek deposit moving to an August Saturday will need topping up to the peak rate. Same-week transfers are case-by-case and depend on whether we can resell the original slot. For group bookings where one guest drops out, the deposit stays with the lead booker. We do not split deposits across multiple cards on a single charter — collect from your group in advance, then one person pays us. It saves chasing IBANs at 11pm the night before. Payment methods and invoicing We accept SEPA bank transfer in euros (instant or standard), card via Stripe link, Revolut, Wise and cash in person at Puerto Banús. We do not take cryptocurrency or PayPal — the fees and chargeback risk do not work for this price point. Every payment generates a Spanish invoice with IVA broken out at 21% , emailed within 24 hours. Companies booking for client entertainment can request the invoice in their CIF, which is useful for Spanish VAT recovery. If you are flying in and want the deposit on a corporate card but the balance on a personal one, just tell us — we can switch the link between payments. Full breakdown of what is and is not included is in the inclusions guide . Booking flow on WhatsApp, step by step From first message to confirmed slot is usually under an hour: Message us with date, group size and rough budget. We reply with two or three suitable boats and a fixed all-in price. You pick the boat — we send a payment link for the 30% deposit. Deposit lands, slot is locked, you receive a written confirmation with the meeting point at Puerto Banús. Seven days before, we send the balance reminder. On the day, you meet the skipper at the dock, no further paperwork. That is the whole flow. No PDF contracts, no broker chains, no scope creep on the price. If anything in the deposit rules is unclear before you book, ask first on WhatsApp — we would rather answer a question now than refund a misunderstood booking later. For a fuller picture of routes and timing, the half-day routes piece shows where the deposit actually takes you. Frequently asked questions How much is the booking deposit for a Marbella yacht charter? Our booking deposit is 30% of the total charter price, paid by bank transfer or card link once you confirm the date and boat on WhatsApp. The deposit secures the slot — without it, the date stays open to other guests. The remaining 70% is due 7 days before departure. For last-minute bookings inside 7 days, the full amount is paid in one go, usually within 24 hours of agreeing the booking. Is there a separate damage or security deposit on top of the price? On Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) there is no extra damage deposit — comprehensive insurance and Spanish IVA are already baked into the €749-€2,299 price. On larger yachts like the Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94 or Canados 86, we may ask for a refundable security hold of €1,000-€3,000 against a card, released within 48 hours of return if the boat comes back in the same condition it left. When is the balance due before departure? The balance — 70% of the charter price — is due 7 calendar days before your departure date. We send a reminder with the same payment link or IBAN you used for the deposit. If you book inside that 7-day window, the full price is settled on confirmation. TITLE: Dietary Restrictions on Yacht Catering in Marbella: What You Need to Know
DESCRIPTION: Learn how to handle dietary restrictions on yacht catering in Marbella. Our fleet offers custom menus for allergies, gluten-free, vegan &amp; halal. Book now.

Home › Guide › Dietary Restrictions on Yacht Catering in Marbella: What You Need to Know By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers When chartering a yacht in Marbella, the standard catering—water, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, and light snacks—covers most guests. But if you or your party have specific dietary restrictions, you need to know what's possible, what's not, and how to plan ahead. This guide covers everything from gluten-free and vegan to halal and kosher catering on our fleet departing from Puerto Banús. Our Standard Inclusions and How They Adapt Every charter from our fleet includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA, and a catering package: bottled water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks (typically olives, crisps, nuts, and finger sandwiches). These can be adjusted for dietary needs at no extra cost when requested 48 hours in advance. For example, we swap standard crisps for gluten-free crackers, or replace sandwiches with vegetable platters for vegan guests. Gluten-Free and Coeliac-Safe Options Gluten-free catering is straightforward in Marbella. Our suppliers in Estepona and Marbella provide gluten-free bread, pasta, and snacks. For coeliac guests, we ensure no cross-contamination by using separate preparation surfaces and sealed packaging. Sample gluten-free menu: grilled chicken skewers, quinoa salad, fresh fruit, and gluten-free brownies. Let us know at booking so we can confirm the menu. Vegan and Plant-Based Catering Vegan options are popular and easy to arrange. Our standard snack platter can be replaced with hummus, baba ganoush, vegetable crudités, olives, and fresh fruit. For longer charters (4+ hours, such as on the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94), we offer mains like vegetable paella, lentil salads, and dairy-free desserts. All drinks included are vegan-friendly (check cava—most is, but we confirm). Halal Catering on Board Halal meat and poultry are available from certified suppliers in Marbella and Málaga. We can arrange halal chicken skewers, lamb kofta, and mixed grills. All alcohol is served separately and clearly labelled. Non-alcoholic beverages are included as standard. Please specify halal requirements at the time of booking so we can coordinate with our caterers. Kosher and Other Religious Dietary Needs Kosher catering requires more lead time due to sourcing. We can arrange pre-packaged kosher meals from Málaga or Marbella, or work with a kosher caterer for fresh options. Expect a lead time of at least one week. Costs may be slightly higher (€15–€30 supplement per person). We also accommodate Hindu (vegetarian) and Jain (no onion/garlic) diets on request. Allergies and Intolerances We handle nut, dairy, egg, shellfish, and soy allergies. Our standard snacks are nut-free upon request. For severe allergies, we recommend bringing your own EpiPen—our crew are first-aid trained but not medical professionals. We label all food clearly and avoid shared utensils. Just tell us at booking, and we'll send a detailed ingredient list before departure. How to Book with Dietary Restrictions To ensure your dietary needs are met, contact us via WhatsApp or email at least 48 hours before your charter. We'll send a menu options sheet and confirm everything in writing. For complex needs (multiple allergies, kosher), a phone call is best. All our boats—from the compact Speedboat to the spacious Ferretti 94 —depart from Puerto Banús, and provisioning is handled locally. Frequently Asked Questions Can you accommodate gluten-free or coeliac-safe meals on a Marbella yacht charter? Yes, we can arrange gluten-free catering for all our charters. Our standard light snacks include gluten-free options, and we can provision a full gluten-free menu (e.g., salads, grilled fish, rice dishes) from trusted suppliers in Marbella and Estepona. Please inform us at least 48 hours in advance so we can avoid cross-contamination. Is halal food available on yacht charters in Marbella? Halal catering can be arranged on request. We work with halal-certified caterers in Puerto Banús and Marbella to provide meat, poultry, and snacks that meet halal standards. All alcohol served is separate and clearly labelled. Let us know your requirements at booking so we can coordinate the menu. Can you provide vegan or plant-based meals on a boat trip? Absolutely. Vegan options are popular and easy to arrange. Our standard snack platter can be replaced with hummus, vegetable crudités, fresh fruit, and plant-based wraps. For longer charters (4+ hours), we can prepare a full vegan menu including mains like grilled vegetables, quinoa salads, and dairy-free desserts. What about kosher food on a yacht charter in Marbella? Kosher catering is possible but requires more lead time due to sourcing. We can arrange kosher-certified meals from suppliers in Marbella or Málaga, including pre-packaged kosher meals and fresh produce. Please contact us at least one week in advance to discuss your specific kashrut level and budget. Do you cater for nut allergies or other serious food allergies? Yes, we take allergies seriously. Our standard snacks are nut-free upon request, and we can avoid all common allergens (dairy, eggs, shellfish, soy) with advance notice. We recommend bringing your own EpiPen if you have severe allergies, as our crew are trained in basic first aid but not medical professionals. Is there an extra charge for dietary-restricted menus on your boats? For most dietary modifications (e.g., gluten-free snacks, vegan mains, halal meat), there is no extra charge if requested at least 48 hours before departure. Premium bespoke menus (e.g., kosher certified, multiple-course gourmet) may incur a small supplement, typically €15–€30 per person. We will confirm any extra costs before you book. Can I bring my own food if I have specific dietary needs? Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks aboard any of our charters. Our crew can store and serve your provisions. However, our standard included catering already covers most dietary requirements, so check with us first—you may not need to bring anything. How do I communicate my dietary restrictions for a yacht charter in Marbella? Simply mention your dietary needs when you enquire via WhatsApp or email. We will send you a menu options sheet and confirm the final menu 48 hours before sailing. For complex restrictions (e.g., multiple allergies, kosher), we recommend a phone call to ensure nothing is missed. Planning a charter with specific dietary needs? TITLE: Marbella Dolphin Watching &amp; Snorkeling Combo: Best Boat Charters
DESCRIPTION: Book a Marbella dolphin watching and snorkeling combo boat charter from Puerto Banús. See wild dolphins, swim in crystal coves, and enjoy included drinks from €749.

Home › Guide › Marbella Dolphin Watching & Snorkeling Combo: Best Boat Charters By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella’s coastline offers some of the best dolphin watching in the Mediterranean, and pairing it with snorkeling in secluded coves makes for an unforgettable day on the water. A Marbella dolphin watching and snorkeling combo boat charter from Puerto Banús lets you spot wild dolphins just 2–5 nautical miles offshore, then drop anchor at crystal-clear spots like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance — so you can relax and enjoy the experience. Why choose a dolphin watching and snorkeling combo in Marbella? Marbella’s position between the Sierra Blanca mountains and the Alboran Sea creates a rich marine ecosystem. Common bottlenose dolphins and striped dolphins are sighted year-round, often within 15–30 minutes of leaving Puerto Banús . Adding snorkeling turns a simple boat trip into a full day of exploration — you get wildlife, swimming, and the Costa del Sol’s famous coastal scenery in one charter. Unlike crowded tourist boats, our private charters give you flexibility. Your skipper can adjust the route based on dolphin activity and sea conditions, ensuring you maximise both sightings and swim time. Our fleet for dolphin watching and snorkeling We offer boats from 6.4m to 29m, accommodating 5 to 12 passengers. For dolphin watching, a stable hull and good visibility are key — here are the top choices: Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) — €749/2h to €2,299/8h. Shaded flybridge, ideal for families. Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) — same pricing. Open cockpit for dolphin spotting. Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) — from €4,719 minimum 4h. Includes a free jet ski for snorkel spots. All boats have shaded areas, a bathroom, and a sound system. See our full boat rental fleet in Marbella for details. Best snorkeling spots near Marbella The Costa del Sol has several coves accessible only by boat. Our skippers know the calmest, clearest spots on any given day: Cala del Faro — rocky coastline, clear water, good for spotting fish and octopus. Cala Cortés — sandy seabed, sheltered from wind, ideal for beginners. Cabopino — near the dunes, with seagrass beds attracting marine life. Estepona coast — quieter waters, often dolphin-rich on the way back. Snorkeling equipment (mask, snorkel, fins) is provided free on all charters. Water temperatures range from 18°C in May to 26°C in August. What’s included in your charter Every booking includes: Licensed skipper (English and Spanish speaking) Fuel for the full trip Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts, fruit) Snorkeling equipment Full insurance and Spanish IVA (21%) No hidden costs. The Mangusta 80 tier also includes a jet ski for free during the charter. Sample itinerary: 4-hour dolphin and snorkel combo Here’s a typical 4-hour trip from Puerto Banús: 10:00 — Board at Puerto Banús. Safety briefing and welcome drink. 10:15 — Depart towards Cabopino or Estepona, depending on dolphin reports. 10:45 — First dolphin sighting. Skipper slows down for photos and observation. 11:30 — Anchor at Cala del Faro for snorkeling. 12:15 — Snacks and drinks served. Relax on deck. 12:45 — Cruise back along the Golden Mile, views of La Concha mountain. 13:00 — Return to Puerto Banús. For an 8-hour charter, add a second snorkel stop and a lunch break at a beach club in Sotogrande or Estepona. No extras. Best time for dolphin watching in Marbella Dolphins are present year-round, but the best conditions for a combo charter are May through October. Sea temperatures are warmest (22–26°C), and the sea is calmest in the morning. July and August are peak season, so book early. Winter charters are still enjoyable — water drops to 15–17°C, but wetsuits can be arranged. Dolphin sightings actually increase in cooler months as baitfish move closer to shore. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter for F1 Grand Prix Weekend: What to Expect
DESCRIPTION: Book a Marbella yacht charter for the F1 Grand Prix weekend. Watch the race from the sea off Puerto Banús. Prices from €749/2h. Reserve now.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter for F1 Grand Prix Weekend: What to Expect By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Booking a Marbella yacht charter for the F1 Grand Prix weekend is one of the most memorable ways to experience the race. Instead of battling crowds on the shore, you anchor just off the circuit with a cold drink in hand, watching the cars scream past from the water. Here’s exactly what to expect, from the best viewing spots to which boat suits your group. Why Watch the F1 Grand Prix from a Yacht in Marbella? The F1 Grand Prix circuit hugs the coastline east of Marbella, with several turns visible from the sea. From a yacht, you get a front-row seat without the noise, queues, or ticket prices of the grandstands. The water offers a natural grandstand: you can anchor 200–300 metres offshore and watch the race unfold. Plus, you have your own bar, sun deck, and skipper to handle everything. It’s a luxury experience that combines the thrill of F1 with the relaxation of a day on the Mediterranean. Which Boats Are Available for the F1 Weekend? Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, just 2 NM from the circuit. Here’s a quick overview of the most popular choices for race day: Tier A (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39): €749/2h, €2,299/8h. Perfect for couples or small groups up to 11 pax. Includes skipper, drinks, snacks, and VAT. Tier B (Mangusta 80, white/grey variants): From €4,719/4h. These 24m yachts come with a free jet ski and accommodate 12 guests. Ideal for a premium experience. Other boats: Azimut 58 Flybridge, Ferretti 94, Pershing 46, and more. Pricing on request via WhatsApp. All include the same inclusions. For full specs, see our Marbella yacht charter fleet . Best Viewing Spots from the Water The most popular anchoring area is off Cala del Faro , a small cove east of the circuit. From here, you see Turns 1–3 and the main straight. Another good spot is near the circuit’s sea wall, where you can watch the pit lane action. Your skipper will know the day’s maritime exclusion zone and anchor you safely outside it. The Coast Guard patrols actively, so expect to stay at least 200 metres from the circuit. What’s Included in Every Charter? All our charters include: a licensed English-speaking skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You don’t pay extra for anything on the day. Just bring sunscreen, a hat, and your camera. Food can be brought aboard (no outside alcohol, please). Timing Your Day on the Water Race day typically starts at 15:00. We recommend departing Puerto Banús by 12:00 for a 2-hour charter (€749) or 11:00 for a 4-hour charter (from €2,299). This gives you time to cruise to the viewing area, anchor, and enjoy pre-race drinks. The atmosphere builds as more yachts arrive, and you’ll hear the roar of engines from the circuit. After the race, you can cruise back to port or extend your day with a swim stop at Cala Cortés or Cabopino . Practical Tips for the F1 Weekend Book early: F1 weekend is Marbella’s busiest. Reserve 4–8 weeks ahead. Dress code: Smart casual. No high heels on board (safety first). Parking: Paid car parks near Puerto Banús. Better to take a taxi. Weather: Late May/early June averages 25–28°C, calm seas. If bad weather looms, we’ll reschedule or refund. Frequently Asked Questions How close can my yacht get to the F1 circuit during the race? You can anchor within 200–300 metres of the circuit’s sea wall, depending on the day’s maritime exclusion zone. The best views are off Cala del Faro, where you see Turns 1–3 and the main straight. The harbour master usually allows spectator anchoring outside the race’s safety perimeter. What is the best time to depart for the F1 Grand Prix from Marbella? Aim to depart Puerto Banús by 12:00 for a 15:00 race start. This gives you 2–3 hours to cruise to the viewing area, anchor, and settle in with drinks. For the full atmosphere, a 4-hour charter (12:00–16:00) covers the build-up and race finish. Which boat is best for a group of 10 people for the F1 weekend? The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) or the Fairline Targa 12m (12m, 10 pax) are ideal. Both have spacious decks and flybridges for unobstructed views. For a larger group, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) offers luxury and space. Are there any restrictions on anchoring near the circuit? Yes, the Spanish maritime authority enforces a 200-metre exclusion zone around the circuit during the race. You must stay outside this zone. The Coast Guard patrols actively. Your skipper will know the legal anchoring spots and will comply with all regulations. Can I bring my own food and drinks on the charter? Our charters include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. You are welcome to bring your own food, but no outside alcohol is permitted. The skipper will store and serve your food if needed. What happens if the weather is bad on the F1 weekend? Marbella averages 25–28°C in late May/early June with calm seas. If strong winds or storms are forecast, we will contact you 24–48 hours ahead to reschedule or cancel with a full refund. Safety is our priority. How far in advance should I book for the F1 Grand Prix? We recommend booking 4–8 weeks ahead. F1 weekend is the busiest of the year in Marbella, and our fleet books out quickly. Early booking also gives you the best choice of boats and time slots. Is there parking near Puerto Banús for my car on race day? Puerto Banús has several paid car parks within 200 metres of the marina. Expect higher demand and prices during F1 weekend. We suggest arriving by taxi or rideshare to avoid parking stress. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Group Size Rules Explained
DESCRIPTION: Discover Marbella yacht charter group size limits for boats from Puerto Banús. Capacity ranges from 5 to 12 guests. Check rules and book your ideal yacht today.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter Group Size Rules Explained By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers When planning a Marbella yacht charter , one of the first questions is: how many people can come aboard? The answer depends on the boat's certified capacity—not on how many seats you think it has. Every vessel in our fleet at Boat Rental Marbella has a strict guest limit set by Spanish maritime law, insurance, and safety equipment. Exceeding it is not allowed, even for a short trip. Here's exactly what you need to know about Marbella yacht charter group size limits for boats departing from Puerto Banús. Why Group Size Limits Exist Each boat is issued a certificado de navegabilidad (navigation certificate) by the Spanish Dirección General de la Marina Mercante. This document specifies the maximum number of persons allowed on board, factoring in life raft capacity, stability calculations, and escape routes. Our charters are insured and licensed under these exact numbers. No skipper will exceed them, and no amount of haggling changes the physics. For a stress-free day out, always book for your exact guest count. Maximum Capacity Per Boat in Our Fleet Our largest yachts—Mangusta 80 (white and grey), Azimut 58 Flybridge, Ferretti 94, Canados 86, K80, and Maiora 26m—all carry a maximum of 12 guests . The Azimut 39 takes 11, the Astondoa 40 takes 9, and the Lagoon 380 catamaran holds 10. Smaller vessels like the Dubhe (8m) and Mariah SX21 (6.4m) are limited to 5 guests . The Speedboat and Red Tide take 6. There is no boat in our fleet that allows more than 12 passengers. Tier A and Tier B Pricing & Capacity Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, including skipper, drinks, and VAT. The Astondoa 40 holds 9 guests; the Azimut 39 holds 11. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) require a minimum 4-hour charter at €4,719 and include a jet ski. These boats hold 12 guests. For any other vessel, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp. Children Count as Guests There is no child discount or infant exemption. Every person on board—including babies and toddlers—counts toward the guest limit. Life jackets are provided for all sizes, but the seat count is fixed. If your group includes children, include them in your total headcount when booking. A family of four with two young children, for example, would need a boat with a capacity of at least 4 guests. What If Your Group Is Larger Than 12? If you have 13 or more guests, you cannot all fit on a single boat from our fleet. The solution is to charter two boats simultaneously. We can coordinate two departures from Puerto Banús—for instance, a Mangusta 80 (12 guests) plus an Azimut 39 (11 guests), giving combined capacity for up to 23. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange a multi-boat charter. This is common for corporate events, wedding parties, and large family reunions. Boats for Small Groups (2–6 Guests) If your group is small, you don't need a 24-metre superyacht. The Dubhe (8m, 5 guests) and Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 guests) are perfect for couples or small families. The Speedboat (7m, 6 guests) and Red Tide (11m, 6 guests) offer a sportier ride. These boats are more affordable and easier to book at short notice. All depart from Puerto Banús and include the same inclusions: skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance. How to Confirm Your Guest Count When you book through Boat Rental Marbella , you'll be asked for the exact number of guests. We recommend confirming this number 48 hours before departure. If your group size changes, let us know immediately. We can switch you to a different boat if capacity permits, but last-minute increases may not be accommodated. The skipper will check the count before casting off—no exceptions. Frequently Asked Questions What is the maximum group size for a yacht charter in Marbella? The maximum is 12 guests on most large yachts like the Mangusta 80, Azimut 58 Flybridge, Ferretti 94, and Canados 86. Smaller boats like the Dubhe or Mariah SX21 take up to 5 guests. All limits are set by the boat's safety certificate and cannot be exceeded. Do children count towards the group size limit? Yes, children of any age count as guests. There is no separate child rate or infant exemption. Each person on board, including babies, occupies a seat and is included in the maximum capacity. Can I add extra guests if we pay more? No. The capacity is a legal safety limit enforced by Spanish maritime authorities and our insurance policy. No additional payment can override it. If your group is larger than 12, consider booking two boats or a larger vessel (none larger than 12 in our fleet). Which boats in your fleet hold the most guests? Twelve guests is the maximum across our fleet. Boats with 12-guest capacity include the Mangusta 80 (white and grey), Azimut 58 Flybridge, Ferretti 94, Canados 86, K80, and Maiora 26m. The Azimut 39 holds 11, and the Astondoa 40 holds 9. What is the smallest boat available for a group? The smallest is the Dubhe (8m) and Mariah SX21 (6.4m), both taking up to 5 guests. The Speedboat (7m) and Red Tide (11m) take 6 guests. These are ideal for couples or small families wanting a short trip from Puerto Banús. How do I book for a group of 13 or more? You would need to charter two boats simultaneously. We can coordinate departures from Puerto Banús on two vessels, such as a Mangusta 80 (12 guests) and an Azimut 39 (11 guests), giving you combined capacity for up to 23. Contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote. Are there any boats that allow more than 12 guests? No. Under Spanish recreational charter regulations, vessels under 24m in length are typically limited to 12 passengers plus crew. Our largest boats (Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Maiora 26m) are all under this threshold and thus capped at 12 guests. What happens if we show up with more guests than allowed? The skipper will refuse to depart until the group size matches the boat's capacity. No refund is given for no-shows or overbooking. To avoid disappointment, confirm your exact guest count when booking and double-check it 48 hours before departure. Ready to book? Check our full fleet at Boat Rental Marbella and choose the right size for your group. For any questions about capacity, pricing, or multi-boat charters, message us on WhatsApp. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

The flybridge offers panoramic views – perfect for group photos. This is the most popular hen party activity for a relaxed, elegant vibe. 3. Swimming and Snorkelling Stops All our charters include stops for swimming. The skipper will anchor at Cala del Faro (a quiet cove 10 minutes from Puerto Banús) or Cala Cortés (15 minutes east). Charter the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) for a 4–6 hour cruise to Estepona (15 NM west). Hen Party Games on the Bow The spacious bow of the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94 is perfect for hen party games – think truth or dare, Mr & Mrs quizzes, or a scavenger hunt. The crew can suggest games and provide props (cards, pens) on request. With the Mediterranean breeze and unlimited drinks, the bow becomes your private party zone. 8. Customisable Duration and Extras All our charters are flexible. The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours, ideal for a quick celebration. For a full day (8 hours), the price is €2,299. Want to add a water slide? A paddleboard? A photographer? We can arrange most extras – just ask via WhatsApp when booking. All prices include IVA (21%), so no surprises. Frequently Asked Questions What is the best boat for a hen party in Marbella? For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m, €4,719 minimum 4h) includes a free jet ski – perfect for party fun. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a spacious flybridge for socialising. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) is a budget-friendly option. Can we bring our own food and decorations on board? Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food, cake, and decorations (balloons, banners, etc.) on any charter. Our crew will help set up. However, glass bottles are not recommended for safety – use plastic cups provided. How far in advance should we book a hen party yacht? Last-minute availability exists but is limited. What happens if the weather is bad on our booking day? If the captain deems conditions unsafe (high winds, storms), we reschedule at no cost or offer a full refund. We monitor forecasts closely and will contact you 24–48 hours before departure. Most days in Marbella are calm, especially morning charters. Is there a DJ or music system on the boats? All our boats have Bluetooth stereo systems – you can connect your own playlist. For larger parties, you can request a portable speaker upgrade (free). We do not provide a DJ, but you can hire one independently; let us know in advance. Can we stop for swimming or snorkelling during the charter? Absolutely. The skipper will anchor at calm spots like Cala del Faro (10 min from Puerto Banús) or Cala Cortés (15 min). We provide snorkelling masks on request. Swimming stops are included in all charters – just tell the crew your preference. What is the minimum age for a hen party yacht charter? Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter in July: Peak Season Tips & Availability By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers July is the heart of summer on the Costa del Sol, and a Marbella yacht charter July peak season booking is one of the most sought-after experiences along the Mediterranean. With sea temperatures hitting 24°C, long sunny days, and vibrant marinas, demand for boats departing Puerto Banús skyrockets. This guide covers everything you need to secure your ideal charter — from pricing and availability to insider tips for navigating the busiest month of the year. Why July is Peak Season for Marbella Yacht Charters July brings the highest concentration of visitors to Marbella, drawn by the Golden Mile’s glamour, Puerto Banús nightlife, and near-guaranteed sunshine. The combination of school holidays, European summer breaks, and perfect sailing conditions means that Marbella yacht charter July peak season inventory is limited. Boats like the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 often get booked weeks in advance. If you want to explore the coastline from Cabopino to Estepona, early planning is non-negotiable. Swim in turquoise water, then head back for lunch at a chiringuito. West to Estepona: Sail 12 NM to Estepona’s marina, passing the dunes of Cabopino. Anchor for a swim stop at Playa del Cristo before returning. Full-day to Sotogrande: For a longer trip (8 hours), head 25 NM to Sotogrande. Enjoy lunch at a beach club and spot the La Concha mountain backdrop on the return. What to Expect: Weather, Crowds, and Sea Conditions July in Marbella averages 30°C air temperature, 24°C sea temperature, and 10–11 hours of sunshine. The levante wind can pick up in the afternoon, so morning charters are smoother. Puerto Banús is busiest on weekends and during the Marbella International Film Festival (usually late June/early July). Midweek charters offer more space and quieter anchorages. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Late Arrival Policy Explained
DESCRIPTION: Understand the Marbella yacht charter late arrival policy. Learn what happens if you&#x27;re late, cancellation terms, and how to avoid losing your deposit. Book with confidence.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter Late Arrival Policy Explained By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Planning a Marbella yacht charter means sun, sea, and luxury — but what happens if you're running late? Whether it's traffic on the Golden Mile, a delayed flight into Málaga, or a long lunch at a Puerto Banús beach club, arriving on time matters. Our late arrival policy is designed to be fair to all guests while keeping the schedule smooth for everyone. Here's exactly what you need to know before you step aboard. Why Punctuality Matters for Your Marbella Yacht Charter Every charter departs from Puerto Banús, and our skippers plan the route based on the booked duration. A 2-hour trip on the Astondoa 40 (€749 for 2 hours) might head to Cala del Faro, while an 8-hour charter on the Azimut 39 (€2,299 for 8 hours) can reach Estepona or Cabopino. Late departure means less time on the water, and the skipper cannot extend the trip into the next booking slot. Our policy protects both your experience and the next group's. The 30-Minute Rule: Grace Period and No-Show We allow a 15-minute grace period after your scheduled departure. During this window, the skipper waits at the dock. If you arrive between 15 and 30 minutes late, the charter starts late but ends at the original time — effectively shortening your trip. After 30 minutes without any contact, the charter is treated as a no-show, and the full fee is forfeited. To avoid this, call or WhatsApp us as soon as you know you'll be delayed. Cancellation Policy at a Glance Time Before Charter Refund 14+ days 50% refund 7–13 days No refund (credit for future booking may be offered) Less than 7 days No refund, no credit No-show (30+ min late, no contact) Full fee forfeited These terms apply to all boats in our fleet, from the Mangusta 80 (€4,719 minimum 4 hours) to the Ferretti 94 (pricing on request). We recommend travel insurance to cover unexpected delays. Late Return to Port: Overtime Charges Returning the yacht late can incur overtime fees of €150–€300 per hour, depending on the boat. The skipper will remind you of the return time 30 minutes before. If you're enjoying a swim at Cala Cortés and lose track of time, the extra charge covers the crew's overtime and any knock-on effects for the next charter. Always agree on extensions with the skipper beforehand. Force Majeure: Weather and Port Closures If severe weather, port closures, or other force majeure events delay your arrival or departure, we handle each case individually. Contact us immediately — we may reschedule your charter or offer a full refund if sailing is unsafe. Our priority is your safety and satisfaction. For example, if a storm rolls in from La Concha, we'll adjust the plan rather than risk a rough sea. How to Avoid Late Arrival Issues Plan to arrive at Puerto Banús 15 minutes before your charter time. Factor in traffic on the A-7 or AP-7, especially in summer. Use our WhatsApp number for real-time updates if delayed. Book a longer charter (e.g., 4 or 8 hours) to have more flexibility. Choose a boat with a larger capacity for groups — the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) is popular for full-day trips. What's Included in Every Charter All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Whether you're on the Lagoon 380 or the Pershing 46 , these inclusions are standard. Late arrival doesn't affect what's included — but it does reduce your time to enjoy them. Frequently asked questions What happens if I arrive late for my Marbella yacht charter? If you arrive late without informing us, the skipper will wait up to 30 minutes. After that, the charter may be cancelled with no refund. We recommend calling or WhatsApp-ing us as soon as you know you'll be delayed. If we can adjust the departure time without affecting other bookings, we'll try to accommodate you. Is there a grace period for late arrivals? Yes, we offer a 15-minute grace period after the scheduled departure time. During this window, the skipper will wait. Beyond 15 minutes, the charter time may be reduced to ensure a safe return. After 30 minutes without contact, the charter is treated as a no-show. Can I get a refund if I cancel my charter due to a late arrival? No, cancellations due to late arrival are not eligible for a refund. However, if you cancel at least 14 days before the charter, you receive a 50% refund. Within 14 days, no refund is given. We recommend travel insurance to cover unexpected delays. What counts as a 'no-show' for a Marbella yacht charter? A no-show is when you fail to arrive at the Puerto Banús departure point within 30 minutes of the booked time and do not contact us. In this case, the full charter fee is forfeited with no refund or rescheduling option. Always keep our WhatsApp number handy. Does bad weather affect the late arrival policy? Yes. If severe weather (e.g., storms, high winds) forces a port closure or makes sailing unsafe, we contact you to reschedule or cancel with a full refund. Late arrival due to weather is handled case-by-case; call us immediately to discuss options. What if I'm late returning the yacht to port? Returning late may incur overtime charges: typically €150–€300 per hour depending on the boat. The skipper will brief you on the return time before departure. Delays beyond 30 minutes without prior agreement may also affect the next charter and result in additional fees. Can I extend my charter if I arrive late? No, the charter duration is fixed from the booked start time. If you arrive late, the end time remains the same, so you effectively lose that time. Extensions are possible only if the boat is available and you agree to pay the additional hourly rate on the spot. How do I contact you if I'm running late? Call or message us on WhatsApp at +34 123 456 789 (or the number on your booking confirmation). We monitor messages up to departure time. Provide your booking name and estimated arrival time, and we'll do our best to adjust. For more tips on planning your trip, read our Marbella yacht charter guide . ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Minimum Age Policy: Who Can Sail
DESCRIPTION: Learn the marbella yacht charter minimum age policy. From age 2 on private charters to 18+ for rentals, our guide covers who can sail from Puerto Banús.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter Minimum Age Policy: Who Can Sail By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Planning a yacht charter in Marbella with family or friends? One of the first questions you'll ask is about the marbella yacht charter minimum age policy. Whether you're bringing toddlers, teenagers, or elderly parents, knowing who can sail is essential for a smooth day on the water. At Boat Rental Marbella , all our charters depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and insurance. Here's everything you need to know about age limits, safety rules, and requirements. Minimum Age for Passengers The minimum age for passengers on any private charter in Marbella is 2 years old . Infants under 2 are not permitted for safety reasons. Children aged 2 to 11 are welcome but must wear a life jacket at all times while on deck. We provide life jackets for all sizes, including toddlers. For boats like the Dubhe (5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax), space is limited, so consider larger vessels like the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) or Ferretti 94 (12 pax) for family groups. Minimum Age for Renters and Skippers To book a yacht charter, the primary renter must be 18 years or older . A valid ID or passport is required at check-in. If you plan to rent a boat without a skipper (self-drive), you need a valid boating licence recognised in Spain and be 18+. However, all our charters come with a licensed skipper, so no licence is needed—just relax and enjoy the Costa del Sol coastline. Age Rules for Different Boat Types Our fleet includes boats of various sizes and layouts, each with its own suitability for different age groups. Here's a quick overview: Boat Max Pax Best for Dubhe (8m) 5 Couples or small families with older kids Astondoa 40 (12.5m) 9 Families with children (stable deck) Lagoon 380 (11.6m) 10 Families, catamaran stability Mangusta 80 (24m) 12 Large groups, all ages Ferretti 94 (29m) 12 Luxury, elderly-friendly boarding For elderly guests or those with mobility issues, the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94 offer easier boarding and more space. Always inform us when booking so we can recommend the best option. Safety Requirements for Children Spanish maritime law requires that children under 12 wear life jackets on deck at all times. Our crew will provide properly sized jackets and brief all guests on safety procedures. We also recommend sun protection (SPF 50+, hats, UV clothing) as the sun on the Mediterranean can be intense, even in spring. The water temperature off Marbella ranges from 16°C in April to 24°C in August. Booking with Infants or Toddlers As noted, infants under 2 are not permitted. For toddlers aged 2–5, we provide infant life jackets and can arrange kid-friendly snacks (fruit, juice boxes) on request. Boats with shaded areas, like the Lagoon 380 catamaran, are ideal for keeping little ones cool. All charters include soft drinks and water, so no need to bring your own. What About Elderly Passengers? There is no maximum age for passengers. However, some boats have steep gangways or ladder access. We recommend the Maiora 26m or Canados 86 for their wide boarding platforms and stable rides. If a guest has limited mobility, please call us on WhatsApp before booking to ensure the boat is suitable. The crew can assist with boarding but cannot carry passengers. Group Bookings and Mixed Ages Planning a birthday, wedding, or corporate event with a mix of ages? Our larger boats like the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) or Ferretti 94 (12 pax) are perfect. All guests must follow the same age rules. For example, a group with a 3-year-old and a 70-year-old is fine—just ensure the child wears a life jacket and the elder can board safely. We can also arrange catering for all ages. Frequently asked questions What is the minimum age for a yacht charter in Marbella? The minimum age for passengers on private charters is 2 years old. Infants under 2 are not permitted due to safety regulations. Children aged 2–11 are welcome but must wear life jackets on deck and be supervised by an adult at all times. Can teenagers charter a boat without an adult in Marbella? No. Anyone under 18 cannot rent a boat or be the primary booking holder. A responsible adult aged 18+ must be present on the charter and sign the contract. Teenagers are welcome as passengers when accompanied by a parent or guardian. Do I need a boating licence to charter a yacht in Marbella? No. All our charters include a licensed skipper, so no licence is required for guests. You simply relax and enjoy the trip. If you wish to rent a boat without a skipper, you must be 18+ and hold a valid Spanish or international boating licence. Is there a maximum age for passengers on Marbella yacht charters? There is no upper age limit. However, some boats have steep steps or limited accessibility. We recommend older guests or those with mobility concerns choose a boat with easy boarding, such as the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94, and inform us in advance. Are life jackets provided for children on your charters? Yes, we provide life jackets for all passengers, including children. Kids under 12 must wear them at all times while on deck. We also carry infant life jackets for children aged 2–5. Please let us know the ages of your children when booking. Can I bring a baby under 2 on a private charter? No. For safety reasons, infants under 2 years old are not permitted on any of our charters, even on private bookings. This policy applies to all boats in our fleet, regardless of size or duration. What happens if a guest is under 18 and we don't have an adult? The charter cannot proceed. All bookings require a responsible adult aged 18+ to be present on the boat and sign the liability waiver. If you are under 18, you must be accompanied by a parent, guardian, or another adult over 18. Do you offer family-friendly charters for kids in Marbella? Absolutely. Families are welcome. Boats like the Lagoon 380 (catamaran) and Astondoa 40 are popular with families due to stable decks and shaded areas. We provide soft drinks, snacks, and can arrange kid-friendly catering on request. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Minimum Age Policy 2026
DESCRIPTION: Discover the Marbella yacht charter minimum age policy for 2026. Guests must be 18+ to book; children welcome on board. Check individual boat limits.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter Minimum Age Policy 2026 By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Planning a yacht charter in Marbella with family or friends? One of the first things to check is the Marbella yacht charter minimum age policy. Whether you're celebrating a milestone birthday or taking the kids out for a day on the Costa del Sol, we've got clear rules to keep everyone safe and legal. Here's everything you need to know for 2026. Minimum Booking Age: 18 Years Old The lead booker for any private charter must be at least 18 years old on the day of departure. This applies to all boats in our fleet, from the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) to the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). We require a valid passport or national ID card at check-in. No exceptions – if you're under 18, a parent or guardian must make the booking and be present throughout the charter. Children Welcome as Passengers There is no minimum age for passengers. Children of any age are welcome on board, provided they are accompanied by a responsible adult. We carry life jackets in multiple sizes, including for infants and toddlers. Just let us know the ages and sizes of children in your group when you book, and we'll have the right equipment ready. All charters include a licensed skipper, so you can relax while the kids enjoy the ride. Passenger Limits by Boat Each boat has a maximum passenger capacity set by Spanish maritime regulations. This includes children and infants. Here are the limits for our most popular vessels: Astondoa 40 (12.5m) – 9 pax Azimut 39 (12.5m) – 11 pax Mangusta 80 (24m) – 12 pax Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) – 12 pax Bandido (11.5m) – 8 pax Dubhe (8m) – 5 pax Fairline Targa 12m – 10 pax Ferretti 94 (29m) – 12 pax Lagoon 380 (11.6m) – 10 pax Pershing 46 (14m) – 10 pax Mariah SX21 (6.4m) – 5 pax Speedboat (7m) – 6 pax Check each boat's page on our Marbella yacht charter hub for exact numbers. Safety Equipment for All Ages Every charter includes life jackets for all passengers , plus safety briefing from the skipper. For families with young children, we recommend bringing sun protection, hats, and extra snacks. The boat has shaded areas and cabins where little ones can nap or escape the midday heat. Our skippers are experienced with family groups and can suggest calm stops like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for safe swimming. No Upper Age Limit We welcome guests of all ages – there is no upper age limit. Whether you're celebrating a 70th birthday or enjoying a retirement trip, you're welcome on board. We recommend that elderly passengers or those with medical conditions consult their doctor before sailing, especially if seas are choppy. The skipper can adjust the itinerary to stay close to the coast and avoid rough water. Booking Process for Families When you book a Marbella yacht charter with children, simply note the ages of all passengers in the booking form or WhatsApp message. We'll prepare the right safety gear and can even suggest a family-friendly itinerary. All our boats depart from Puerto Banús , with easy parking and access. You can also ask about adding a jet ski or water toys – some boats like the Mangusta 80 include a jet ski free of charge for 4-hour+ charters. What About Teenagers? Teenagers aged 13–17 are welcome as passengers, but they cannot be the lead booker. If you're planning a celebration for a 16th or 17th birthday, a parent or guardian must book and accompany the group. We often host teenage groups for swimming, snorkelling, and exploring the coast from Marbella Marina to Estepona . The skipper ensures everyone stays safe and respects the boat rules. ID Requirements at Check-In On the day of your charter, the lead booker must present a valid passport or national ID card to confirm their age. We do not accept photocopies or digital photos. If the booker cannot prove they are 18 or older, the charter will be cancelled without refund. For passengers, we do not routinely check ID unless there is a dispute about age – but it's always good to carry identification. There is no minimum age for passengers – children of any age are welcome on board, provided they are accompanied by a responsible adult. Can I charter a boat if I am under 18? All bookings are made in the adult's name and they are responsible for the group. Are children allowed on yacht charters in Marbella? Yes, children of all ages are welcome on our private charters. We provide life jackets in various sizes, including for toddlers and infants. Parents are responsible for supervising children at all times, especially near the water and on the swim platform. Do you provide life jackets for children? Yes, we carry life jackets suitable for children of different ages and weights. Please let us know the ages and sizes of children in your group when booking so we can have appropriate equipment ready. We also have buoyancy aids for younger passengers. However, we recommend that elderly passengers or those with medical conditions consult their doctor before boarding, as the boat may move in choppy conditions. If the booker cannot prove they are 18 or older, the charter will be cancelled without refund. Always ensure the booking is made by an adult. TITLE: Yacht Charter Marbella: Music &amp; Noise Curfew Rules
DESCRIPTION: Learn Marbella yacht charter noise curfew rules. Puerto Banús enforces 23:00 quiet hours. Plan your party charter with our fleet. Book now.

Home › Guide › Yacht Charter Marbella: Music & Noise Curfew Rules By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Planning a yacht charter in Marbella with music and dancing? You need to know the local noise curfew rules. Puerto Banús, the main departure point for our fleet, enforces quiet hours from 23:00 to 09:00. This means loud parties must wrap up by 23:00, or you risk fines. In this guide, we explain the regulations, how they affect your charter, and how to plan a perfect day on the water without breaking the rules. Puerto Banús Noise Curfew: The Basics Puerto Banús marina operates under Andalusian port regulations that limit noise levels after 23:00. The permitted decibel level is around 55 dB—roughly the volume of a normal conversation. This applies to music, engines, and any amplified sound. Our licensed skippers are trained to enforce these rules, so you'll never accidentally breach them. Daytime charters (e.g., 10:00-18:00 or 12:00-20:00) are completely unaffected. For example, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax, departs Puerto Banús) is perfect for a daytime cruise with music. Book an 8-hour slot from 10:00 to 18:00 and enjoy the Mediterranean without curfew concerns. See our full fleet . Why the Curfew Exists The curfew protects coastal residents and other boaters from excessive noise. Marbella's Golden Mile and Puerto Banús have luxury apartments and hotels close to the marina. After 23:00, the area is expected to be quiet. Fines for non-compliance start at around €600 and can be higher for repeat offences. The skipper is legally responsible, so they will ask you to lower music if needed. For a hassle-free evening, consider a sunset charter that returns to port by 23:00. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax, departs Puerto Banús) offers a flybridge perfect for watching the sun go down over La Concha mountain. Daytime Party Charters: No Curfew Worries If you want loud music and a party atmosphere, book a daytime charter. Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, including skipper, drinks, and snacks. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, departs Puerto Banús) is our premium party boat, with a minimum 4-hour charter at €4,719 that includes a free jet ski. All daytime charters avoid the curfew entirely. Popular daytime anchorages include Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, where you can swim, jet ski, and dance. Just remember that after sunset (around 20:00-21:00 depending on season), music should be lowered even in open anchorages. Evening and Sunset Charters Sunset charters (e.g., 19:00-23:00) are a popular choice for couples and small groups. You can enjoy dinner, drinks, and music as the sun sets over the Mediterranean. However, the curfew applies from 23:00, so your charter must end by then. The Fairline Targa 12m (12m, 10 pax, departs Puerto Banús) is ideal for a sunset cruise with its open cockpit and smooth ride. After 23:00, you cannot remain in the marina with music playing. Some clients opt to anchor outside the marina (e.g., off Cabopino) but still risk complaints from nearby properties. We recommend returning to port on time. Other Marinas and Anchorages Marbella Marina and Cabopino have similar curfews (23:00-09:00). Estepona and Sotogrande marinas may have slightly different hours, but your skipper will brief you on departure. In open anchorages like Cala del Faro, there are no formal rules, but common sense applies: after 22:00, keep music low to avoid disturbing other boats. For a quiet overnight, consider the Lagoon 380 (11.6m, 10 pax, departs Puerto Banús) – a catamaran perfect for anchoring in a peaceful cove without loud music. How to Book a Curfew-Compliant Charter When booking, specify your preferred time slot. Our team will recommend the best boat and duration. For party groups, we suggest daytime 8-hour charters (e.g., 10:00-18:00) on the Mangusta 80 (White) or Azimut 39 . For sunset lovers, book a 4-hour evening slot (19:00-23:00) on the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax). All charters include a licensed skipper who knows the local rules. You can focus on having fun while we handle compliance. Check our full fleet . Frequently Asked Questions What is the noise curfew for yacht charters in Marbella? In Puerto Banús, noise curfew runs from 23:00 to 09:00. During this time, music must be kept low (around 55 dB) to avoid disturbing other vessels and coastal residents. Daytime charters are unaffected. Can I play loud music on a yacht charter in Marbella? Yes, during daytime hours (before 23:00) you can enjoy music at reasonable levels. Our boats have sound systems, but the skipper will ensure volumes stay legal. After curfew, only background music is allowed. What happens if I break the noise curfew on my charter? Port authorities can issue fines starting around €600 for a first offence. The skipper is responsible and may terminate the charter early if rules are ignored. Always follow the crew's guidance. Are there quiet anchorages near Marbella for evening charters? Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are popular spots, but after sunset low music is required. For a peaceful evening, consider a sunset cruise that returns to port before 23:00. Do all Marbella marinas have the same noise rules? Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and Cabopino all enforce similar curfews (23:00-09:00). Estepona and Sotogrande may have slightly different hours; your skipper will brief you on departure. Which boats are best for a party charter in Marbella? The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is ideal for daytime parties with its spacious deck and included jet ski. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a flybridge for music and dancing. Can I book a charter after 23:00 in Marbella? No, our charters depart from Puerto Banús and must return by curfew. The latest standard slots are sunset charters (e.g., 19:00-23:00). For late-night options, consider a private event at a beach club. What is included in a Marbella yacht charter with you? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% IVA. Why October Works for a Marbella Yacht Charter October in Marbella delivers 24°C air temperatures, 8–10 hours of daily sunshine, and sea conditions that are flat-calm more often than not. The prevailing westerly breeze stays light (10–15 knots), and swell rarely exceeds half a metre. That means no chop, no white-knuckle rides — just smooth cruising from Puerto Banús to the coves east of Marbella or west to Estepona. The water is warm enough for swimming until late afternoon, and the beaches along the Golden Mile are empty enough that you’ll feel like you have the coast to yourself. Cala Cortés (30 min east) — A small, sheltered beach near Cabopino. Great for a relaxed lunch stop. Estepona (12 NM west, 1 hour) — The old town marina offers a charming stop for tapas. The sea is usually flat on this route in October. Sotogrande (20 NM west, 1.5 hours) — For a full-day trip, head to the luxury marina; the beach clubs are quieter in October. Marbella Marina (15 min east) — A short hop from Puerto Banús, ideal for a sunset cocktail on the water. Other boats are priced on request, but expect similar discounts of 10–20%. What to Wear and Bring in October Daytime is warm enough for shorts and a T-shirt, but bring a light jacket or sweater for the evening cruise back to port. The sun is still strong, so sunscreen (SPF 50) and sunglasses are essential. Swimwear and a towel are a must — you’ll want to jump in. If you’re prone to seasickness, note that October seas are generally calm, but a mild antihistamine or ginger tablets are a sensible precaution. Most days have calm seas, making it ideal for cruising the Costa del Sol. Which boats are available for October charters from Puerto Banús? Our full fleet operates year-round from Puerto Banús. The remaining 50% is due 7 days before your charter date . For last-minute bookings made within that week, the full amount is taken at the time of booking. This structure gives you flexibility while securing your preferred vessel and slot. Accepted payment methods We accept two payment methods: bank transfer and credit/debit card (Visa, Mastercard, Maestro). All card payments are processed through a secure, PCI-compliant gateway – we never store your card details. Bank transfers are handled via standard secure banking channels. We do not accept cash or American Express. If you need to split the payment across two cards, just let us know at booking and we'll arrange it. Our fleet includes 18 boats ranging from the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) for intimate trips to the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) for grand celebrations. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks – no hidden extras. With transparent pricing and secure payment, you can book with confidence. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter for Photoshoots: Lighting and Backdrops
DESCRIPTION: Plan a marbella yacht charter photoshoot in Puerto Banús with tips on golden-hour light, coastal backdrops, and our fleet of 12 boats from €749/2h.

Home › Guide › Marbella Yacht Charter for Photoshoots: Lighting and Backdrops By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella yacht charter photoshoot opportunities are abundant thanks to 300+ sunny days, a fleet of 12 well-maintained boats, and a coastline that shifts from luxury marina to wild coves in under 20 nautical miles. Whether you are shooting editorial fashion, product campaigns, or personal portraits, the combination of Mediterranean light and a floating set is hard to beat. This guide covers lighting conditions, backdrop choices, boat selection, and practical logistics so you can plan a shoot that delivers without surprises. Why Marbella for a Yacht Charter Photoshoot Marbella sits on the Costa del Sol at roughly 36.5°N latitude, which means sunrise and sunset light paths are predictable and long. The sea breeze keeps temperatures moderate even in July (average 28°C), and the absence of industrial haze means colour saturation is naturally high. Puerto Banús, our departure point, is a working marina with 915 berths, lined with white buildings and palm trees. Within 15 minutes of leaving the harbour, you reach open water where the only backdrop is the Sierra Blanca mountains or the African coast on clear days. Best Lighting Conditions for Your Shoot Golden hour in Marbella runs from roughly 07:00 to 09:00 and 19:00 to 21:00 between May and October. During these windows, the sun sits low, casting long shadows and warming skin tones without the need for heavy diffusion. For a high-fashion editorial look, position talent on the bow of a boat like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) with the sun behind them for rim light. Overcast days (common in November–February) provide soft, even light ideal for beauty or product shots. Avoid midday in July unless you want high-contrast shadows—use a reflector or shoot under the flybridge overhang. Choosing the Right Boat for Your Photoshoot Our fleet spans from the Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) for intimate shoots to the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) for multi-room setups. For a balance of deck space and clean lines, the Mangusta 80 (Grey or White) offers a 24m platform with a large sun pad and flybridge. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) has a raised helm station that frames shots well. If you need a jet ski in frame, the Mangusta 80 charters include one free. All boats depart from Puerto Banús, so you can shoot the marina architecture on your way out. Top Backdrop Locations Along the Coast From Puerto Banús, head west to Cabopino (3 NM) for natural dunes and a red-and-white lighthouse. Continue to Cala del Faro (7 NM) for a rocky cove with turquoise water. Further west, Cala Cortés (10 NM) offers a pebble beach and pine forest backdrop. For a luxury marina setting, stay inside Puerto Banús and shoot against the superyacht dock or the La Concha mountain. Eastbound, Marbella Marina (4 NM) has a long promenade with restaurants and palm trees. Each location is within a 20-minute cruise at 15 knots. Practical Tips for a Smooth Photoshoot Charter Book a minimum of 2 hours to cover one location and lighting setup. For multiple backdrops, choose a 4-hour or 8-hour charter. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours and include drinks and snacks. The Mangusta 80 requires a 4-hour minimum at €4,719 but includes a jet ski. Communicate your equipment list (tripods, drones, lighting) at booking so the skipper can allocate deck space. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and 21% IVA—no extra charges. Bring a spare battery and memory card; the light changes fast. What to Expect from the Crew Your skipper is there to navigate, anchor safely, and manage the boat. They can help position the vessel for optimal light or backdrop but are not stylists or photographers. If you need a second crew member for assistance, we can arrange one. The crew will serve drinks and snacks as agreed, but you are free to bring your own catering. For large productions, consider booking the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) for indoor and outdoor shooting spaces.
