TITLE: What to Bring on a Marbella Boat Charter: Packing Checklist
DESCRIPTION: What to bring on a boat charter in Marbella: full packing checklist. Sunscreen, towels (some), swimwear, layers — and what the boat already provides so you don&#x27;t double-pack.

Home › Guide › What to Bring on a Marbella Boat Charter: Packing Checklist By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella charters are pretty well-stocked — operators provide most of what you'd think to pack. This list keeps it tight: bring what's actually useful, skip what's redundant. Pack this Swimwear — worn or in a small dry bag. Bring two if it's a full day. Hat with chin strap — wind takes baseball caps overboard at 12 knots. Polarised sunglasses — reduces glare for spotting fish and dolphins; non-polarised burns your eyes by hour three. Sunscreen 50+, reef-safe — apply before boarding (15 min for activation), reapply every 90 minutes. The on-board sun doubles by reflection off the water. A light layer — t-shirt or rash vest. The 15-knot apparent wind underway is cooler than it sounds, and the foredeck sun without cover gets brutal mid-afternoon. Cash for the tip — €20-€50 for a great half-day, €50-€100 for a full day, 10% of charter price on luxury yachts. Skippers prefer cash. Phone in a Ziploc bag — instant waterproof pouch for swim stops. €0, works perfectly. ID — passport or EU national card, charter contracts ask for one. Reusable water bottle — the boat has water; refill rather than burn through plastic. BYO drinks if you want them — cava, prosecco, wine and spirits all welcomed on private charters. Operators chill the fridge for you. The boat already has Beach-size towels, fresh per guest Snorkel masks and fins in standard sizes An inflatable donut or paddleboard (or both) Fridge with water, soft drinks, ice Light snacks: fruit, crisps, almonds, biscuits Bluetooth speaker — bring your playlist on your phone Life jackets, all sizes First-aid kit, sun cream samples (don't rely on these) Skip this Big inflatable toys — operator provides better ones. Cooler box — boat's fridge handles drinks. Heavy shoes — bare feet on deck, sandals for the marina walk only. Full-frame camera with no waterproofing — sea spray will reach it; a phone in a Ziploc covers 95% of shots. Hotel slippers, plastic bags, single-use anything — Spanish marinas police plastic litter aggressively. For specific trips Sunset cruise: add a light jumper — temperature drops 4-6°C after the sun sets. See sunset cruise Marbella . Fishing charter: closed-toe shoes for the rod-fighting position, a long-sleeve UPF shirt for the offshore run. See fishing boat rental Marbella . Gibraltar day trip: passport (Gibraltar is outside the EU), warmer layer for the longer offshore run. See Gibraltar day trip by boat . Kids on board: additional UPF rash vests, junior snorkel masks, change of clothes for the ride home. See boat rental with kids in Marbella . Day-of essentials Sunscreen applied 15 minutes before boarding. Phone charged to 100% (or a power bank). Cash in your pocket (not the bag — easier to find for the tip). Light breakfast inside you — empty stomach + sea motion = nausea risk. Arrive 15 minutes before departure with everyone in the group. For broader options on which charter fits your day, the boat rental Marbella hub compares every boat type. Frequently asked questions Are towels provided? Yes on every reputable Marbella charter — beach-size towels, fresh per guest. Bring a microfibre one if you prefer your own. Do I need water shoes? Not for normal swim stops over sand. Useful for rocky snorkel entries (Cala del Faro) or beach club tender rides. Bare feet are standard on deck. Should I bring my own snorkel? Masks and fins are provided. Bring your own if you have a prescription mask or want guaranteed fit. Do I need to bring food? No on charters with included snacks (which is most of them). Yes if you want a proper lunch — either BYO sandwiches, cheese and fruit, or pre-order a catered lunch box (€25-€60 per head, 24 h notice). Hot food and full meals don't usually come with the standard charter package. What about kids' equipment? Bring junior snorkel masks (charter masks are adult-sized), UPF rash vests, sun hats with chin straps, and a change of dry clothes for the drive home. Life jackets in infant/child/youth sizes are provided by every operator — mention ages when booking. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: White Night Puerto Banús (Noche en Blanco): Best Yacht Spot to Watch
DESCRIPTION: Watch White Night Puerto Banús from a private yacht. Best views, fireworks &amp; DJ sets. Book your boat now for the Noche en Blanco 2025.

Home › Guide › White Night Puerto Banús (Noche en Blanco): Best Yacht Spot to Watch By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers The White Night Puerto Banús (Noche en Blanco) is the Costa del Sol’s most glamorous all-night cultural event. Every summer, the marina transforms into a open-air stage with live music, DJ sets, art installations, and a spectacular fireworks display. But the best seat in the house isn’t on the dock – it’s on the water. Chartering a private yacht from Boat Rental Marbella gives you front-row views of the fireworks, your own drinks, and total freedom to enjoy the night your way. What is White Night Puerto Banús? Noche en Blanco is a European cultural phenomenon that started in Paris. In Puerto Banús, it’s an annual night-long festival featuring free concerts, street theatre, art exhibitions, and a grand fireworks finale over the marina. The event typically runs from early evening until 6am, with thousands of people flooding the Golden Mile and Puerto Banús. The dress code is white – hence the name – and the atmosphere is electric. Why Watch from a Yacht? Watching from a private yacht eliminates the crowds, the queues for drinks, and the struggle to find a good view. You can anchor just off the marina and watch the fireworks directly overhead. Our boats come with a skipper, so you don’t have to worry about navigation or parking. You control the music, the drinks, and the company. Plus, you can retreat to a quieter spot if the party gets too loud – or join the action whenever you like. Our Fleet for White Night We offer 18 boats departing from Puerto Banús, suitable for groups from 5 to 12 guests. Here are the top picks for watching the fireworks: Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 guests) – Spacious flybridge and sun deck, perfect for a group. Includes a free jet ski. From €4,719 for 4 hours. Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 guests) – Excellent upper deck visibility and luxury interior. Pricing on request. Pershing 46 (14m, 10 guests) – Sporty and stylish, ideal for a smaller party. Pricing on request. Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 guests) – Affordable option from €749 for 2 hours, includes drinks and snacks. Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 guests) – Great value for a larger group, same price as Astondoa 40. All charters include skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. No hidden costs. How to Book Your Yacht for White Night White Night is one of the busiest nights in Puerto Banús, so early booking is essential. Contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, group size, and any special requests (e.g., catering, decorations). We’ll confirm availability and send you a booking link. A deposit secures your slot. For the best experience, we recommend a 4-hour charter from 9pm to 1am – you’ll catch the sunset, the party atmosphere, and the fireworks at midnight. Alternatively, a 2-hour charter from 11pm to 1am works if you’re on a tighter schedule. What to Expect on the Night Dress in white (or your favourite white outfit). Bring a light jacket – it can get cool on the water after midnight. The skipper will position the boat for optimal views of the fireworks. You can play your own music through the boat’s sound system. Drinks and snacks are included, but you’re welcome to bring your own food or decorations. The fireworks typically start around midnight and last 15-20 minutes. After that, the DJ sets continue until dawn – you can stay on the water or head back to the marina to join the party on land. Other Tips for White Night Puerto Banús Arrive at Puerto Banús early (8pm) to find parking – it gets very busy. Bring cash for any on-land purchases, though most bars accept cards. If you’re prone to seasickness, take a preventative tablet an hour before departure. Book a return taxi in advance – Uber and taxis are scarce after 2am. Check the official event dates on the Marbella tourism website – usually late June or early July. Frequently asked questions What is White Night Puerto Banús? White Night (Noche en Blanco) is a cultural festival held annually in Puerto Banús, featuring free outdoor concerts, art installations, street performances, and a spectacular fireworks display. It runs from evening until dawn, turning the marina into a vibrant party hub. Why watch White Night from a yacht? A private yacht gives you front-row seats to the fireworks and DJ sets without the crowds. You can enjoy unlimited drinks, your own music, and the comfort of a luxury boat. Plus, you can anchor close to the action or find a quieter spot – your choice. Which boats are best for watching the fireworks? Any of our boats can position for great views, but the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 guests) offers a spacious flybridge and sun deck – ideal for a group. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 guests) also has excellent upper deck visibility. For smaller groups, the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 guests) is a stylish option. How much does a yacht charter cost for White Night? Prices start from €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (includes skipper, drinks, snacks, and VAT). For a premium experience, the Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request – just WhatsApp us. What is included in the charter price? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. Spanish IVA (21%) is also included. No hidden extras – you just bring your guests and enjoy. Can I bring my own food or decorations for White Night? Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and decorations. Many guests bring a white-themed picnic or birthday cake. Just let us know in advance so we can prepare the boat. We also offer catering options on request. How do I book a yacht for White Night Puerto Banús? Contact us via WhatsApp or email to check availability for your preferred date. We recommend booking at least 2 weeks in advance as White Night is very popular. We’ll confirm the boat, time, and any special requests. What time should I start my charter for White Night? The fireworks usually start around midnight, so a 2-hour charter from 11pm to 1am works well. For a full evening, book from 9pm to 1am (4 hours) to enjoy the sunset, the party atmosphere, and the fireworks. TITLE: White Party Marbella: Pre-Party Yacht Charter With Dress-Code Tips
DESCRIPTION: Plan your white party Marbella yacht charter with dress-code tips and boat options from €749/2h. Book your pre-party cruise from Puerto Banús today.

Home › Guide › White Party Marbella: Pre-Party Yacht Charter With Dress-Code Tips By Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella · Updated 20 May 2026 · Reviewed by local Marbella skippers Marbella's white party scene is legendary, and nothing sets the tone like a pre-party yacht charter along the Costa del Sol. Whether you're heading to a club in Puerto Banús or hosting your own celebration, a white party Marbella yacht experience combines luxury, sunset views, and the iconic all-white dress code. With boats departing from Puerto Banús, you can cruise the coastline with drinks, music, and a VIP vibe before the night begins. Why Choose a White Party Yacht Charter? A white party yacht charter is more than just a boat ride—it's a statement. You'll glide past the Golden Mile, sip cava, and soak up the Mediterranean sun while dressed in your finest white attire. It's the perfect way to kick off an evening, whether you're celebrating a birthday, a hen party, or simply a night out in Marbella. Our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks, so all you need to do is show up in white. Dress Code Tips for Your White Party Yacht Getting the dress code right is key. Here's what to wear for a white party Marbella yacht charter: All-white attire: White dresses, linen shirts, trousers, or jumpsuits work best. Avoid off-white or cream—stick to pure white for the full effect. Footwear: Deck shoes, sandals, or espadrilles are ideal. Avoid black soles that can mark the deck, and skip high heels (they're not practical on a boat). Swimwear: Wear your swimsuit under your outfit if you plan to swim or use the jet ski (included on Tier B boats). Accessories: Sunglasses, a white hat, and a light jacket for the evening breeze are smart additions. No dark colours: Black, navy, or bright colours break the white party theme—keep it monochrome. Stag and hen specifics Stag and hen weekends in Marbella usually combine the boat party with one of three add-ons: Jet-ski session tied to the charter — skipper coordinates pickup, €60–€90/person. Beach club entry at Nikki Beach, Ocean Club or Sala Beach — the boat tenders the group ashore, you spend 90 minutes, then re-board. Beach club entry is separate (€50–€150 minimum spend per head). Catering on board — paella party, sushi platter or full hot lunch. €30–€80 per head, 24 h notice. For a quieter group experience that's more day-out than party, the yacht charter Marbella page covers standard private charters. What you can and can't do Yes: BYO alcohol on private charters, loud music at anchor outside marina limits, jumping off the boat at the anchor stop, costume changes on board, inflatable swans, drone photography (your own pilot). No: glass on deck (cans and plastic only on most boats), confetti or anything that ends up in the water (Spanish marina fines are punishing), open flames (no shisha or candles), drugs (the skipper will return to port and the deposit is forfeit). Spanish maritime alcohol law applies to the skipper, not guests — but be sensible. Best months for a Marbella boat party Late June through early September is peak — water at 22–24 °C, long daylight, the marina scene at full volume. July is the busiest single month. Mid-September keeps the warmth and drops the price 10–15%. May, June and October work for groups happy with 19–21 °C water (swims are shorter but everything else still flies). Outside that window party charters still run but the on-deck temperature won't sustain a long anchor stop. TITLE: Boat Rental Marbella No License 2026: Self-Drive 5m / 15hp Boats
DESCRIPTION: License-free boat rules in Spain (5m / 15hp / 2 NM). Our skippered fleet starts at €749 — no license needed because we provide the captain. Full guide to no-license boating in Marbella.

Home › Boat Rental Marbella No License 2026: Self-Drive 5m / 15hp Boats Skip the licence question: our fleet ( Astondoa 40 , Azimut 39 ) is crewed by a licensed skipper, so you don't need any license at all. From €749 for 2 h , group capacity 9–11 guests. The page below covers the licence-free rules if you want to drive a smaller boat yourself. The short version: yes, you can rent a boat in Marbella with no licence, no certificate, and no formal sailing experience — as long as you stick to the Spanish rule set of 5 metres hull, 15 horsepower engine, 2 nautical miles from the coast, daylight only, and you're at least 18 years old. From €130 for 2 hours, this is the cheapest and most flexible way to get on the water on the Costa del Sol. The Spanish no-licence rules (2026) Spain's leisure boating regulation 875/2014 defines the licence-free category — known officially as navegación sin titulación — with the following limits: Hull length: 5 metres maximum for motor boats, 6 metres for sail-only boats. Engine power: 15 hp maximum (no exceptions — operators are checked). Distance from coast: 2 nautical miles (~3.7 km). Most coastal anchorages from Marbella sit well inside this. Time of day: daylight only. You must return before sunset. Captain age: 18 years or over with a valid ID. Alcohol: the same blood-alcohol limit as driving applies to the helm operator. For the broader licensing picture (and what you can charter if you have a foreign certificate), see our Spain boat licence rules guide. Where to pick up your no-licence boat Three Marbella locations operate licence-free fleets: Cabopino — biggest no-licence fleet on this stretch of coast. Easiest exit (the harbour mouth is wide and the immediate water is shallow and forgiving). Most affordable. Puerto Banús — fewer no-licence operators, slightly higher prices, but a great location if you want to anchor off Nikki Beach for the afternoon. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) — small no-licence fleet, central location, easy parking nearby. If you're staying further west, Estepona has additional licence-free operators that are 15–20% cheaper than Puerto Banús for the same boat. Skip the licence — book skippered If you want the simpler path: book our crewed fleet. Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 run €749 for 2 h with the skipper included — no licence, no exam, no on-the-dock briefing. The page below explains the official Spanish licence-free rules for context, but they only apply if you want to drive a small (under 5 m / 15 hp) self-drive boat yourself. What to expect — your first time at the helm Arrive 30 minutes before departure. You'll show ID and credit card, sign a one-page rental contract, and the operator walks you through: Throttle and gear shift. One lever — forward, neutral, reverse. Outboards on 15 hp boats are simple. Steering. Either a wheel or, on the smallest boats, a tiller. Anchor. How to drop it and how to retrieve it without trapping fingers. VHF radio. Channel 16 is emergency; you'll rarely need it within 2 NM. Coastal exclusion zones. The 200 m swimmer buoy zone along every Marbella beach — you cannot cross it under power. Refuel procedure. Where the fuel dock is, payment method. The whole briefing takes 10–15 minutes. Then you take the boat for a controlled lap of the marina with the operator watching from the dock, and after that you're on your own. Good itineraries for a 4-hour licence-free trip From Cabopino: head west 20 minutes to anchor off the Cabopino dune beach, swim and snorkel for an hour, push west another 15 minutes to Río Real, swim again, return to Cabopino with 30 minutes of buffer. Stays inside 2 NM the whole time. From Puerto Banús: head east 25 minutes to anchor off Nikki Beach (water is deeper here, anchor sets cleanly), swim, optional tender visit, return via the Golden Mile coastline so you see the famous waterfront mansions. Easy first-timer route. Is a no-licence boat right for you? Yes if: you're a group of 2–4, you want flexibility on timing, you'd rather drive yourself than be a passenger, and you're comfortable with light wind chop. No if: you're 6+ people (capacity is 4–5 max), you want speed (15 hp is slow), you want range (2 NM is tight), or you don't enjoy thinking about wind and tide. For larger groups, look at yacht charter Marbella with a skipper, or compare all options on the boat rental Marbella hub. Frequently asked questions Can I really rent a boat in Marbella without a licence? Yes. Spanish maritime law permits any adult over 18 to operate a boat up to 5 metres long with an engine up to 15 hp (or a sailboat up to 6 m), within 2 nautical miles of the coast, in daylight, with no licence and no formal training. You'll do a 10-minute on-the-dock briefing with the operator covering throttle, anchor and VHF radio, then you're on your way. What can a 15 hp boat actually do? A 5 m / 15 hp boat in Marbella cruises at 8–12 knots fully loaded with 4 adults, planes lightly when solo or with two. You'll comfortably reach the anchor spots off Marbella Marina, Río Verde or the Cabopino dunes within 20–30 minutes. Estepona and Puerto Banús-to-Cabopino in a day is realistic. How much is a no-license boat rental in Marbella? €130 for 2 hours in low season (October to May), €180 in high season (June to September). Full half-day (4 hours) is €220–€280. Full day (8 hours) is €380–€450. Most operators take a refundable security deposit of €200–€300. Is fuel included? Practice varies. About half of Marbella operators include fuel in the rental price; the other half operate on 'return with the tank as you found it'. Either way, a 15 hp engine burns very little — a 2-hour cruise typically uses €8–€15 of petrol. What happens if I damage the boat? Standard policy: the security deposit covers minor damage. For anything larger, every license-free rental in Spain comes with mandatory third-party liability insurance, which the operator carries. You're not personally liable beyond the deposit unless you've been reckless or drunk at the helm. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

Home › Boat Rental Puerto Banús 2026: Marbella's #1 Marina Puerto Banús is the marina you've already seen in Instagram reels — superyachts moored stern-to along Pier 1, Lamborghinis parked nose-out on Calle Ribera, and the Bocaccio nightclub a 90-second walk from the pontoon. It's also the practical centre of Marbella yacht charter: roughly 60% of bookable inventory on the Costa del Sol berths here. Boat rental from Puerto Banús starts at €250 for a 2-hour skippered trip. Why Puerto Banús dominates Marbella boat rental Three reasons it's the default departure port: Depth and berth size. Puerto Banús has 915 berths and the deepest harbour mouth on the Marbella stretch — the only marina locally that can routinely accommodate yachts over 30 metres. If you want a true luxury charter, you'll be here. Fleet concentration. Sixty per cent of charter inventory between Estepona and Cabopino is moored at Puerto Banús. Catamarans, sportfishers, party yachts and crewed superyachts cluster here. Marina infrastructure. Refuelling, fresh water, electrical hook-up, marine chandlers and ten dockside restaurants for pre-boarding lunch — Puerto Banús has all of it within a 5-minute walk. Puerto Banús vs Marbella Marina breaks down the trade-offs for non-Banús options. Where to meet the boat Puerto Banús is laid out as eight long parallel pontoons running south-east into the harbour, numbered 1 through 8 from west to east: Pier Typical fleet Walk from car park 1 Superyachts 30 m+, charter brokers 3 min 2–3 Luxury motor yachts 14–22 m 5 min 4–5 Motor yachts 10–14 m, catamarans 6 min 6 Sportfishers, sailing yachts 7 min 7 Day boats, licence-free, small sailboats 8 min 8 Working fleet, fuel dock 10 min Your charter will send you the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before. Show up 15 minutes early — Mediterranean charters leave on time, and missing the slot in summer often means losing the booking. Parking, food and pre-board essentials Parking: Aparcamiento Puerto Banús (underground) is the only sane option in July and August. €2/hour, €18 for the day. Free street parking exists west of the marina but never works mid-summer. Quick lunch before boarding: Antonio's Beach Club (tapas, walking distance), Picasso (Spanish, faster service), or grab a bocadillo from the SuperSol on Calle Ribera. Forgot something: Galerías El Corte Inglés (5-minute walk west) for sunscreen, swimwear, hats, electronics, and a basic pharmacy. Follow @boatrentalmarbella Frequently asked questions Why book from Puerto Banús instead of Marbella Marina? Three reasons: fleet depth, boat size (anything 18 m or above is here by default), and the marina experience. Marbella Marina is more central if you're in the old town. Where exactly do I meet the captain? Each charter sends you a pontoon number and slip 24 hours ahead — typical meeting points are Piers 1 (super-yachts), 2–3 (10–16 m), 6 (sailing/fishing), 7 (day boats and licence-free). Is there parking at Puerto Banús? Yes — underground Aparcamiento Puerto Banús, €2/hour or €18 for the day. Arrive 30 minutes early on summer Saturdays. What boats are available from Puerto Banús? Full Costa del Sol charter inventory: motor yachts 10–25 m, sail/power catamarans 38–55 ft, deep-sea sportfishers, luxury superyachts 24 m+, and a small fleet of licence-free runabouts. Can I sleep on board? Yes on yachts 12 m and above. Overnight charters from Puerto Banús start at €1,200/night for a 4-berth 12 m yacht and rise to €4,000+ for crewed 20 m superyachts. On the water with us Real charters with real groups across Marbella, Puerto Banús and the Costa del Sol. TITLE: Astondoa 40 — Astondoa 12.5m Charter from Puerto Banús
DESCRIPTION: Charter the Astondoa 40 from Puerto Banús — 12.5 m, 9 guests, skipper &amp; fuel included. From €749 for 2h.

Home › Boats › Astondoa 40 Astondoa 12.5 m motor yacht · 9 guests · departs Puerto Banús Astondoa 40 — known as "Fufi" — is the Spanish equivalent of the classic Mediterranean motor yacht. Built in Cádiz by Astondoa, fitted out in teak and cream leather, and at home anchoring off the Golden Mile for a long lunch. 12.5 metres, 9 guests, skipper and fuel included. Quick specs: 12.5 m · 9 pax · skipper included · departs Puerto Banús. From €749 for 2h. Drinks, fuel, insurance & VAT included. Hourly pricing Same boat, same crew — price scales with duration. Pick the length that fits your day: Duration Price (EUR) 2h €749 3h €1,049 4h €1,299 5h €1,549 6h €1,799 7h €2,099 8h €2,299 All prices include: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, vat (21%). No hidden marina fees, no fuel surcharge for the standard coastal route. What makes this boat work Flybridge with shaded bimini and second helm Cream-leather saloon with table-for-six and AC Two private cabins for overnight charter Spanish-built (Astondoa, Cádiz) — local provenance Snorkel gear, inflatable donut & paddleboard on board What's included on every charter Licensed skipper for the full duration Drinks on board: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (fruit, crisps, almonds, biscuits) Fuel for the standard Marbella–Estepona–Cabopino cruising loop Insurance and full safety equipment (life jackets, flares, first aid) Spanish IVA (VAT, 21%) — no surprise at checkout Water toys: snorkel masks, inflatable donut, paddleboard Towels for every guest Catered lunch, premium spirits, DJ and beach-club tender service can be added when you book. Where it departs from The Astondoa 40 berths at Puerto Banús — the deepest charter marina on the Marbella coast and the most central pickup for guests staying on the Golden Mile. You will get the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before departure. Underground parking is 5 minutes from the pontoon. Typical day on the Astondoa 40 Boarding at Puerto Banús — welcome drinks, safety briefing. Westbound run along the Golden Mile, past Marbella Club and Puente Romano. Anchor stop at Cala del Faro or Río Verde for swim, snorkel and paddleboard. Lunch on board (BYO or catered platter). Optional tender to a beach club, or continue east towards Cabopino. Return to Puerto Banús, sunset over La Concha mountain. How to book WhatsApp us with your date and group size. We confirm availability within minutes, hold the boat for 24 hours with no deposit, and lock the booking once you confirm. Full refund up to 7 days out, weather cancellations always 100% refundable. How many guests can the Astondoa 40 carry? 9 guests for day charter. Overnight capacity is lower — typically 4–6 in cabins on a 12.5 m yacht. Is the skipper included? Yes — every charter on the Astondoa 40 comes with a licensed Spanish skipper. The captain handles navigation, anchoring and route planning. You and your group are guests for the day. What's included in the price? Skipper, fuel for the standard coastal route, drinks (water and soft drinks), Spanish IVA (VAT) and insurance. Catered lunch and alcohol are extras you can add when booking. Can we go further than the standard route? Yes — longer or further itineraries (Gibraltar day trip, Estepona-Sotogrande loop) are bookable. A fuel surcharge applies for itineraries beyond the standard 12–15 NM coastal cruise. We will quote it before you commit. What happens if the weather is bad? Skipper calls the night before. If forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 or sea state makes the trip unsafe, you rebook or get a full refund. TITLE: Cancellation Policy — Boat Rental Marbella
DESCRIPTION: Cancellation policy for Boat Rental Marbella charters: 100% refund 7+ days before, weather cancellations always refundable, transparent sliding scale.

Home › Cancellation Policy Effective 1 May 2026 · Apply to all skippered charters from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona and Sotogrande. Summary in one line Cancel 7+ days before = full refund. Skipper-cancelled for weather = full refund, always. Full schedule When you cancel Refund Notes 7+ days before charter 100% Full refund, no questions. 3–6 days before 50% The other 50% covers skipper hold + fuel sourcing. 48 hours – 3 days 25% Higher because we've usually turned other bookings down. Inside 48 hours 0% Booked period is committed. Day-of no-show 0% Skipper waited at the dock. Weather cancellations If the skipper cancels the charter because of wind, sea state or thunderstorm risk, you always get a 100% refund or a free reschedule to a date of your choice. The call is made the evening before based on Windguru / AEMET forecasts. Light rain alone is not a cancellation reason on the Costa del Sol — drizzle clears within minutes, the sea is usually flat. How to cancel Message Andra on WhatsApp with your booking date. Refunds are processed back to the original payment method within 3–7 business days. Rescheduling vs cancelling Rescheduling is always free if we can find a new date in the same season. If the new date is in a different season (e.g. December → August), the new-season price applies — we'll show you the difference before you confirm. Add-ons and catering Add-ons booked separately (catered lunch, photographer, custom decoration) follow the catering provider's own cancellation terms — we forward 100% of what we collect, no markup. We'll always show you those terms when you add the line item. Force majeure If the charter is impossible because of events outside our control (port closure, government health order, harbour-wide emergency), you get a 100% refund regardless of how close to the date. Travel insurance We recommend buying travel insurance with cruise / charter cover for any booking over €1,500. Standard travel insurance often excludes private yacht charter — read your policy. Questions? Message Andra on WhatsApp or email hello@boatrentalinmarbella.com . ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter WhatsApp now — average reply under 5 minutes. TITLE: Catamaran Rental Marbella for Families: Spacious and Stable
DESCRIPTION: Book a catamaran rental Marbella family charter for a spacious, stable day on the Costa del Sol. From €749/2h with skipper, drinks &amp; snacks included.

Home › Catamaran Rental Marbella for Families: Spacious and Stable If you are looking for a catamaran rental Marbella family charter that combines space, stability, and all-inclusive ease, the Lagoon 380 is your best option. Unlike monohull motor yachts, catamarans offer a wide, stable platform that does not roll in a swell, making them ideal for young children, elderly parents, or anyone prone to seasickness. Our Lagoon 380 departs from Puerto Banús and can carry up to 10 passengers plus a licensed skipper. Prices start at €749 for two hours and go up to €2,299 for a full eight-hour day – and that includes drinks, snacks, fuel, insurance, and tax. No hidden extras, no surprises. Why a catamaran works for families A catamaran’s twin-hull design gives it a much wider beam than a monohull of the same length. The Lagoon 380 measures 11.6 metres overall but offers a living space that feels more like a 15-metre yacht. The saloon opens directly onto the aft deck, creating a single indoor-outdoor area where parents can keep an eye on children while preparing snacks. Because the hulls are separate, there is no deep V-shaped cabin to trip over – the saloon floor is flat, and the side decks have high guardrails. Children can move around without the constant tilt of a heeling monohull. Our family-friendly catamaran: Lagoon 380 The Lagoon 380 is the only catamaran in our fleet. It is 11.6 metres long, 6.5 metres wide, and can take up to 10 passengers. It departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s most famous marina. The boat has four double cabins and two heads (toilets), so a large family can spread out. The forward trampoline nets are a favourite spot for children to lie on and watch the coastline. The shallow draft of just 1.2 metres means the skipper can anchor very close to the beach at spots like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, where the water is shallow and calm enough for safe swimming. What’s included in the price Every charter on our Lagoon 380 includes a licensed skipper, all fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), and a complimentary bar with soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Light snacks are also provided. You do not need to bring anything except sunscreen and towels. The skipper handles navigation, anchoring, and safety briefings, so you can relax. If you want to bring your own food or a birthday cake, the galley has a fridge and a stove. There is no extra charge for mooring at a restaurant dock, though any restaurant bill is yours. Where you can go on a family catamaran trip Departing from Puerto Banús, you have several family-friendly routes. A popular half-day option heads east towards Cabopino, a protected dune reserve with a sandy beach and calm water. The distance is about 5 nautical miles, so the sailing time is short. For a full day, you can go west to Estepona or even Sotogrande, where the coastline opens up with wider bays. The skipper will anchor for swimming and snorkelling at spots like Cala Cortés, a small cove with clear water and rocky seabed. The shallow draft of the catamaran allows it to get closer to the shore than most motor yachts. Pricing and booking Our Lagoon 380 is priced in Tier A: €749 for two hours, €1,199 for four hours, €1,699 for six hours, and €2,299 for eight hours. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and tax. For longer charters or custom requests, simply contact us via WhatsApp. We do not charge extra for cleaning or mooring. To see the full range of boats, visit our Marbella boat rental hub. Safety and comfort for all ages The Lagoon 380 has high bulwarks and guardrails all around, reducing the risk of falls. The saloon door can be latched open or closed, and the galley is equipped with a gas cooker and a fridge. Lifejackets for children and adults are on board, and the skipper will give a short safety briefing before departure. The boat’s stability means that even on a breezy day, you will not feel the constant rolling that can upset younger children. Many families choose a four-hour morning charter (10:00–14:00) when the sea is calmest and the sun is not too strong. Frequently asked questions Is the Lagoon 380 suitable for young children? Yes. The Lagoon 380’s wide beam and low freeboard make it very stable, so toddlers and older kids can move around safely. The skipper will anchor in calm, shallow bays like Cala Cortés where children can swim under supervision. What is included in the catamaran rental price? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), and an open bar of soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, plus light snacks. No extra charges for mooring or cleaning. How many people can the Lagoon 380 carry? The Lagoon 380 has a maximum capacity of 10 passengers plus the skipper. This makes it ideal for a family group or two families sharing the day. Do we need to bring our own food and drinks? No. Soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks are provided. If you want a specific meal or cake for a celebration, you can bring your own – there is a fridge and galley on board. Can we swim and snorkel during the trip? Absolutely. The skipper will anchor at protected spots like Cala del Faro or Cabopino, where the water is calm and clear. Snorkelling gear is usually available on request. What happens in bad weather? Your safety is the priority. If the forecast shows strong winds or high seas, we will offer you a free reschedule or full refund. The skipper monitors conditions and will adjust the route. How do we book a catamaran for our family? Contact us via WhatsApp on the number shown on our website. We will confirm availability for the Lagoon 380, send you a booking link, and answer any specific requests you have. Ready to book your catamaran rental Marbella family day out? TITLE: Catamaran Rental Marbella 2026: Sail &amp; Power Catamarans from €350
DESCRIPTION: Looking for catamaran rental in Marbella? Our current fleet is two 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts from Puerto Banús (€749/2h). Plus a full guide to catamaran charter options in Marbella.

Home › Catamaran Rental Marbella 2026: Sail & Power Catamarans from €350 Our fleet right now: two 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts — the Astondoa 40 (9 guests) and Azimut 39 (11 guests). From €749 for 2h , up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day . Skipper, fuel, drinks, insurance and VAT included. Catamaran rental in Marbella means a stable twin-hull platform, far more deck space than a monohull yacht of the same length, and a flat-water ride that keeps even seasickness-prone guests relaxed. Prices start at €350 for 4 hours on a 38-ft sail catamaran from Puerto Banús and scale up to €2,400 for a full day on a 50-ft luxury catamaran. Why a catamaran beats a monohull in Marbella Three concrete advantages for the typical Marbella day charter: Stability. The Costa del Sol gets a 12–18 knot easterly (the levante) most summer afternoons. A monohull yacht heels 15–20°; a catamaran heels 2–3°. Glasses stay on tables, kids stay calm, and lunch stays on the plate. Deck space. A 40 ft catamaran has the deck area of roughly a 55 ft monohull. Bridgedeck cockpit, two helm stations, forward trampolines for sun-lounging, and a swim platform across the full stern. Shallow draft. Catamarans typically draw 1.1–1.3 metres versus 2.0–2.5 metres for a monohull. The skipper can anchor closer to shore at Cala del Faro, Cabopino dunes or Río Real — which puts the swim ladder over clearer, calmer water. Catamaran rental Marbella prices 2026 Catamaran 4 h low 4 h high Full day 8 h Day guests 38–42 ft sail catamaran €350 €420 €1,000–€1,250 10–12 44–48 ft sail catamaran €450 €580 €1,400–€1,700 12 40–45 ft power catamaran €520 €680 €1,600–€1,900 10–12 50 ft luxury catamaran €700 €900 €1,800–€2,400 12–14 Prices include skipper, fuel and standard drinks. For comparison with motor yachts of similar length, see yacht charter Marbella . Sail vs power catamaran Sail catamarans are the romantic, atmospheric option. When the wind cooperates (most afternoons May–October), the skipper unfurls the genoa and you sail in near silence at 6–8 knots. The downside: under engine alone, sail catamarans only do 7–9 knots, so reaching Sotogrande and back in 4 hours isn't realistic. Power catamarans cruise at 18–22 knots with twin diesels, which makes long itineraries viable, but you trade the quiet sail experience for engine noise. For pure swim-and-lunch days where range doesn't matter, the sail catamaran is almost always the better experience. For Gibraltar trips or anywhere you want to cover ground, the power cat or a motor yacht makes more sense. Catamaran itineraries from Puerto Banús The 4-hour catamaran sweet spot from Puerto Banús: depart 11:00, motor west 30 minutes to clear the marina traffic, raise sails off Río Verde, sail the Golden Mile coastline downwind, drop sails and anchor at Río Real for a long swim, motor back to Puerto Banús by 15:00 in time for a beach club afternoon. For a full day, add a second anchor stop at Cala del Faro past Estepona — total round trip about 35 NM. If you have kids on board , the flat catamaran deck is dramatically safer than a heeling monohull. What's included Standard catamaran charter inclusions in Marbella are identical to motor yacht charters: skipper, fuel, soft drinks, water, ice, light snacks, snorkel gear and an inflatable SUP or donut. Many catamaran operators also throw in fishing rods (light tackle for tuna or mackerel) and the use of the on-board BBQ at anchor — ask when booking. Best months for catamaran rental in Marbella May, June, September and October are ideal — steady 8–14 knot wind for proper sailing, sea temperature 19–23 °C, and 10–15% lower prices than peak July/August. July and August deliver hotter weather but variable wind: sometimes glassy mornings (motor only) and stronger afternoons. November to March, sail catamaran trips still run on calm days but choice of operators is more limited. How to book Tell us your date, group size, sail vs power preference and budget. We'll send back specific catamaran options with photos and exact pricing. Confirm with 50% deposit; balance on the boarding day. The full boat rental Marbella hub compares catamarans against every other boat type if you're still deciding. Frequently asked questions Why pick a catamaran over a monohull yacht? Two reasons: stability and deck space. A catamaran sits flat on the water with two hulls — even in 15-knot wind there's almost no heel — so guests who get queasy on monohulls can usually relax. The bridgedeck and forward trampolines also give you 40–50% more usable deck space at the same overall length, which matters when you have 10+ guests. How much is catamaran rental in Marbella? Catamaran rental in Marbella starts at €350 for 4 hours on a 38-ft sail catamaran in low season, €420 in high season. An 8-hour day on the same catamaran is €1,000–€1,250 fully crewed. Larger 50-ft catamarans run €600 for 4 hours and €1,800–€2,400 for a day. Sail catamaran or power catamaran? Sail catamarans give you the option to actually sail when the wind is right (typically a Levante easterly mid-afternoon) — it's quieter and more romantic, but slower. Power catamarans (twin-engine, no rig) deliver 18–22 knot cruising, which matters if you want to reach Sotogrande or Cabopino on the same day. For lunch-and-swim half-days, sail catamarans win on atmosphere. How many guests fit on a catamaran? Day-charter capacity on the standard 38–42 ft catamarans in Marbella is 10–12 guests. Larger 50-ft catamarans take 14–16 day guests. Overnight capacity is lower — typically 6–8 in cabins. For groups above 16, look at boat party options on motor yachts. Are catamarans good for snorkelling? Excellent. The shallow draft (often 1.2 m vs 2.5 m for a monohull) lets the skipper anchor closer to shore, which usually means clearer water for snorkelling. The wide swim platforms at the stern make getting in and out far easier than climbing down a monohull ladder. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

DESCRIPTION: Wann ist die beste Zeit zum Bootfahren in Marbella? Wassertemperatur, Wind und Preise Monat für Monat — und warum Juni &amp; September ideal sind.

Bootsverleih › Beste Reisezeit Marbella hat über 300 Sonnentage im Jahr, und Bootscharter sind ganzjährig möglich. Aber es gibt klare Unterschiede bei Wassertemperatur, Wind, Andrang und Preisen . Hier die ehrliche Monatsübersicht. Monat für Monat Zeitraum Wasser Bewertung April–Mai 16–19°C Sonnig, ruhig, günstig — Wasser noch frisch zum Schwimmen Juni 20–22°C ★ Ideal — warm, ruhig, vor dem großen Andrang Juli–August 23–25°C Hochsaison — wärmstes Wasser, aber voll & nachmittags windig September 22–24°C ★ Ideal — warmes Wasser, weniger Andrang, ruhiger Oktober 20–22°C Mild und ruhig, gutes Preis-Leistungs-Verhältnis Nov–März 15–17°C Charter möglich (Sightseeing, Delfine), zum Schwimmen kühl Die beste Wahl: Juni & September Wer flexibel ist, bucht Juni oder September : Das Meer ist warm genug zum Schwimmen (22–24°C), der Wind ist meist schwach, es ist deutlich weniger los als im Hochsommer — und die Preise sind oft niedriger als im Juli/August. Mehr dazu in unserer Preisübersicht . Tageszeit zählt auch Unabhängig vom Monat ist der Vormittag meist am ruhigsten — der typische Costa-del-Sol-Nachmittagswind („viento de poniente“) frischt erst später auf. Für Romantik ist die Sonnenuntergang-Tour unschlagbar, dann ist die See abends wieder glatt. Fragen zur besten Zeit für euch? URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/de/blog/bootsfuehrerschein-spanien/
TITLE: Bootsführerschein in Spanien: Regeln 2026 für Marbella einfach erklärt
DESCRIPTION: Bootsführerschein in Spanien — wann du keinen brauchst, welche deutschen Scheine gelten und wie führerscheinfreies Fahren in Marbella funktioniert (5 m / 15 PS).

Bootsverleih › Bootsführerschein Spanien Die häufigste Frage deutscher Gäste: „Brauche ich für ein Boot in Marbella einen Führerschein?“ Die kurze Antwort: oft nicht. Hier sind die Regeln für 2026 klar erklärt. Wann du KEINEN Führerschein brauchst Mit Skipper — bei ~95% unserer Charters fährt ein lizenzierter Skipper. Du brauchst gar nichts und kannst dich entspannen. Führerscheinfrei selbst fahren — erlaubt bei Booten bis 5 m Rumpflänge und max. 15 PS , innerhalb 2 Seemeilen von der Küste, nur bei Tageslicht, Fahrer mindestens 18 Jahre. Siehe unsere Boote ohne Führerschein ab €230/2h. Welche deutschen Scheine in Spanien gelten Wer ein größeres Boot selbst steuern will, braucht eine anerkannte Lizenz. In der Praxis werden gängige internationale Scheine akzeptiert: SBF-See (Sportbootführerschein See) — für küstennahe Fahrten meist ausreichend. SKS (Sportküstenschifferschein) — für größere/weitere Charter. ICC (International Certificate of Competence) — international anerkannt. Bring immer das Original-Zertifikat (kein Foto) mit. Im Zweifel schick uns vorab ein Bild auf WhatsApp — wir sagen dir, ob es für das gewünschte Boot reicht. Die führerscheinfreien Grenzen im Detail Regel Grenze Bootslänge max. 5 m Motorleistung max. 15 PS Abstand zur Küste max. 2 Seemeilen Tageszeit nur bei Tageslicht Mindestalter Fahrer 18 Jahre Unser Tipp Die meisten Gäste buchen einfach mit Skipper — kein Papierkram, kein Stress, und ihr lernt die besten Buchten von einem Local. Wer den Nervenkitzel selbst fahren will, nimmt ein führerscheinfreies Boot oder eine geführte Jetski-Tour . ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verifizierte Bewertungen Ab €749 2h Charter mit Skipper WhatsApp jetzt — durchschnittliche Antwort in unter 5 Minuten. Julio y agosto son pico de demanda con calor máximo (sea 24°C) pero también vientos de levante por las tardes que pueden hacer la mar más movida. Por qué pagar el patrón vale la pena Cero papeleo: sólo subes a bordo y disfrutas. Conoce la costa: nuestros patrones saben dónde está el agua más calmada, los mejores fondeos, los chiringuitos donde se puede atracar. Maniobras complicadas: entrar a Puerto Banús un sábado de agosto no es trivial. El patrón lo resuelve. Más diversión, menos estrés: tú con cava en la mano, no con el manual del barco. Reglas de embarcaciones sin licencia en España Si prefieres conducir tú mismo, las reglas estatales son: Eslora máxima del casco: 5 metros (motor) o 6 m (vela) Potencia máxima del motor: 15 caballos Distancia máxima desde costa: 2 millas náuticas Sólo horario diurno (hasta la puesta de sol) Conductor con 18 años cumplidos y DNI/pasaporte Sin sobrepasar el límite de alcohol en sangre del conducir Qué incluye nuestro alquiler con patrón Patrón con titulación PER (Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo) o superior Combustible para la ruta costera estándar Bebidas: agua, refrescos, cerveza, vino blanco y cava Snacks ligeros Snorkel, donut hinchable, paddleboard, toallas Seguro completo a terceros y daños propios IVA español (21%) — sin sorpresas en el muelle Precios Duración Yate con patrón 2 horas €749 4 horas €1.299 6 horas €1.799 8 horas €2.299 Qué incluye un día con patrón frente a alquilar sin licencia Aspecto Con patrón (nuestra flota) Sin licencia (5 m / 15 cv) Capacidad 9–12 invitados 4–5 personas Distancia de la costa Sin límite práctico Máximo 2 millas náuticas Horario Día completo o noche Solo de día (antes del ocaso) Bebidas a bordo Cava, vino blanco, cerveza, refrescos Lo que lleves tú Comodidades Aire acondicionado, baño, cocina Bañera básica, sin baño Conducción Patrón profesional Tú conduces Precio 2 h €749 total €130–€180 total Precio por persona (grupo de 6) ~€125 por persona ~€25 por persona si grupo de 5 Para grupos pequeños y presupuesto ajustado, una embarcación sin licencia tiene sentido. Para cualquier cosa con grupo de 6+, comodidad, bebidas a bordo o llegar a sitios bonitos como Cala del Faro, nuestra opción con patrón sale a cuenta y es infinitamente más relajante. Sobre los patrones de nuestra flota Todos nuestros patrones tienen titulación oficial española (PER — Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo, o superior) y al menos 5 años de experiencia en la Costa del Sol. Conocen cada fondeo, cada chiringuito accesible por mar, las horas óptimas para evitar el viento de levante, y cómo maniobrar Puerto Banús un sábado de agosto. Hablan español, inglés y la mayoría también francés o italiano. Dejarán que conduzcas un rato en tramos seguros si te apetece probar el timón. Reglamentación española al detalle La normativa de embarcaciones sin titulación viene del Real Decreto 875/2014. Los límites simultáneos son: Eslora máxima: 5 m (motor) / 6 m (vela) Potencia máxima: 15 cv Distancia máxima de la costa: 2 millas náuticas (~3,7 km) Solo horario diurno, sin pernoctación Conductor con al menos 18 años cumplidos Misma normativa de alcohol que para conducción terrestre El Servicio Marítimo de la Guardia Civil hace patrullas aleatorias en julio-agosto, especialmente entre Puerto Banús y Cabopino. Si te paran y el motor supera los 15 cv en una embarcación supuestamente "sin titulación", la multa al alquilador es de €600+ y al conductor le retiran el timón en el momento. Por eso los operadores serios son estrictos con el límite. Preguntas frecuentes ¿De verdad puedo alquilar un barco sin licencia en Marbella? Sí — con dos opciones. Reserva un yate con patrón profesional (nuestra flota, desde €749/2h) y eres pasajero, sin ningún requisito de licencia. O alquila una embarcación pequeña (hasta 5 m / 15 cv) en operadores especializados, sin titulación pero con limitaciones (2 NM de costa, sólo de día). ¿Hace falta saber navegar? No con nuestros barcos — el patrón conduce. Sólo te sientas, disfrutas y das instrucciones del tipo "un baño más antes de volver". ¿Cuántos puedo llevar? Astondoa 40 hasta 9 invitados, Azimut 39 hasta 11, Mangusta 80 hasta 12. ¿Puedo conducir un rato? Sí, durante tramos seguros y bajo supervisión del patrón. Es una práctica habitual y los patrones suelen estar encantados de que pruebes el timón. Con nuestros clientes a bordo Charters reales con grupos reales en Marbella, Puerto Banús y la Costa del Sol. TITLE: Política de Privacidad — Alquiler de Barcos Marbella
DESCRIPTION: Política de privacidad de boatrentalinmarbella.com — qué datos recopilamos cuando nos contactas por WhatsApp, email o formulario, cómo los usamos y tus derechos RGPD.

Política de Privacidad Última actualización: 1 de junio de 2026 Esta política explica cómo Boat Rental Marbella (sitio web boatrentalinmarbella.com , en adelante "nosotros") recopila, utiliza y protege tus datos personales cuando visitas el Sitio, nos contactas por WhatsApp, email o el formulario de reserva, o contratas un chárter. Cumplimos con el Reglamento (UE) 2016/679 (RGPD) y la LOPDGDD 3/2018. 1. Responsable del tratamiento Boat Rental Marbella Marbella, Andalucía, España (29602) WhatsApp / teléfono: +358 400 406 194 2. Datos que recopilamos Reservas y consultas — nombre, teléfono, email, fechas de interés, número de personas, puerto de salida, idioma, y el texto libre que envíes. Pagos — para reservas confirmadas el pago lo procesa el operador del barco (normalmente Stripe, Revolut Business o transferencia). No guardamos números de tarjeta completos. Datos técnicos — dirección IP, navegador, dispositivo, página de origen, páginas vistas y marcas de tiempo. Recogidos por los logs del servidor y Google Analytics 4 (ID G-SPMRYJVTHD). Cookies — ver apartado 7. 3. Para qué los usamos (base jurídica) Atender tu consulta y organizar el chárter — base: ejecución de un contrato o medidas pre-contractuales (art. 6.1.b RGPD). Compartir la reserva con el operador / patrón — base: contrato (art. 6.1.b). Cumplir con la normativa fiscal y marítima española (facturación, lista de pasajeros si el capitán la requiere) — base: obligación legal (art. 6.1.c). Mejorar el Sitio y medir tráfico — base: interés legítimo (art. 6.1.f). Comunicaciones comerciales — solo con tu consentimiento expreso (art. 6.1.a), revocable en cualquier momento. Actualmente no enviamos newsletter. 4. Destinatarios Operador / patrón asignado al chárter (nombre, teléfono, fecha, número de personas). Procesadores de pago (Stripe, Revolut, etc.). Proveedores de comunicación — WhatsApp (Meta Platforms Ireland Ltd.), Gmail / Google Workspace, Instagram y Facebook (Meta). Alojamiento y analítica — GitHub Pages (Microsoft Corp., EE. UU.), Cloudflare, Google Analytics 4 (Google Ireland Ltd.). Hacienda, aseguradoras y autoridades cuando exista obligación legal. Algunos proveedores están en EE. UU. Las transferencias se basan en el Marco de Privacidad de Datos UE–EE. UU. o en las Cláusulas Contractuales Tipo de la Comisión Europea. 5. Conservación Consultas sin reserva — eliminadas en 12 meses. Reservas y facturas — 6 años (obligación fiscal española). Logs y analítica — 14 meses (por defecto en Google Analytics). Registros de consentimiento — hasta su retirada más un periodo razonable de archivo. 6. Tus derechos Puedes ejercer en cualquier momento los derechos de acceso, rectificación, supresión, limitación, oposición, portabilidad y retirada del consentimiento, enviándonos un WhatsApp al +358 400 406 194 con copia de un documento de identidad. Responderemos en un máximo de 30 días. También puedes reclamar ante la Agencia Española de Protección de Datos (AEPD) . 7. Cookies Estrictamente necesarias — para mostrar el Sitio, recordar el idioma y el borrador del formulario. No requieren consentimiento. Analítica — Google Analytics 4 ( _ga , _ga_* ) de forma anónima. Puedes desactivarlas con el complemento de inhabilitación de Google Analytics . Embeds externos — solo activan cookies si haces clic en enlaces a Instagram, Facebook o WhatsApp. 8. Menores El Sitio no se dirige a menores de 14 años y no recopilamos conscientemente sus datos. Los menores pueden viajar a bordo del chárter — sus datos los facilita el padre, madre o tutor legal en la reserva. 9. Seguridad El Sitio funciona sobre HTTPS. Los mensajes por WhatsApp están cifrados de extremo a extremo por Meta. Los registros internos se guardan en servicios cloud (Google Workspace) con acceso controlado y verificación en dos pasos. 10. Cambios Podemos actualizar esta política cuando sea necesario; la fecha de "Última actualización" arriba indica la versión vigente. Los cambios sustanciales se anunciarán en la portada durante al menos 30 días. 11. Contacto Cualquier consulta de privacidad: WhatsApp +358 400 406 194 . Consulta también nuestros Términos y Condiciones .

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/es/terminos/
TITLE: Términos y Condiciones — Alquiler de Barcos Marbella
DESCRIPTION: Términos y condiciones de los alquileres de barcos reservados a través de boatrentalinmarbella.com — reserva, precios, pago, cancelación, meteorología, seguridad, responsabilidad y ley aplicable.

Términos y Condiciones Última actualización: 1 de junio de 2026 Estos términos regulan cualquier reserva, chárter, consulta o uso del sitio boatrentalinmarbella.com ("el Sitio"), operado por Boat Rental Marbella ("nosotros"). Al enviar una consulta, aceptar un presupuesto, abonar una señal o embarcar en un barco contratado, confirmas que has leído, entendido y aceptado estos términos. 1. Sobre nosotros Boat Rental Marbella es una agencia independiente de chárter con base en Marbella, Andalucía, España. Coordinamos chárteres con patrón de yates, catamaranes, lanchas y motos de agua que operan desde Puerto Banús, Puerto Deportivo de Marbella (Virgen del Carmen), Cabopino, Estepona y Sotogrande. Los chárteres son operados por patrones con licencia española y empresas colaboradoras con los permisos marítimos y comerciales correspondientes. WhatsApp / teléfono: +358 400 406 194 2. Proceso de reserva Nos envías una consulta a través del formulario, WhatsApp, email o DM de Instagram/Facebook, indicando fecha, número de personas y barco o experiencia preferida. Respondemos con disponibilidad y presupuesto por escrito (normalmente en pocas horas, a menudo en minutos durante el horario europeo). La reserva queda confirmada solo cuando (a) enviamos confirmación por escrito y (b) se recibe la señal o el pago total acordados. Te enviamos la dirección de salida, contacto del patrón, hora de quedada y briefing por WhatsApp al menos 24 horas antes del chárter. 3. Precios, pago y qué incluye Los precios se indican en euros (€), incluyen el IVA español (actualmente 21%) y — salvo indicación expresa — incluyen patrón con licencia, combustible para la ruta costera estándar, refrescos y agua, equipo de snorkel, paddleboard, toallas y seguro estándar. Lo siguiente normalmente no está incluido salvo mención: catering, bebidas premium, combustible de juguetes acuáticos (p. ej. salida en moto de agua fuera del chárter), tasas de atraque en puertos de terceros, propinas y extras opcionales (DJ, fotógrafo, decoración). Las formas de pago varían según el operador — habitualmente una señal del 30–50% por tarjeta, enlace Stripe, Revolut o transferencia SEPA para confirmar, y el resto en efectivo o tarjeta el día del chárter. Los términos concretos figuran en el presupuesto escrito. 4. Política de cancelación, modificación y reembolso Salvo que tu confirmación indique algo distinto, las condiciones estándar son: 7 o más días antes de la salida — reembolso del 100% de lo abonado. Entre 3 y 6 días antes — reembolso del 50%. Menos de 48 horas antes — sin reembolso. No presentarse — sin reembolso. Los cambios de fecha son gratuitos si se solicitan con al menos 72 horas de antelación y dependen de disponibilidad. 5. Política meteorológica El patrón es la única autoridad para decidir si la salida puede realizarse con seguridad. Si la previsión de viento supera la fuerza 4–5 (~20+ nudos), el estado de la mar es inseguro, o la autoridad portuaria emite una advertencia, el patrón aplazará el chárter — habitualmente la tarde anterior o antes de las 08:00 del mismo día. En ese caso te ofrecemos: una nueva fecha sin coste adicional (sujeto a disponibilidad), o el reembolso íntegro de lo abonado. La lluvia ligera por sí sola no es motivo de cancelación en la Costa del Sol. Si cancelas por motivos meteorológicos cuando el patrón considera la salida segura, aplican las condiciones del apartado 4. 6. Seguridad, conducta y autoridad del patrón A bordo, el capitán tiene plena autoridad legal y operativa sobre el barco y los pasajeros conforme a la legislación marítima española. El capitán puede, a su entera discreción y sin reembolso: denegar el embarque a personas visiblemente ebrias, bajo efectos de drogas o con conducta agresiva; alterar la ruta, acortar el chárter o regresar a puerto por motivos de seguridad; obligar al uso de chaleco salvavidas; prohibir el baño, los saltos desde el barco o el uso de juguetes acuáticos cuando las condiciones no sean seguras. Todos los pasajeros deben seguir las instrucciones del capitán y el briefing de seguridad. Los menores de 12 años deben llevar chaleco siempre que el barco esté en navegación. Las mascotas solo se aceptan si se ha acordado previamente y deben llevar un chaleco específico para perros aportado por el propietario. 7. Conducta, daños y limpieza Las drogas de cualquier tipo están terminantemente prohibidas a bordo — el capitán está obligado por ley a regresar a puerto y avisar a la Guardia Civil. El cliente es responsable de cualquier daño al barco, equipamiento, juguetes acuáticos o bienes de terceros causados por él o sus invitados por negligencia o dolo, incluyendo cristalería rota, tapicería manchada, equipo de snorkel perdido o dañado y limpieza extraordinaria (p. ej. vómitos, derrames de comida que requieran limpieza profunda — entre 150 € y 500 €). 8. Retrasos y prolongaciones El chárter comienza a la hora acordada. Si llegas tarde, el chárter finaliza igualmente a la hora prevista, sin extensión ni reembolso. Prolongar el chárter el mismo día suele ser posible si el siguiente turno está libre — el tiempo adicional se cobra al 80–100% de la tarifa horaria y debe acordarse con el patrón antes de finalizar el turno reservado. 9. Seguro Todo barco contratado cuenta con el seguro de casco y responsabilidad civil obligatorio según la legislación española. No cubre objetos personales (móviles, cámaras, joyas, gafas de sol), condiciones médicas preexistentes ni pérdidas causadas por el incumplimiento de las normas de seguridad. Recomendamos contratar un seguro de viaje con cobertura de chárter, especialmente en reservas de alto valor o grupos numerosos. 10. Fotografía y contenido Ocasionalmente el patrón o un operador de dron pueden tomar fotos o vídeos cortos durante el chárter. Al embarcar consientes el uso no identificativo de este material en nuestra web y redes sociales (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube). Si no quieres aparecer, comunícaselo al patrón antes de zarpar y te excluiremos. Puedes retirar el consentimiento sobre imágenes ya publicadas escribiéndonos por WhatsApp al +358 400 406 194 . 11. Responsabilidad En la máxima medida permitida por la ley española, nuestra responsabilidad por cualquier pérdida derivada de tu chárter se limita al importe total pagado por dicho chárter. No respondemos por daños indirectos o consecuenciales (vuelos perdidos, alojamiento, pérdida de disfrute) causados por cancelaciones meteorológicas, fuerza mayor (temporales, órdenes gubernamentales, huelgas, pandemias, cierres portuarios) o actos de terceros. Nada en estos términos excluye la responsabilidad por fallecimiento o lesiones personales causadas por negligencia, ni cualquier otra responsabilidad que no pueda excluirse según la normativa española de consumo. 12. Divulgación de afiliación Algunos enlaces del Sitio apuntan a operadores o plataformas de reserva de terceros. Cuando recomendamos un operador podemos recibir una comisión si reservas a través nuestro. Esto nunca afecta al precio que pagas y solo destacamos operadores que nosotros mismos contrataríamos. 13. Propiedad intelectual Todo el texto, fotos, vídeos y gráficos del Sitio son © Boat Rental Marbella o se usan bajo licencia. Puedes compartir páginas concretas en redes sociales y enlazar al Sitio libremente. No puedes republicar, raspar (scraping) ni usar el contenido para entrenar modelos de IA sin nuestro permiso escrito. Los nombres de marcas de barcos (Astondoa, Azimut, Mangusta, Ferretti, etc.) pertenecen a sus respectivos fabricantes y se utilizan solo a efectos descriptivos. 14. Ley aplicable y jurisdicción Estos términos se rigen por la legislación española. Cualquier conflicto con un consumidor se resolverá ante los tribunales del lugar de residencia del consumidor dentro de la UE, o ante los tribunales de Marbella (Málaga) para clientes fuera de la UE. Los consumidores también pueden usar la plataforma europea de resolución de litigios en línea en ec.europa.eu/consumers/od

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/experiences/africa-from-marbella-by-boat/
TITLE: See Africa from Marbella by Boat: The Day-Trip Reality
DESCRIPTION: Can you see Africa from Marbella by boat? Yes — the Rif Mountains sit 75 NM south. Honest 10h day-trip reality + charter options. Ask on WhatsApp.

Home › See Africa from Marbella by Boat: The Day-Trip Reality Yes, you can see Africa from Marbella by boat — on a clear morning the Rif Mountains of northern Morocco rise across the Alborán Sea roughly 75 nautical miles to the south. What you can't do is sail there and back in a day. This page is the honest breakdown: what's actually visible from the water, which charter routes give the best African sightlines, and why the romantic idea of a day-trip to Tangier under sail doesn't survive contact with customs, fuel ranges, and the Levante wind. If you want African views with a glass of cava in hand, we'll show you how. If you want to set foot on African soil today, we'll point you to the ferry. The geography in three numbers Marbella sits on the northern shore of the Alborán Sea, the western basin of the Mediterranean. Three numbers matter for anyone asking whether Africa is reachable by boat: 75 NM — straight-line distance from Puerto Banús to the nearest Moroccan coast (Ceuta/Tangier area). 851m — height of Jebel Musa, the prominent African peak visible from Marbella on clear days. 14 NM — width of the Strait of Gibraltar at its narrowest point, west of Tarifa. The 75 NM figure is the critical one. At a cruising speed of 22-25 knots — typical for our Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 — that's three to three and a half hours one way. Round-trip with margin: a full 8-10 hour day burning fuel before you even step off the boat. What you can actually see on a clear day From a charter departing Puerto Banús, the African horizon becomes visible once you're 2-3 NM offshore and the coastal heat haze drops away. On the cleanest days — typically after rain or during a steady Poniente westerly — you'll see: The Rif mountain range as a long ridgeline along the southern horizon Jebel Musa as a distinct triangular peak, one of the two classical Pillars of Hercules The Rock of Gibraltar to the west, often more prominent than Africa itself The Spanish enclave of Ceuta as a thin smudge of land below the mountains Binoculars help. So does altitude — the flybridge of an Azimut 58 gives you a measurably better sightline than a deck-level cockpit. Bring polarised sunglasses to cut Mediterranean glare. Best months and times for African visibility Visibility from Marbella across the Alborán is dictated by humidity, wind direction, and Saharan dust. The pattern is consistent year to year: Period African visibility Notes April-June Excellent Long mornings, low haze, calm seas July-August Moderate Heat haze + occasional Levante dust September-October Excellent Clearest light of the year November-March Variable Sharp after rain, rough sea state Always book a morning slot if Africa is on your wish-list. Departures between 9am and 11am give you 3-4 hours of clear horizon before afternoon haze builds. By 3pm in summer the coastline often disappears even if the morning was crystal clear. Why we don't run day-trips to Morocco Clients regularly ask if we can charter to Tangier for lunch. The answer is no, and here's the full reasoning rather than a vague brush-off: Customs clearance — exiting Schengen by private vessel requires advance Guardia Civil paperwork. Moroccan entry processing in Tangier adds 60-90 minutes per direction. Weather margin — the Strait of Gibraltar is one of the windiest stretches in Europe. Levante easterlies can build from 10 to 30+ knots within hours. You need a confirmed 12-hour weather window. Fuel range — most charter motoryachts can complete the round-trip, but at the cost of cruising slowly and arriving with minimal reserve. Not a sensible day-trip profile. Insurance — our coverage is regional. Cross-border crossings require dedicated policy extensions and licensed offshore crews. If Africa as a destination is the goal, drive an hour west to Tarifa and take the FRS or Inter Shipping fast ferry — it crosses to Tangier-Ville in 60 minutes and costs around €40 each way. The best charter routes for African views If you want African horizon views from a private boat, the route matters more than the boat. Three options from our fleet , ranked by Africa-visibility payoff: 1. Puerto Banús → Estepona → Punta Chullera (8 hours) The westbound run takes you progressively closer to the strait. By Punta Chullera, near the Sotogrande border, you're 60 NM from the African coast with Jebel Musa squarely on the southern horizon. A full-day Astondoa 40 charter at €2,299 covers this comfortably with two swim stops. 2. Puerto Banús → Cabopino → Marbella east (4 hours) Eastbound runs trade African visibility for the dramatic backdrop of La Concha mountain. Africa is still visible but at a steeper angle. Good for guests who prioritise coastal scenery over the strait. 3. Mangusta 80 sunset cruise to Estepona (4 hours minimum) The Mangusta 80 at €4,719 includes a free jet ski and runs a sunset-direction route that puts the African coast in silhouette during golden hour. Photographically the best Africa-view charter we offer. What's included on every charter Whichever route you choose, every booking covers the full experience without bolt-on surprises: Licensed Spanish skipper with local Alborán knowledge Fuel for the agreed route Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava Light snacks (tapas-style platters) Full insurance and safety equipment Spanish IVA (21%) — no hidden tax on your final bill What's not included: lunch ashore at Estepona or Sotogrande, jet ski fuel beyond the included allowance on the Mangusta 80, and any Moroccan landing fees (which is academic given we don't cross). Booking the right boat for Africa views For two to nine guests prioritising clear horizon views, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are the natural pick — the cruising speed gets you west to Sotogrande waters within 90 minutes, leaving plenty of time for swim stops and sightseeing. For groups of 10-12, the Azimut 58 Flybridge gives you the deck height that genuinely improves the sightline across 75 NM of sea. Larger groups or special occasions step up to the Mangusta 80 , where the included jet ski adds a memorable detour on the way back to Puerto Banús. Whichever you choose, message us on WhatsApp with your date and group size — we'll confirm a morning slot that gives the best Africa visibility for that week's weather pattern. Frequently asked questions Can you actually see Africa from Marbella? Yes, on clear days. The Rif Mountains of northern Morocco — particularly Jebel Musa at 851m — are visible from Marbella's higher viewpoints like La Concha and from boats a few miles offshore. Visibility is best in spring and autumn mornings when humidity is low and the Levante wind hasn't kicked up haze. From sea level you typically need to be 3-5 NM offshore for a clean sightline across the 75 NM gap. How far is Africa from Marbella by boat? Roughly 75 nautical miles (140 km) south to the nearest Moroccan coast near Ceuta or Tangier. At a comfortable cruising speed of 22-25 knots, a one-way crossing takes 3 to 3.5 hours in calm conditions. Round-trip that's 6-7 hours of pure navigation, before you factor in customs, fuel, and weather margin — which is why we don't run it as a day charter. Can I do a same-day boat trip from Marbella to Morocco? Not realistically on a private charter. The crossing requires Schengen exit clearance, Moroccan customs entry, and a weather window that holds for 10+ hours. Most clients who want to set foot in Africa drive 1h to Tarifa and take the 1-hour fast ferry to Tangier instead. For boat-based Africa views, we recommend a sunset charter from Puerto Banús toward Estepona — the African coastline is clearest at golden hour. Which Marbella boat charter gets closest to Africa? Sotogrande and Estepona-direction routes put you 5-8 NM closer to the African coast than Marbella centre. From a Tier A charter like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, a 4-hour westbound run reaches the waters off Manilva with Jebel Musa clearly visible to the south on a clear day. For longer-range visibility a Man

Home › Experiences › Bachelor & Hen Parties Marbella has a specific Sunday script for bachelor and hen weekends — and the yacht charter is the anchor activity. This page is the practical playbook: which boat fits which group size, the standard 4-hour itinerary that builds the whole day, BYO rules, DJ add-ons, and how to coordinate the beach-club tender so you arrive when the day is already going. Best boat for the group size Group Boat Price (4 h half-day) 6–8 guests Astondoa 40 €1,299 — €162 per head 9–11 guests (hen + stag sweet spot) Azimut 39 €1,299 — €118 per head 10–12 luxury Mangusta 80 €4,719 — €393 per head, jet ski included For groups over 12 we split into two boats and synchronise itineraries — same price per boat, both arriving at Cabopino together. The standard 4-hour script 14:00 — Boarding at Puerto Banús . Welcome drinks, photos at the pontoon. Ice tubs already chilled. 14:30 — Cast off . Slow cruise west past Marbella Club and Puente Romano. The Golden Mile mansions are part of the show. 15:15 — Anchor stop at Río Verde . Swim, donut, paddleboard. Music up. This is where the day actually starts. 16:30 — Optional beach-club tender to Nikki Beach or Ocean Club. What's included vs paid extras Included in the €749 / €1,299 / €4,719 price: licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks (crisps, almonds, fruit), insurance, VAT, ice tubs, snorkel masks, inflatable donut and paddleboard, Bluetooth sound system. Photographer on board — €200 / hour, we can recommend one. Beach club entry — paid separately at the club, €50–€150 minimum per head. Jet ski hour — €200 (free on Mangusta 80 charter). BYO rules Yes: bring your own spirits, mixers, beer top-ups, additional cava, snacks, costume pieces, inflatable swans, music playlists, decorations. Cans and plastic only on deck — no glass. Spanish marina fines for broken glass overboard are punishing and we enforce this. No confetti, no glitter — anything that ends up in the water is a problem. No open flames — no shisha, no candles, no cake-candle photo moments unless we coordinate ashore. Drugs are an instant return-to-port — security deposit forfeit. Don't. Best months for stag & hen Late June through early September is the season. Sea is 22–24 °C, the marina scene is at full volume, beach clubs are in peak operation. July is the busiest single month — book 4 weeks ahead for Saturday slots. Mid-September keeps the warmth and drops prices 10–15% — best value for groups happy with a slightly quieter scene. Avoid Saturday in August if you want last-minute availability. Booking and deposit Tap Book now top-right.
