TITLE: Tarifa Day Trip by Boat from Marbella
DESCRIPTION: Tarifa day trip from Marbella by boat: 50+ NM each way, 8-12h total, best on fast yachts. Skipper, drinks, IVA included. WhatsApp us to plan it.

Home › Tarifa Day Trip by Boat from Marbella A Tarifa day trip from Marbella by boat is the longest single-day itinerary we run. Puerto Banús to Tarifa is around 52 nautical miles one way, which means a 104 NM round trip, a 10-12 hour charter window, and a fast yacht. It is doable, it is spectacular when the Strait of Gibraltar is calm, and it is the wrong call on a windy day. This guide explains exactly when it works, which boats handle it, and what the day looks like from cast-off to return — see our full Boat Rental Marbella hub if you would rather stay closer to home. The route: Puerto Banús to the Strait of Gibraltar Every charter departs Puerto Banús. The skipper tracks the coast south-west past Estepona (10 NM), Sotogrande (22 NM) and Punta Chullera (28 NM), then crosses the bay of Algeciras with Gibraltar visible to port. From there it is 18 NM of open water to Isla de las Palomas, the official southernmost point of mainland Europe. Africa sits only 14 km across the Strait, and the Rif mountains are usually clear on the horizon. The return leg is identical in distance but often slower — Levante winds tend to build from late morning, putting a head sea on the way back. The skipper plans the day around that, leaving Puerto Banús by 08:00 and aiming to be east of Sotogrande again by 17:00. Best boats for the Tarifa run This itinerary lives or dies on cruising speed and sea-keeping. We will only put you on a yacht that can hold 22+ knots in a moderate chop and carry enough fuel for the full 104 NM. In practice that means: Pershing 46 — 14m, 10 pax, fast planing hull, our default Tarifa boat Fairline Targa 12m — 12m, 10 pax, sporty and economical Azimut 58 Flybridge — 17.6m, 12 pax, comfortable for larger groups Mangusta 80, K80, Canados 86, Ferretti 94 — 24-29m flagships for premium charters The Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Bandido, Dubhe, Lagoon 380, Mariah SX21, Red Tide and 7m speedboat are not offered for Tarifa — range, fuel, or hull type rule them out. If you want a slower, more relaxed day, see our overnight yacht charter or multi-day itineraries instead. Timing, winds and when to book The Strait of Gibraltar has two named winds. Levante blows from the east, Poniente from the west, and either can hit 30-40 knots when the pressure gradient sharpens. Above 25 knots sustained, we do not push past Estepona. The skipper makes the call by 07:00 on departure day using AEMET data and a final look at the swell. Best windows by month, based on our own log: Month Sea temp Wind risk Verdict May 17-19°C Low-medium Good, cool swims June 19-21°C Low Best month July 21-23°C High (Levante) Book flexible dates August 22-24°C High Same — Levante peaks September 22-23°C Low-medium Best month, tied with June October 20-22°C Medium Good early, cooling fast What you actually do once you arrive Most groups expect to dock in Tarifa town for lunch. That is rarely the right call. Tarifa marina is small, busy with kite-school traffic and not built for visiting yachts on a short stop. The better plan is to anchor off Isla de las Palomas or Playa de Los Lances Norte, swim in genuine Atlantic water, and have lunch on board. The crew lays out the included snacks, drinks and bar before you arrive. Cetacean sightings are the other draw. Between Punta Chullera and the Strait you cross known feeding grounds for striped and common dolphin pods. From June to October, pilot whales are routine and orca sightings spike in July-August when tuna shoals move through. The skipper will idle the engines if a pod surfaces nearby — no chasing, no guarantees. Pricing and what is included Tarifa pricing is on request rather than off the standard grid. The day is longer than our published 8-hour rate and burns roughly twice the fuel of a Marbella-Estepona loop. Every charter still includes: Licensed Spanish skipper Fuel for the full itinerary Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks on board Insurance and all safety equipment Spanish IVA at 21% For comparison, a standard 8-hour day on our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) is €2,299 all-in along the Marbella-Estepona coast. The Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum. Tarifa quotes sit above those baselines and we will give you a firm number in writing once we know your date and group size. How to book and what we need from you WhatsApp is the fastest channel. Send your date, group size, and whether you have a preferred boat. We will reply with a quote, a recommended yacht, and the Tarifa-specific safety briefing. A 30% deposit secures the date; balance is due on the day. If weather forces a cancel, you choose between a full refund or a rebook — your call, no quibble. Pair this trip with our other long-range experiences if you want to keep going: a single overnight turns the Tarifa run into a relaxed two-day cruise with an anchor stop in Sotogrande or Gibraltar bay. Frequently asked questions Is a Tarifa day trip from Marbella by boat actually realistic? Yes, but only on a fast yacht and only when the Strait of Gibraltar is calm. The round trip is roughly 104 nautical miles, so you need a boat that cruises at 22-30 knots and a full 10-12 hour window. On a slow displacement boat or a windy Levante day, it is not realistic and we will recommend an overnight charter instead. How long does it take to reach Tarifa by boat from Puerto Banús? At 22 knots cruising speed, expect about 2 hours 20 minutes each way. The Pershing 46 and Mangusta 80 can push closer to 30 knots in flat water, cutting that to under 1 hour 45 minutes. We always plan for slower returns, since afternoon winds tend to stiffen as you approach the Strait. Which boats in your fleet are suitable for the Tarifa run? Fast planing yachts only: the Pershing 46, Fairline Targa 12m, Azimut 58 Flybridge, Mangusta 80, K80, Canados 86 and Ferretti 94. Smaller speedboats and the Lagoon 380 catamaran are not suitable — range, sea state and fuel consumption rule them out. We will match boat to forecast on the day. What does a Tarifa day trip cost from Marbella? Pricing is on request because Tarifa sits outside our standard 2-hour and 8-hour grid. Expect a full-day charter rate plus a fuel surcharge reflecting the extra distance. All charters still include licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, snacks, insurance and 21% Spanish IVA. WhatsApp us with your date for a firm quote. What will we see between Marbella and Tarifa? You pass Estepona, Sotogrande and Gibraltar in the distance, then enter the Strait where Africa is clearly visible only 14 kilometres away. Dolphin pods are routine off Punta Chullera, and pilot whales and orcas are seen between June and October. The skipper slows for sightings when conditions allow. What can we actually do once we reach Tarifa? Most charters anchor off the Isla de las Palomas or Playa de Los Lances for a swim and lunch on board, rather than docking. Tarifa town is busy, kite-surf focused and not yacht-friendly for short stops. A 60-90 minute swim stop on the Atlantic side is the standard plan before heading back. When is the best month to do this trip? Late May through early October, with June and September the sweet spot. July and August can bring the Levante wind, which funnels through the Strait at 25-40 knots and shuts the day down. Spring and autumn give calmer water but cooler swims — sea temperature drops below 19°C outside summer. What happens if the weather turns? Safety call sits with the skipper. If the Levante or Poniente is forecast above 25 knots, we will not push past Estepona — we redirect to a sheltered Marbella itinerary or reschedule at no extra cost. You will know the call by 18:00 the evening before, and again at 07:00 on departure day. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book n

TITLE: Water Sports Yacht Day Marbella: Wakeboard, Banana, Donut, SUP
DESCRIPTION: Water sports yacht day Marbella from €749/2h: wakeboard, banana, donut, SUP, snorkel. Skipper, drinks, VAT included. WhatsApp to book.

Home › Water Sports Yacht Day Marbella: Wakeboard, Banana, Donut, SUP A water sports yacht day in Marbella means leaving Puerto Banús on a private charter with the toys already rigged: wakeboard behind a 14-metre Pershing, banana and donut for the group, SUP boards and snorkel masks for the anchor stops. Tier A boats start at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for a full 8-hour day, with a licensed skipper, drinks and 21% VAT included. For bigger groups the Mangusta 80 ships with a free jet ski from €4,719 for 4 hours. Start at the Boat Rental Marbella hub if you want to compare the full fleet first. What you actually do on a water sports day The day splits into three modes: towed toys, anchor play and cruising. A normal four-hour charter runs roughly 25% wakeboard or surf rope work, 30% banana and donut rotations, 30% anchored at a calm cala with SUP, snorkel and swimming, and the rest under way along the Golden Mile. The skipper sets the order based on wind — towed toys first if the Levante is forecast to build, anchor stops first if the morning is glassy and you want the wake to clean up later. Wakeboard tow speed sits at 18-22 knots behind a planing boat like the Pershing 46 or Fairline Targa 12m . Banana and donut runs cruise at 12-15 knots — fast enough to skip across boat wakes, slow enough that a six-year-old can hold on. SUP tows behind the dinghy or a slow-cruising motorboat work at 8-10 knots. Best boats in our fleet for water sports Not every hull tows a wakeboard cleanly. You want a flat, wide transom, a proper swim platform and enough torque to hold 20 knots through a turn. Three boats stand out: Boat Length Pax Why it works Pershing 46 14m 10 Hard-chine planing hull, holds a clean wake at 20 kts Fairline Targa 12m 12m 10 Big bathing platform, easy rope launches Azimut 58 Flybridge 17.6m 12 Flybridge spotting, shaded lounge between runs Mangusta 80 24m 12 Free jet ski included, garage tender Astondoa 40 12.5m 9 Best Tier A price for SUP + banana day If you are weighing options against a flat-out wake boat, our comparison of the Pershing 46 vs Azimut 39 covers the towing differences in detail. Where we anchor for the flattest water Wakeboard quality is 90% about water surface. The skipper reads the wind and picks one of four go-to spots: Río Verde — 10 minutes east of Puerto Banús, sandy bottom at 8-12 m, glassy most mornings before 11:00. Cala del Faro — small bay 15 minutes west, sheltered from the easterly Levante. Cala Cortés — deeper anchorage, good for the banana when the cala is full of swimmers. Golden Mile corridor — the standard wakeboard run between Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina, depth 8-15 m, sandy bottom. On a Levante day we cruise further west toward Estepona; on a Poniente day we run east toward Cabopino. Sotogrande is too far for a 4-hour charter — that one needs the full 8-hour day. What is included in the price Every water sports charter from our fleet includes the same baseline: licensed Spanish skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks and the 21% Spanish IVA already in the headline number. SUP boards and snorkel masks are on the boat by default. Towed toys — wakeboard, banana, donut — are available on selected hulls and we confirm availability on WhatsApp before you pay a deposit. What you do not pay extra for: skipper tip is not expected (legal in Spain but never demanded), and there is no fuel surcharge on standard Marbella-Estepona-Cabopino routes. Pricing at a glance Boat tier Duration Price What you get Tier A (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) 2 hours €749 SUP, snorkel, skipper, drinks, VAT Tier A 8 hours €2,299 Full day with banana/donut on request Mangusta 80 4 hours (min) €4,719 Free jet ski included Pershing 46, Fairline, Azimut 58 On request WhatsApp Wakeboard-grade towing setup Safety, kids and non-swimmers Every towed toy run uses coastguard-approved life jackets, child and adult sizes, included free. The skipper holds a Spanish commercial licence, carries VHF radio and a first-aid kit, and will not run towed toys in swells above 0.8 metres. Children from age six can ride the banana with a parent. Non-swimmers stay on the swim platform with a jacket and can paddle a SUP in the lee of the hull — the boat itself blocks any chop. If anyone in the group is nervous about open water, mention it on WhatsApp and we pick a sheltered anchor like Cala del Faro for the first hour to let them settle in. What to bring Swimwear, towel and reef-safe sunscreen — the Mediterranean sun at noon is unforgiving. A light layer for the cruise back at 17:00 if the wind has built. GoPro or phone in a waterproof case — wakeboard footage from the swim platform looks great. Cash is unnecessary; we accept card and bank transfer for the balance. Seasickness tablets if you are sensitive — take them 30 minutes before boarding. Everything else — drinks, snacks, SUP, snorkel, life jackets — is on the boat. Pair the day with a sailing experience earlier in the week if you want a slower contrast, or a booze cruise for the evening crowd. How to book WhatsApp is the fastest channel. Tell us the date, group size, whether you want wakeboard specifically, and whether children are riding. We confirm the boat, toys on board and total price within the hour during Spanish business hours. A 30% deposit holds the date; the balance is due on the day, by card or transfer. Cancellation up to 7 days before is fully refundable; inside 7 days we move the booking to another date subject to availability. Frequently asked questions Which boats in your fleet are best for water sports? For towed toys the Pershing 46, Fairline Targa 12m and Azimut 58 Flybridge are ideal — they have the transom space, swim platform and torque to pull a wakeboard cleanly at 20 kts. For larger groups the Mangusta 80 carries a free jet ski. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) handle banana, donut and SUP well at the €749/2h price point. Do I need experience to wakeboard or ride the banana? No. The skipper briefs every guest before the first run and starts towed toys at a slow plane so first-timers can find their balance. Wakeboard beginners usually stand up by the third attempt behind a Pershing 46 at 18-20 kts. Banana and donut runs need zero skill — just hold on. Children from age 6 can ride the banana with a parent on board. What toys are included versus extra? SUP boards, snorkel masks and noodles are included on every charter at no extra cost. Wakeboard, banana and donut are available on selected boats and quoted on WhatsApp depending on date and group size. The Mangusta 80 ships with a complimentary jet ski. We do not invent toy menus — ask us by message and we confirm what is on the boat you book. Where do you anchor for water sports off Marbella? We pick the flattest water of the day. Río Verde (10 minutes east of Puerto Banús) is glassy most mornings. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés sit just west and give shelter when the easterly Levante kicks in. The Golden Mile stretch between Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina is the standard wakeboard corridor — sandy bottom, no rocks, depth 8-15 m. How long does a proper water sports day need? Four hours is the realistic minimum: 15 minutes to leave port, 30 minutes setup, two hours of rotation across wakeboard, banana, donut and SUP, then 45 minutes anchored for swimming, snorkel and lunch. Eight hours (€2,299 on Tier A) lets a group of 8-10 ride properly, take a long anchor break and still cruise back along the Golden Mile. What is included in the price? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks and Spanish IVA at 21%. SUP and snorkel gear are on board. Towed toys (wakeboard, banana, donut) and jet ski availability vary by boat — we confirm on WhatsApp before you pay a deposit. Is it safe for kids and non-swimmers? Yes. Coastguard-approved life jackets in child and adult sizes are mandatory on every

TITLE: Wedding on Boat in Marbella: Boats, Logistics, Legal Note
DESCRIPTION: Wedding yacht charter Marbella — ceremony on a flybridge under La Concha, reception with cava on board, photo coordination. Astondoa 40, Azimut 39 or Mangusta 80. From €1,299.

Home › Experiences › Wedding on Boat A wedding on a yacht in Marbella is the rarest charter we run — usually one Saturday per high-season month — and the most logistically rich. This page covers the actual playbook: which boat for the ceremony vs the reception, whether you need a registrar on board (mostly no — see below), how we coordinate the photographer's shot list, and what the day actually looks like start-to-finish. The legal piece (read this first) A wedding ceremony on a Spanish-flagged charter yacht is not legally binding in Spain unless you've completed the civil paperwork ashore beforehand. What most couples do: complete the civil registration at Marbella town hall a few days earlier (treat as a paperwork formality), then do the actual ceremony on board with a celebrant, family, friends — the day everyone remembers. Religious / symbolic-only ceremonies on board are common and need no paperwork. Tell us which route you want when you enquire. Best boat for a wedding Wedding size Boat Notes Couple + 4–6 closest people (elopement) Astondoa 40 "Fufi" Intimate. Teak and cream interior, one ceremony spot on the bow. €749 / 2h. Up to 11 guests Azimut 39 Flybridge for the ceremony, saloon for the reception. €749 / 2h. 12 guests, full luxury reception Mangusta 80 "Nina" Marble galley supports a proper catered reception. Sea-Doo for the post-vows fun. €4,719 / 4h. 20–30 guests (two boats) Azimut 39 + Astondoa 40 in tandem Both anchor side-by-side at Río Verde for the ceremony, separate reception spaces. Quoted on WhatsApp. The day, hour-by-hour 12:00 — Hair and makeup ashore at the hotel (Don Carlos, Marbella Club, Puente Romano common). We'll coordinate transfer. 14:30 — Boarding at Puerto Banús with the wedding party + photographer. Champagne welcome, white-rose decoration we've pre-loaded. 15:00 — Cast off , slow cruise to the ceremony anchorage (Río Verde or Cala del Faro, picked the morning of based on wind). 15:30 — Anchor and ceremony . Bow set up with the celebrant. Music via the on-board system (sent us your processional + recessional 24h before). 16:00 — Group photos at La Concha and the swim platform. 16:30 — Reception begins. Cava + canapés we've coordinated with the catering team. 18:00 — First dance on the flybridge . Couple-only swim if you want it. 19:30 — Return to Puerto Banús , met by transfers to the dinner venue. What we coordinate vs what you bring We coordinate: the boat, skipper, fuel, drinks (cava + wine + beer on board), light snacks, anchor location selection (wind-aware), basic deck flowers/runner if you ask. Standard inclusions apply. You coordinate (we recommend vendors): celebrant or registrar, hair / makeup, photographer / videographer (your own or a Marbella name we can recommend), wedding cake, catered hot food, DJ for any after-party ashore, hotel and transfers. Marbella wedding photographers we've shot with We don't take a cut and we don't sell photography. But the wedding photographers who deliver consistently good Marbella yacht shoots tend to be: Spanish-based shooters who know the light (sun angle drops fast after 17:00 in spring/autumn), have shot with our crew before (they know the boat's best angles), and bring a second shooter for the dock + reception split. Ask us for a current shortlist when you enquire — we update it every season. Pricing example Typical small-wedding all-in (boat + crew + drinks-on-board + light deck dressing, on the Azimut 39 for 8 guests + couple, 4-hour charter): €1,299 . Add a catered cocktail-hour reception (€55/head x 10) = €1,849. Plus your photographer, celebrant, dinner ashore, hotel. Bigger weddings on the Mangusta 80 start around €4,719 for the boat alone. Booking and lead time Wedding bookings: 3–6 months ahead for July/August Saturdays. Off-season (Oct–May): often available 4 weeks ahead. 50% deposit secures the date, balance 14 days before. Weather backup plan: we always identify a Plan-B date 1 week later when you book. Weather-cancelled weddings get 100% refund or free reschedule. Tap Book now top-right with date, party size, ceremony / reception split, and which boat you've shortlisted. We respond within 30 min during business hours with a full quote and timeline. Follow @boatrentalmarbella Frequently asked questions Can we legally get married on the yacht? The ceremony on the boat is not legally binding in Spain by itself — you complete the civil registration at Marbella town hall beforehand (treat as paperwork), then the on-board ceremony is the actual celebration. Religious or symbolic-only ceremonies need no paperwork. Do you provide a celebrant? No — we can recommend Marbella-based bilingual celebrants (English / Spanish, sometimes French). Or you bring your own from home. The celebrant's fee is separate from the charter. What if the weather is bad? The skipper assesses the night before. If forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 or sea state makes the ceremony unsafe / uncomfortable, we reschedule to the Plan-B date we agreed at booking, or refund 100%. No partial-fee fights. Can we bring our own cake / catering? Yes. We're charter, not catering — most weddings bring an external chef or catering team. We coordinate dock-side pickup and on-board service. Catered platters (€55–€80/head) can also be arranged through our partner caterers. How many guests can come? Up to 12 on the Mangusta 80, 11 on the Azimut 39, 9 on the Astondoa 40. A wine tasting yacht Marbella charter combines the luxury of a private boat with the rich flavours of Andalusia. Depart from Puerto Banús, sip fino sherry as the sun sets over La Concha, and anchor off Cala Cortés with a glass of Ronda red. We handle everything: the skipper, the drinks, the snacks, and the navigation. You just bring your palate. Why combine wine tasting with a yacht charter? Andalusia produces some of Spain’s most distinctive wines—from the dry, saline finos of Jerez to the bold, high-altitude reds of Ronda. Tasting these vintages on the water elevates the experience: the sea air sharpens your senses, and the views of the Costa del Sol provide a stunning backdrop. A Marbella yacht charter lets you explore coves, swim in crystal-clear water, and enjoy a curated wine flight—all without leaving the boat. TITLE: Fishing Boat Rental Marbella 2026: Deep-Sea &amp; Inshore Charters
DESCRIPTION: Fishing boat rental Marbella — our 12.5 m motor yachts (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) can run light tackle and trolling from €749/2h. Plus full guide to deep-sea and inshore fishing in Marbella.

Home › Fishing Boat Rental Marbella 2026: Deep-Sea & Inshore Charters Our fleet right now: two 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts — the Astondoa 40 (9 guests) and Azimut 39 (11 guests). From €749 for 2h , up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day . Skipper, fuel, drinks, insurance and VAT included. Fishing boat rental in Marbella covers two distinct experiences: a quiet 2-hour inshore trip pulling sea bream and mackerel from 20-metre reefs off the Golden Mile, or a 4–8 hour deep-sea expedition chasing dorado and tuna 8–15 nautical miles offshore over deeper seamounts. Prices start at €220 with skipper, mate, rods and bait included. Fishing on our motor yachts Our 12.5 m fleet ( Astondoa 40 , Azimut 39 ) can run light tackle inshore fishing and trolling on request — same pricing as any other charter, €749 for 2 h up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day . For dedicated deep-sea sportfishing with heavy tackle, livewell and a mate, message us and we will source a specialist boat through partners. What you can catch off Marbella, by season Dorado (mahi-mahi) — June to October. Schooling fish, electric strikes on trolled feathers, 4–12 kg average. The headline summer target. Bluefin tuna — May to July. Heavy tackle, stand-up gear, fish ranging 50–150 kg. Strict ICCAT quota: most Marbella charters are catch-and-release for recreational anglers. Amberjack — year-round, peak winter. Jigging or live bait over deep reefs at 30–80 m. Strong fighters, 5–25 kg. Bonito and Atlantic bonito — September to November. Easy fast action, great for kids. Inshore: sea bream, sea bass, mackerel, barracuda — year-round, light tackle, ideal beginner targets. Inshore vs deep-sea — which to book? Inshore (2–4 hours): stay within 2 NM of the coast, fish reefs and wrecks at 15–40 m depth, calm water, lighter tackle, lower price. Best for families, kids, first-timers, anyone who gets queasy. Deep-sea (4–8 hours): run 8–15 NM offshore to seamounts and current edges, troll lures or drift live bait, target bigger species, longer travel time, more boat motion. Best for serious anglers and groups happy to commit a full day. If you're undecided, the half-day inshore is the safer first booking — you can always upgrade next trip. For everything we offer across the fleet, the boat rental Marbella hub compares fishing alongside yachts and catamarans. Departure ports Puerto Banús hosts the biggest deep-sea sportfishers (10–12 m Bertram-class). Cabopino is the cheapest pickup for inshore trips and the closest harbour to the productive eastern reefs around Río Real. La Bajadilla fishing port (next to Marbella Marina) keeps the working-fleet feel and is where local skippers with 30+ years on the water are based. Estepona works for west-bound offshore tuna trips when current is favourable. What's included on every fishing charter Licensed skipper plus a mate (deep-sea) or just skipper (small inshore) Rods, reels and complete terminal tackle (hooks, leaders, swivels) Live bait (sardines, mackerel, squid) appropriate to target species Ice box for your catch Soft drinks and water Filleting and bagging of keepers at the dock Fishing licence coverage under the boat's commercial permit Not included: lunch (BYO sandwich or order a catered lunch box), alcohol (most skippers ask you to wait until you're back at the dock), and trip insurance for offshore days. Sample full-day offshore itinerary 07:00 Boarding at Puerto Banús pontoon 4, fuel and bait loaded the night before. 07:30 Run 45 minutes south-east to the Marbella seamount. 08:15 Set the trolling spread — 4 rods, lures alternating between dorado feathers and tuna konas. 11:00 First drift over deeper reef for amberjack and possible swordfish bonus. 13:00 Lunch break, drift fishing continues with lighter gear. 14:30 Run east towards Calahonda seamount for the afternoon bite. 16:00 Reel in, run back to Puerto Banús, mate fillets your catch on the dock. Booking tips For deep-sea, book a forecast window — Marbella's east wind (levante) above 15 knots makes serious offshore work uncomfortable. The skipper will reschedule weather days at no cost. For dorado specifically, July through September is peak — book 2 weeks out for a Saturday slot. Inshore trips can usually be booked same-day on WhatsApp. Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Inshore 2-hour trips start at €220 (low season) or €260 (high season) on a 6–8 m boat for up to 6 anglers. Deep-sea half-day (4 hours) costs €450 on a 9–11 m sportfisher. Full-day 8-hour offshore charters run €780–€950 depending on boat size and fuel allowance. All include skipper, mate, rods, reels, bait and tackle. What fish will I catch? From May to October the headliner is dorado (mahi-mahi) — schooling fish, fast bites, great for first-time anglers. Bluefin tuna season is May–July; expect 50–150 kg fish on heavy tackle. Amberjack are reliable year-round at 30–80 m depth. Bonito and Atlantic bonito show up September–November. Inshore trips target sea bream, mackerel, sea bass and small barracuda. Do I need a fishing licence? Yes — Andalucía requires a Licencia de Pesca Marítima de Recreo for sport fishing. Reputable charters include the licence in the boat's commercial permit, so as a paying guest you're covered. You'll need ID on board (passport or EU card) and the skipper handles paperwork. Can I keep the fish? Yes, within the daily quota. Andalucía allows 5 kg per person per day for recreational sea fishing, plus one fish over the size limit. Bluefin tuna is catch-and-release only during open season unless your skipper holds a commercial bluefin quota. Is it suitable for beginners? Yes — the mate does the heavy lifting and walks you through reel mechanics in 5 minutes. Inshore trips are the easiest first experience: shorter run-out, calmer water, lighter tackle. What you can do on the water The Marbella coast is the perfect playground for a one-hour Sea-Doo session. The 200 m coastal swim-zone rule applies, but outside it you have miles of open water along the Golden Mile and east towards Cabopino. Typical 1-hour itineraries: Puerto Banús → Río Verde → back — quick tour past the marquee beach clubs (Nikki Beach, Ocean Club). Puerto Banús → Marbella Marina → back — see the Golden Mile from the water, including Marbella Club and Puente Romano. Combine with a yacht anchor stop — if you've booked a charter, anchor off Río Verde and let guests take turns on the jet ski for 10 minutes each. The Sea-Doo experience Modern Sea-Doo PWC are easy to ride — twist-grip throttle, lanyard kill switch, intuitive handlebars. The 10-minute on-the-dock briefing covers everything you need to know. You will: Throttle and brake. Right thumb forward to go, release to coast. Sea-Doos have an iBR brake-and-reverse system that makes docking much easier than older jet skis. Kill switch lanyard. Clipped to your life jacket. If you fall off, the engine stops automatically. The 200 m rule. Stay outside the swim-buoy line along every Marbella beach. Penalties are stiff and the Guardia Civil del Mar patrol. Wake awareness. Slow inside the Puerto Banús harbour mouth and when passing other boats — keep your wake low. After the briefing you take the Sea-Doo out for a controlled lap of the marina with our staff watching, and from there you're on your own. What's included on every jet ski rental Sea-Doo personal watercraft for the duration Life jacket (rider + passenger) On-the-dock briefing — 10 minutes, plus a controlled lap of the marina Fuel for normal coastal use (about €15 of petrol per hour) Insurance — third-party + damage cover, deductible held against credit card pre-authorisation Spanish IVA (VAT, 21%) Dry bag for your phone and keys Not included: GoPro / camera rental (bring your own, we can mount it), professional photographer (€200 for an hour-long shoot on a chase boat — ask when you book), and gratuity for the briefing crew. TITLE: Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella 2026: Superyachts &amp; Crewed Charters
DESCRIPTION: Luxury yacht charter Marbella from €749 on our 12.5 m Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 motor yachts — licensed skipper, drinks and VAT included. Crewed charters from Puerto Banús.

Home › Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella 2026: Superyachts & Crewed Charters Our fleet right now: two 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts — the Astondoa 40 (9 guests) and Azimut 39 (11 guests). From €749 for 2h , up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day . Skipper, fuel, drinks, insurance and VAT included. Luxury yacht rental in Marbella means stepping onto an 18-to-30-metre crewed yacht at Puerto Banús, being greeted by a stewardess with a chilled glass, and handing the day over to a professional crew while you and your guests do nothing but enjoy it. Day charters start at €2,400 and scale up to €18,000 for the largest superyachts. Weekly charters begin around €25,000 plus expenses. Where the line is Yacht charter in Marbella exists on a continuum, and "luxury" is the top end of it. Practical breakpoints: 10-15 m motor yacht: private day boat. Captain only. Comfortable but not luxury. Covered on the yacht charter Marbella page. 18-22 m crewed yacht: entry-level luxury. Captain + stewardess. Hospitality-fitted interior, real galley, fold-out swim platform, water toys included. This is where most luxury bookings sit. 24-30 m yacht: superyacht. Full crew (captain, mate, chef, stewardess). Multiple staterooms, chef-prepared meals, on-board tender. Day charter possible but most owners prefer multi-day. 30 m+ megayacht: charter market thins out fast. Usually weekly or longer, crew of 5-10, full-board. Our fleet — luxury-tier amenities at the 12.5 m size class We currently run two crewed 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts at €749 → €2,299 per charter — both with cream-leather interiors, AC saloon, two cabins, and the same skipper-fuel-drinks-insurance-VAT inclusion as larger luxury yachts. See Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 for full specs. For 18 m+ proper superyachts (weekly charters, APA model, full crew with chef) we can source through partners — message us with dates and group size. Weekly charter and the APA model Multi-day and weekly charters on 24 m+ yachts use the global APA system. The base charter fee covers the yacht, captain and crew salaries. On top of that you pre-pay an Advance Provisioning Allowance (APA) of 25-30% which funds fuel, marina fees, food, drink and any extra crew expenses during the trip. Captain accounts for every euro and refunds the unused portion at the end. A 24 m yacht for a week in low season: ~€25,000 + ~€7,000 APA. Same yacht in August: ~€40,000 + €10,000 APA. The crew and what they actually do Captain: commands the yacht — navigation, anchoring, all decisions affecting safety. Holds at least a 200 GT commercial licence. Your single point of contact for the trip. Stewardess: guest service. Welcomes you on board, mixes drinks, plates the food, handles cabin turn-down on overnight charters, keeps the saloon and decks tidy. The stewardess role on a luxury yacht is the difference between "nice boat" and "luxury experience". Chef: from 24 m up. You fill in a preference sheet before the charter — favourite cuisines, dietary requirements, allergies — and the chef builds a tailored menu for the trip. Provisioning is done from local markets (Mercado de Puerto Banús, La Cañada). Deckhand / mate: handles lines, fenders, tender operation, water-toys deployment, and keeps the exterior immaculate. Often a deck cadet building sea time toward their captain qualification. Where you can go Day charters cover the Costa del Sol — Sotogrande in the west, Cabopino in the east, with the Golden Mile coast in between. A long day with an early start reaches Gibraltar by boat from Marbella (100 NM round trip, only viable on flat-sea days). Overnight luxury charters extend the cruising window along the Marbella coast and the Strait. Weekly bookings out of Marbella typically anchor the itinerary around Puerto Banús and the Golden Mile stops most guests want to see. The booking process WhatsApp inquiry — dates, group size, length (day/weekend/week), special requirements. Shortlist — we send 2-4 specific yachts with photos, plans, crew bios, day rates and any APA estimates. Hold — preferred yacht is held for 48 hours while you decide. Contract — MYBA Worldwide standard charter agreement, 50% deposit at signing. Preference sheet — sent 2-3 weeks ahead, captures food/drink/itinerary preferences. Boarding — exact pier number sent 24 hours ahead, balance paid on or before the day. For the full picture of what else is available across the fleet, see the boat rental Marbella hub. TITLE: Mangusta 80 vs Ferretti 94: Which Luxury Yacht for Marbella?

Home › Mangusta 80 vs Ferretti 94: Which Luxury Yacht for Marbella? Choosing between the Mangusta 80 and the Ferretti 94 for your Marbella yacht charter is a matter of style, space, and budget. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Cancellation Policy: What You Should Know
DESCRIPTION: Learn the Marbella yacht charter cancellation policy: refund terms, weather clauses, and deposit rules. Book with confidence for your Puerto Banús trip.

Home › Marbella Yacht Charter Cancellation Policy: What You Should Know Booking a Marbella yacht charter cancellation policy is straightforward, but knowing the rules can save you money and stress. Whether you're planning a sunset cruise on an Astondoa 40 or a full-day adventure on a Mangusta 80, our cancellation terms protect both parties. This guide covers deposit requirements, refund timelines, weather clauses, and tips for rescheduling—so you can book with confidence from Puerto Banús. Deposit and Payment Terms To secure any yacht from our fleet—from the Marbella yacht charter lineup to specific models like the Azimut 58 Flybridge—a 50% deposit is required at booking. The remaining 50% is due 7 days before departure. For last-minute bookings (within 7 days), full payment is taken upfront. All payments include Spanish IVA (21%), and we accept bank transfers and major credit cards. Cancellation Windows and Refunds Our refund policy is tiered by how far in advance you cancel: 30+ days before charter: Full refund of all payments. 14–29 days before charter: 50% refund of total charter cost. Less than 14 days before charter: No refund. Cancellations must be submitted in writing via email or WhatsApp. Verbal requests are not accepted. Refunds are processed within 5–10 business days to the original payment method. Weather-Related Cancellations The safety of our guests and crew is paramount. If the licensed skipper determines that weather conditions pose a risk (e.g., winds exceeding 25 knots, heavy storms, or dangerous sea states), the charter will be cancelled with a full refund or the option to reschedule. We do not cancel for light rain or moderate chop—only for conditions that compromise safety. This policy applies to all boats, including the Ferretti 94 and smaller vessels like the Mariah SX21. Late Arrivals and No-Shows If you arrive late to Puerto Banús or fail to show up, no refund is given. The charter departs at the booked time and returns as scheduled, regardless of your arrival. We recommend arriving 15 minutes before departure to check in and meet the crew. This rule applies to all charters, from the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) to the Mangusta 80 (Grey). Rescheduling and Date Changes You can change your charter date at no charge up to 14 days before departure, subject to availability. Within 14 days, date changes are treated as cancellations and follow the refund policy above. To reschedule, contact us via WhatsApp or email with your preferred new dates. We'll confirm availability and adjust the booking. Special Cases: Private Events and Corporate Charters For private events, corporate charters, or multi-boat bookings (e.g., a fleet of several Marbella yacht charter vessels), separate cancellation terms may apply. These are agreed in writing at the time of booking. Always review your contract for specific clauses regarding deposits, refunds, and force majeure. How to Cancel or Modify Your Booking To cancel or modify your charter, send an email to our bookings team or a WhatsApp message with your booking reference. We will confirm receipt and process the refund or change according to the policy. Keep a record of your cancellation request until the refund is received. For urgent changes, WhatsApp is fastest. Frequently asked questions What is the deposit required to book a Marbella yacht charter? A 50% deposit is required to secure any charter from our fleet. The remaining balance is due 7 days before departure for standard bookings, or immediately for last-minute charters (within 7 days). Deposits are taken via bank transfer or card. Can I get a full refund if I cancel my yacht charter? Yes, if you cancel at least 30 days before the charter date, you receive a full refund of any payments made. Cancellations between 14 and 29 days receive a 50% refund. No refunds are given for cancellations less than 14 days before departure. What happens if bad weather forces cancellation? If the licensed skipper deems conditions unsafe (e.g., winds over 25 knots, heavy storms), you get a full refund or the option to reschedule at no extra cost. We do not cancel for light rain or moderate chop—only for safety risks. Do I get a refund if I arrive late to Puerto Banús? No. Late arrivals or no-shows are not refundable. The charter departs at the booked time and returns as scheduled, regardless of your arrival time. We recommend arriving 15 minutes early at the marina. How do I cancel my booking? All cancellation requests must be sent in writing via email or WhatsApp to our team. Verbal cancellations are not accepted. We will confirm receipt and process the refund according to the policy timeline. Can I change the date of my charter instead of cancelling? Yes, date changes are allowed up to 14 days before the charter at no charge, subject to availability. Within 14 days, changes are treated as cancellations and subject to the refund policy. Contact us to check availability. How long does it take to receive a refund? Refunds are processed within 5–10 business days after we confirm the cancellation. The amount is returned to the original payment method (bank transfer or card). International transfers may take longer depending on your bank. Is the cancellation policy the same for all boats in your fleet? Yes, our standard cancellation policy applies to all boats in our fleet, including the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80, and others. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter License Requirements: Do You Need One?
DESCRIPTION: Do you need a licence to charter a yacht in Marbella? Find out the Marbella yacht charter license requirements, plus boat specs, prices, and inclusions.

Home › Marbella Yacht Charter License Requirements: Do You Need One? Planning a yacht charter in Marbella and wondering about the Marbella yacht charter license requirements ? You don't need one. All our charters include a fully licensed skipper who takes care of everything—navigation, mooring, safety, and even the drinks. You just show up, relax, and enjoy the Mediterranean. Here's everything you need to know about licences, age limits, and what's included. No Licence Needed for Our Charters Every boat in our fleet departs from Puerto Banús with a professional skipper. That means zero paperwork, no exams, and no prior experience. Whether you're on a Boat Rental Marbella Astondoa 40 or a Mangusta 80, the skipper handles the helm. You simply sit back and take in the views of La Concha, the Golden Mile, and the Costa del Sol coastline. What About Bareboat Charters? If you want to rent a boat without a skipper (bareboat), Spanish law requires a recreational licence called the PER (Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo). This allows you to operate vessels up to 15m in length. However, we don't offer bareboat hire—all our charters are skippered. So for our fleet, a licence is never needed. Age and ID Requirements You must be at least 18 to book a charter. All passengers need a valid ID or passport, which the skipper checks at departure. There's no upper age limit, but everyone should be comfortable boarding and moving around the boat. Life jackets are provided for all sizes, including children. Our Fleet at a Glance We have 18 boats ranging from a 6.4m speedboat to a 29m Ferretti 94. All depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. Here are the highlights: Boat Length Max Pax Price From Astondoa 40 12.5m 9 €749/2h Azimut 39 12.5m 11 €749/2h Mangusta 80 (White/Grey) 24m 12 €4,719/4h Ferretti 94 29m 12 On request For all other boats, pricing is on request via WhatsApp. Every charter includes fuel, a licensed skipper, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%). Can You Drive the Boat Yourself? Yes, with the skipper's permission. If you're curious about handling a 12.5m Azimut or a 24m Mangusta, the skipper can let you take the helm in open water. This is a great way to get a feel for yacht handling without needing a licence. The skipper remains responsible for safety, so you can learn in a controlled environment. Local Destinations You Can Visit From Puerto Banús, you can cruise to Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or head west to Estepona and Sotogrande. The skipper knows the best spots for swimming, snorkelling, and lunch at a beach club. No licence required—just pick your destination and enjoy. International Licence Holders If you already hold an International Certificate of Competence (ICC), RYA, or USCG licence, it's not needed for our charters. But it's a nice backup. For bareboat hire elsewhere in Spain, you'll need to check with local authorities whether your licence is recognised. Frequently Asked Questions Do I need a licence to charter a yacht in Marbella? No, not for a skippered charter. All our boats come with a licensed skipper who handles navigation, mooring, and safety. You simply relax and enjoy the coast. If you want to rent a boat without a skipper (bareboat), you'll need a Spanish recreational licence (PER) or an equivalent international certificate. What is the PER licence and do I need it? The Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo (PER) is a Spanish qualification for operating recreational boats up to 15m in length. It requires theory and practical exams. For our charters, you don't need it—our skippers take care of everything. Only consider it if you plan to hire a bareboat in Spain. Can I drive the boat myself during a skippered charter? Yes, with the skipper's permission and supervision, you can take the helm in open water. The skipper remains responsible for safety and navigation. This is a great way to get a feel for handling a boat without needing a licence. What if I have a foreign boating licence? International licences (ICC, RYA, USCG) are not required for our skippered charters. However, for bareboat hire in Spain, you may need to exchange your licence for a Spanish one or obtain a temporary permit. Check with the local maritime authority. Is there an age limit for chartering a yacht in Marbella? You must be at least 18 years old to book a charter. There's no upper age limit, but all passengers must be physically able to board and move around the boat. Children are welcome, and life jackets are provided for all sizes. What documents do I need to bring on the day? Just a valid ID or passport for all passengers. No licence, no certificate. The skipper will check your ID at departure. We recommend bringing sunscreen, sunglasses, and a light jacket for the evening. Can I charter a boat without a skipper in Marbella? Yes, but only if you hold a valid Spanish recreational licence (PER or similar). Our fleet is available for skippered charters only—we don't offer bareboat hire. If you want to go solo, you'll need to approach a bareboat specialist with your licence. What happens if I have a licence but still want a skipper? No problem. You can still book a skippered charter and enjoy a stress-free day. Some experienced boaters prefer having a skipper to handle mooring and local knowledge. Just let us know when booking. Ready to book? Check our full fleet on Boat Rental Marbella and choose your perfect boat. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/marbella-yacht-charter-weather-august/
TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Weather in August: Hottest Month Guide
DESCRIPTION: Plan your Marbella yacht charter in August with our guide to the hottest month. Average 31°C, calm seas, and 12 hours of sun. Book your boat today.

Home › Marbella Yacht Charter Weather in August: Hottest Month Guide August is the hottest and sunniest month for a Marbella yacht charter . With average highs of 31°C, sea temperatures of 26°C, and 12 hours of daily sunshine, it’s the peak of the Mediterranean summer. This guide covers everything you need to know about Marbella yacht charter weather in August – from wind patterns to peak-season tips – so you can plan the perfect day on the water. Average August Weather in Marbella August is Marbella's hottest month. Expect clear blue skies, negligible rain, and a UV index of 9–10 (very high). Here’s the breakdown: High temperature: 31°C (88°F) Low temperature: 22°C (72°F) Sea temperature: 26°C (79°F) Sunshine: 12 hours/day Rainfall: Wind: 5–15 knots (light thermal breezes) These conditions are ideal for cruising, swimming, and anchoring at calas like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés . The sea is calmest in the morning, with a gentle breeze picking up by midday. Best Time of Day for Your Charter To avoid the peak heat, consider a morning or late-afternoon departure. A 10:00–14:00 slot lets you enjoy the calmest sea and cooler temperatures. For sunset lovers, a 16:00–20:00 charter offers golden light and a dip before heading back to Puerto Banús . Our Marbella yacht charter weather August guide recommends bringing a light jacket for the return leg – evening breezes can drop to 22°C. Home › Marbella Yacht Charter Weather in July: Peak Season Heat July in Marbella is the heart of summer — daytime highs hit 30°C, the sea temperature climbs to 23–25°C, and rain is virtually non-existent. For a Marbella yacht charter weather July is about as good as it gets: 12+ hours of sunshine, calm seas, and a cooling breeze off the Mediterranean. Whether you're planning a 2-hour spin from Puerto Banús or an 8-hour cruise to Estepona, this guide covers what to expect and how to pick the right boat for peak season. Average July Temperatures and Conditions Daytime highs average 30°C, with overnight lows around 22°C. Humidity sits at 50–60%, so the 'feels like' temperature can reach 33°C in direct sun. The Douglas sea scale is 1–2 (slight to smooth), with swell height under 0.5m near the coast. Westerly winds (Poniente) bring flat seas; easterly Levante winds can create a light chop of 0.3–0.8m but rarely exceed 1m. For the most stable ride, morning departures (9:00–12:00) are best. Afternoons can see a thermal breeze pick up, but it's still safe for all boat sizes in our fleet. TITLE: Marbella Yacht Charter Weather in May: What to Expect
DESCRIPTION: Plan your Marbella yacht charter in May with our guide to weather, sea conditions, and top tips. Average 22°C, 10h sun, calm seas. Book now.

Home › Marbella Yacht Charter Weather in May: What to Expect Planning a Marbella yacht charter in May means choosing one of the best windows of the year. The Mediterranean is waking up from winter, the sun is warm but not scorching, and the sea is calm. Whether you're eyeing a luxury motor yacht or a speedy rib, May delivers reliable conditions. Here's exactly what to expect from the weather, sea state, and how to make the most of your day on the water. Average Temperatures and Sunshine May in Marbella brings average daytime highs of 22°C, frequently nudging 26°C on the warmest afternoons. Overnight lows hover around 15°C, so evenings on deck are pleasant with a light jacket. You'll enjoy roughly 10 hours of sunshine per day – sunrise around 07:10, sunset near 21:15. UV index is moderate to high (6-7), so sunscreen is essential even if it feels cool. Sea Temperature and Swimming Conditions The Mediterranean off Marbella warms to 17-19°C in May. That's refreshing – not bath-warm like August's 26°C. Most guests do swim, especially after a few hours of sun. The water is clear, with visibility often exceeding 15 metres. If you're sensitive to cooler water, a short wetsuit top makes a difference. Popular swimming spots like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés offer sheltered coves where the water warms slightly. Wind and Sea State May is characterised by light to moderate winds, typically 5-15 knots from the west or southwest. The Mediterranean is generally calm, with wave heights rarely exceeding 0.5 metres (Douglas scale 1-3). Mornings are often glassy calm, with a gentle sea breeze picking up by midday. This is ideal for all vessel types – from the nimble Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) to the stately Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). The occasional Levante (easterly) wind can bring a short chop, but our skippers easily adjust the itinerary. Rainfall and Cloud Cover May is dry: average rainfall is just 30mm, spread over 4-5 days. Showers are typically brief – a passing cloud that clears within 30 minutes. Cloud cover averages 30-40%, meaning mostly blue skies. The chance of a full-day washout is extremely low. If a shower does appear, your yacht's interior provides a comfortable refuge. What to Pack for a May Charter Light layers: T-shirts, shorts, a jumper or windbreaker for evenings. Swimwear and a towel (towels are provided on all our charters). High-SPF sunscreen (SPF 50+ recommended), sunglasses, and a hat. Comfortable non-slip shoes (deck shoes or trainers). A light waterproof jacket – just in case of a passing shower. Best Time of Day for Your Charter Morning charters (10:00-14:00) offer the calmest seas and cooler temperatures – perfect for a relaxed cruise to Cabopino or Estepona. Afternoon charters (14:00-18:00) are warmer and ideal for swimming and sunbathing. Evening charters (18:00-sunset) are magical: the light softens, the sea flattens, and you'll watch the sun dip behind La Concha mountain. For couples or small families, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is a cosy option starting at €749 for 2 hours. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, and VAT. Comparing May with Other Months May sits in the sweet spot between spring and summer. It's warmer than April (18°C average) but cooler and less crowded than June (26°C). Sea temperature is 2-3°C cooler than June, but the lack of humidity makes it more comfortable on deck. If you want excellent weather without peak-season prices, Marbella yacht charter in May is hard to beat. Frequently asked questions Is May a good month for a yacht charter in Marbella? Crowds are smaller than in summer, so you'll find more availability and better rates. What should I wear for a yacht charter in Marbella in May? Yes, but the sea temperature is around 17-19°C, which many find refreshing rather than warm. Most guests do swim, especially in the afternoon when the water has warmed slightly. If you're sensitive to cooler water, a short wetsuit top can help. What is the wind like in Marbella in May? Wind conditions are generally light to moderate, averaging 5-15 knots. The prevailing wind is from the west or southwest. Sometimes a light Levante (easterly) breeze picks up, but it rarely disrupts sailing. Calm mornings are common, with a gentle sea breeze in the afternoon. Are there any weather risks for yacht charters in May? May is low-risk. Our team will tailor a package for you. Frequently asked questions What is included in a motor yacht rental in Marbella? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No extra charges for these items. How much does it cost to rent a motor yacht in Marbella? Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours and go up to €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) are €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours and include a free jet ski. Other yachts are priced on request. Where do the motor yachts depart from in Marbella? All our motor yachts depart from Puerto Banús, the famous marina in Marbella. You can also arrange pickup at Marbella Marina or other nearby points upon request. How many people can a motor yacht accommodate? Capacity varies by boat: from 5 guests (Dubhe, Mariah SX21) up to 12 guests (Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Canados 86, Ferretti 94, K80). Most mid-range boats hold 9–11 guests. Can I bring my own food and drinks on the yacht? Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our standard provision includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and snacks. We also offer catering upgrades – ask via WhatsApp. What is the best time of year for a motor yacht rental in Marbella? The season runs from April to October, with peak conditions June–September (sea temp 22–26°C, air 28–35°C). May and October offer quieter marinas and milder temperatures. Year-round rentals possible with weather check. Do I need a boating license to charter a motor yacht? No. All our charters include a licensed skipper who handles navigation, docking, and safety. TITLE: Overnight Yacht Rental Marbella: Sleep Aboard Guide
DESCRIPTION: Book an overnight yacht rental Marbella and sleep aboard in Puerto Banús. Prices from €2,299 for 8 hours. Speak to our team today.

Home › Overnight Yacht Rental Marbella: Sleep Aboard Guide Sleeping aboard a yacht in Marbella is the ultimate way to experience the Costa del Sol after dark. With overnight yacht rental Marbella , you can anchor in a quiet cove or stay dockside at Puerto Banús, wake up to sea views, and enjoy a morning swim before heading back. Our fleet includes everything from the 12.5m Astondoa 40 to the 29m Ferretti 94, all moored in Marbella's most glamorous marina. Why Choose an Overnight Yacht Rental in Marbella? An overnight charter lets you escape the crowds and enjoy the Mediterranean at your own pace. Instead of rushing back to port, you can watch the sunset from the flybridge, dine under the stars, and sleep in a cabin with the gentle sound of waves. It's ideal for couples celebrating an anniversary, groups of friends on holiday, or families looking for a unique experience. Puerto Banús is lively at night, but from the deck of your yacht you get front-row seats to the glittering marina lights without the noise. Our Overnight Fleet: Which Yacht Fits Your Group? We offer a range of yachts suitable for overnight stays, each with comfortable cabins and onboard facilities. Here's a quick overview: Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – €749/2h → €2,299/8h. Perfect for small groups or couples. Two cabins, saloon, and a spacious sundeck. Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – Same pricing as Astondoa. Slightly more cabin space, ideal for families. Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – From €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a free jet ski. Three cabins, flybridge, and a large saloon. Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) – Pricing on request. Luxurious with four cabins, a master suite, and a jacuzzi on the flybridge. Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) – A sportier option for smaller groups, with one cabin and a cockpit perfect for al fresco dining. For a full list, visit our boat rental Marbella page. What's Included in Your Overnight Charter? Every overnight charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). For Mangusta 80 bookings, a jet ski is included free for the duration. You can also arrange catering, extra drinks, or a private chef – just let us know when booking. The skipper stays onboard overnight, ensuring your safety and handling any questions. Where Will You Sleep? Popular Anchorage Spots Your skipper can recommend several spots depending on the weather and your preferences. Popular overnight locations include: Cala del Faro – A quiet cove near the Marbella lighthouse, calm waters and stunning sunrise views. Cala Cortés – Sheltered bay with clear water, ideal for a morning swim. Puerto Banús dock – Stay in the marina if you want to step ashore for dinner or nightlife. The yacht remains safe and secure. Cabopino – A short cruise east, with a sandy beach and relaxed vibe. Your skipper will anchor or dock as you wish, and you can adjust the plan during the charter. How to Book an Overnight Yacht in Marbella Booking is straightforward. Contact us via WhatsApp or phone – we'll confirm availability for your preferred date and yacht. A deposit secures the charter, and the balance is due on the day. We recommend booking at least a week in advance for overnight stays, especially in summer (June–September). You can also check our Marbella yacht charter page for the full fleet and pricing. Frequently Asked Questions Can I sleep overnight on a yacht in Marbella? Yes, you can sleep aboard many of our yachts moored in Puerto Banús. Overnight charters typically run from late afternoon to the next morning, with the yacht remaining docked or anchored near Marbella's coast. All bookings include a skipper who stays onboard, and you'll have access to cabins, bathrooms, and the saloon. Which yachts are available for overnight rental in Marbella? Our overnight fleet includes the Astondoa 40 (up to 9 guests), Azimut 39 (11 guests), Mangusta 80 (12 guests), and larger models like the Ferretti 94 (12 guests). Each yacht has sleeping cabins, a galley, and onboard amenities. Smaller boats like the Bandido (8 guests) are also suitable for shorter overnight trips. What is included in an overnight yacht rental Marbella? Every overnight charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). For Mangusta 80 bookings, a jet ski is included free for the duration. Food catering can be arranged on request. How much does an overnight yacht rental cost in Marbella? Tier A yachts (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. Mangusta 80 models start at €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter. For other yachts, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp. Overnight stays typically require a full-day or extended charter. Where do overnight yacht charters depart from? All our yachts depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's premier marina. You'll board at our designated dock, and the skipper will take you along the Costa del Sol. Overnight stays can be anchored near Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or within the marina itself, depending on your preference. Can I bring my own food and drinks on an overnight yacht? Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks. Our standard package includes soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. If you'd like a catered meal, let us know in advance and we can arrange it at an additional cost. The yacht's galley is available for storage. Is a skipper required for overnight yacht rental? Yes, a licensed skipper is included with every charter. They will handle navigation, docking, and safety. You do not need a boating license. The skipper stays onboard overnight and ensures the yacht is secure. You are free to relax and enjoy the experience. How many people can sleep on a yacht in Marbella? Capacity varies by yacht: the Astondoa 40 sleeps up to 9 guests, Azimut 39 sleeps 11, Mangusta 80 sleeps 12, and the Ferretti 94 also sleeps 12. Smaller boats like the Bandido sleep 8. Cabins are air-conditioned and include en-suite bathrooms where available. ★★★★★ 4.9 · 87 verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/privacy/
TITLE: Privacy Policy — Boat Rental Marbella
DESCRIPTION: Privacy policy for boatrentalinmarbella.com — what data we collect when you contact us via WhatsApp, email or the booking form, how we use it, and your GDPR rights.

Privacy Policy Last updated: 1 June 2026 This privacy policy explains how Boat Rental Marbella (operating the website boatrentalinmarbella.com , hereafter "we", "us", "the Site") collects, uses and protects your personal data when you visit the Site, contact us via WhatsApp, email or the booking form, or charter a boat through us. We comply with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (Regulation (EU) 2016/679, "GDPR") and Spanish data-protection law (LOPDGDD 3/2018). 1. Who is the data controller? The data controller for personal data collected through this Site is: Boat Rental Marbella Marbella, Andalucía, Spain (postal code 29602) WhatsApp / phone: +358 400 406 194 2. What data do we collect? We only collect data that you give us, or that is generated automatically when you browse: Booking and enquiry data — name, phone number, email address, date(s) of intended charter, group size, departure port, language preference, and any free-text message you send via the booking form, WhatsApp, email, or Instagram/Facebook DM. Payment data — for confirmed bookings, payments are processed by the boat operator's own payment provider (typically Stripe, Revolut Business, or bank transfer). We do not store full card numbers on this Site. Technical data — IP address, browser type, device type, referring page, pages viewed, and timestamps. Collected via standard server logs and Google Analytics 4 (measurement ID G-SPMRYJVTHD). Cookies — see section 7 below. 3. Why do we use it (legal basis)? To answer your enquiry and arrange your charter — legal basis: performance of a contract or pre-contractual steps at your request (GDPR art. 6(1)(b)). To share your booking with the relevant boat operator (e.g. the skipper, charter company, or marina) so they can run the trip — legal basis: contract (art. 6(1)(b)). To comply with Spanish tax and maritime law — e.g. issuing invoices, recording passenger lists where the captain requires them — legal basis: legal obligation (art. 6(1)(c)). To improve the Site and measure traffic — legal basis: legitimate interest in operating an effective website (art. 6(1)(f)), with anonymised analytics where possible. To send you marketing emails or WhatsApp messages — only with your explicit consent, which you can withdraw at any time (art. 6(1)(a)). We do not currently run a marketing newsletter. 4. Who do we share it with? We only share your data with parties that need it to deliver your charter or run the Site: The boat operator / skipper assigned to your charter (name, phone, date, group size). Payment processors (Stripe, Revolut, etc.) when you pay online. Communication providers — WhatsApp (Meta Platforms Ireland Ltd.), Gmail / Google Workspace, Instagram and Facebook (Meta) for the messages you send through those channels. Hosting and analytics — GitHub Pages (Microsoft Corp., US), Cloudflare, Google Analytics 4 (Google Ireland Ltd.). Tax authorities, insurers and law enforcement if we are legally required to do so. Some of these providers are based outside the EU/EEA (United States). Transfers rely on the EU–US Data Privacy Framework and/or the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses. 5. How long do we keep it? Enquiries that don't lead to a booking — deleted within 12 months. Booking records and invoices — kept for the period required by Spanish tax law (currently 6 years). Server logs and analytics — typically 14 months (Google Analytics default). Marketing consent records — until you withdraw consent, plus a reasonable archival period. 6. Your rights Under GDPR you can, at any time, ask us to: Confirm what data we hold about you and give you a copy (right of access). Correct any inaccurate or out-of-date data (right of rectification). Delete your data when it is no longer needed (right of erasure / "to be forgotten"). Restrict or object to certain processing. Receive your data in a portable format. Withdraw any consent you have given (without affecting prior lawful processing). To exercise any of these rights, message us on WhatsApp at +358 400 406 194 with a copy of an identifying document. We will reply within 30 days. If you are not satisfied with our response, you can complain to the Spanish Data Protection Agency ( Agencia Española de Protección de Datos — www.aepd.es ). 7. Cookies The Site uses a small number of cookies and similar technologies: Strictly necessary — to display the Site, remember your language choice (EN/ES) and your booking-form draft. No consent needed. Analytics — Google Analytics 4 (cookies _ga , _ga_* ) to measure visits anonymously. You can opt out at any time by installing the Google Analytics Opt-out add-on or by clearing cookies in your browser. Third-party embeds — videos hosted on this domain do not set cookies. Embedded social-media links (Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp) only set cookies if you click them. 8. Children The Site is not directed at children under 14. We do not knowingly collect personal data from anyone under 14. Children may, of course, travel on a charter — in that case, their parent or legal guardian provides the booking data. 9. Security The Site is served over HTTPS. Data sent through WhatsApp is end-to-end encrypted by Meta. Internal records are stored in access-controlled cloud services (Google Workspace) protected by two-factor authentication. No system is 100% secure, but we apply commercially reasonable measures. 10. Changes to this policy We may update this policy from time to time — the "Last updated" date at the top will always show the current version. Material changes will be highlighted on the homepage for at least 30 days. 11. Contact Any privacy question or request: WhatsApp +358 400 406 194 . See also our Terms & Conditions .

TITLE: Sailboat Rental Marbella: Crewed &amp; Bareboat Options
DESCRIPTION: Discover sailboat rental Marbella with crewed and bareboat options. Explore the Costa del Sol from Puerto Banús. Book your sailing charter today.

Home › Sailboat Rental Marbella: Crewed & Bareboat Options Experience the freedom of the Mediterranean with a sailboat rental Marbella from Puerto Banús. Whether you prefer a crewed charter with a professional skipper or a bareboat adventure for licensed sailors, our fleet offers options for every level. Explore hidden coves, anchor off the Golden Mile, or sail to Estepona—all with full inclusions like fuel, drinks, and insurance. No hidden extras, just the sea. Our Sailboat Fleet in Marbella We offer two sailboats departing from Puerto Banús. For larger groups, the Lagoon 380 (11.6m, 10 pax) is a spacious catamaran ideal for day trips or overnight charters. The Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) is a classic monohull perfect for couples or small families. Both boats are maintained to high standards and include all safety equipment. Crewed vs Bareboat: What's Right for You? Crewed charter includes a licensed skipper who handles navigation, anchoring, and safety. You sit back, sip a cold beer, and enjoy the views. Perfect for first-timers or those who want a relaxing day. Bareboat rental is available for experienced sailors with a valid license. You take the helm and plan your own itinerary. We provide a briefing on local conditions, but the adventure is yours. Contact us via WhatsApp to discuss requirements. Inclusions on Every Charter All our sailboat rentals include: licensed skipper (crewed only), fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No surprises—just bring your towel and sunscreen. Popular Sailing Destinations from Marbella Cabopino – 8 NM east, sandy beach and dunes. Cala del Faro – 5 NM west, quiet cove with crystal water. Estepona – 15 NM west, charming marina and old town. Sotogrande – 25 NM west, luxury marina and polo clubs. Cala Cortés – 10 NM east, secluded swimming spot. Your skipper will choose the best route based on wind and weather, ensuring a safe and memorable trip. Pricing and Booking Pricing for sailboat rentals is on request via WhatsApp. As a reference, our motor yachts start from €749/2h (Tier A). Contact us for a custom quote on the Lagoon 380 or Dubhe. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during summer. To book, send a WhatsApp message with your preferred date, number of guests, and any special requests. We'll confirm availability and send you a quote within hours. Why Choose Our Sailboat Rental Marbella? We operate directly from Puerto Banús, the heart of Marbella's luxury boating scene. No middlemen, no inflated prices. Our skippers know the coast intimately—from the Golden Mile to the cliffs of Cala del Faro. Every charter is tailored to your group, whether it's a family day out, a birthday celebration, or a romantic sunset sail. Explore our full boat rental Marbella options for more choices, including motor yachts and speedboats. Frequently asked questions What sailboats are available for rental in Marbella? Our fleet includes the Lagoon 380 (11.6m, 10 pax) and Dubhe (8m, 5 pax), both departing from Puerto Banús. For specific availability and pricing, please contact us via WhatsApp. Can I rent a sailboat without a skipper in Marbella? Yes, bareboat rental is available if you hold a valid sailing license and have sufficient experience. However, we recommend a crewed charter for a stress-free experience, especially if you're unfamiliar with local waters. What is included in a crewed sailboat charter? Every crewed charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You just bring your swimwear and sunscreen. Where can I sail from Marbella? Popular sailing destinations include Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coast of Gibraltar. Your skipper can tailor the itinerary based on wind conditions and your preferences. How much does a sailboat rental in Marbella cost? Pricing depends on the boat and duration. For sailboats, rates are available on request via WhatsApp. As a reference, our motor yachts start from €749/2h (Tier A). Contact us for a custom quote. What is the best time of year for sailing in Marbella? The sailing season runs from April to October, with peak conditions in June through September. Average summer temperatures are 28-32°C, and the Mediterranean sea is calm, making for ideal sailing. Do I need a license to rent a sailboat bareboat in Marbella? Yes, for bareboat rental you need a valid sailing license recognized in Spain (e.g., PER or equivalent). You must also demonstrate experience. Crewed charters require no license—just relax and enjoy. How do I book a sailboat rental in Marbella? Book directly via WhatsApp. Provide your preferred date, number of guests, and any special requests. TITLE: Speedboat Rental Marbella: High-Performance Charters from Puerto Banús
DESCRIPTION: Book a speedboat rental Marbella from Puerto Banús. 6 passengers, 7m boat, skipper included, from €749/2h. High-performance charters with drinks &amp; snacks.

Home › Speedboat Rental Marbella: High-Performance Charters from Puerto Banús For a fast, exhilarating way to explore the Costa del Sol, a speedboat rental Marbella from Puerto Banús is hard to beat. Our 7-metre speedboat seats up to 6 passengers and comes with a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and all taxes included. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours, making it an affordable option for a short blast along the coast or a full-day adventure. Departing from Puerto Banús, you can reach Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés in minutes, or simply enjoy the thrill of high-performance cruising with the iconic La Concha mountain as your backdrop. Speedboat Specifications & Capacity Our speedboat is 7 metres long and can carry up to 6 passengers. It’s powered by a high-performance engine that delivers quick acceleration and a smooth ride, even in light chop. The open layout gives everyone unobstructed views, and there’s ample seating for the whole group. Ideal for couples, small families, or a group of friends wanting a private charter without the size or cost of a larger yacht. Inclusions – What’s Covered Every charter includes: Licensed professional skipper Fuel for the entire trip Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava Light snacks Insurance and safety equipment Spanish IVA (21%) – no hidden extras You just bring your swimwear and sunscreen. TITLE: Sunset Cruise Marbella 2026: 2-Hour Charters from €250
DESCRIPTION: Sunset cruise Marbella from €749 for 2 hours on our Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 motor yacht. Departure 1h before sunset from Puerto Banús — skipper, drinks and VAT included.

Home › Sunset Cruise Marbella 2026: 2-Hour Charters from €250 The Marbella sunset cruise is two hours, leaves an hour before the sun touches the horizon, and ends just as the lights come on along the Golden Mile. From €250 with skipper, fuel, drinks and snacks included — and BYO cava is welcomed on every boat we work with. When the sunset cruise actually leaves Departure is 60–75 minutes before official sunset, so you're at anchor with a drink in hand when the colour starts. Local sunset times by month: Month Sunset (Marbella) Cruise departs December 18:30 17:15 March 19:30 18:15 May 21:25 20:10 June 21:55 20:40 July 21:45 20:30 August 21:10 19:55 September 20:45 19:30 October 19:45 18:30 Plan to arrive at the marina 15 minutes before departure — there's no "please wait for us" on a sunset booking when the actual sun is on a fixed schedule. The standard route From Puerto Banús, the classic 2-hour route is: Slip out of the marina mouth heading south-east, displacement speed. Cruise west along the Golden Mile — the stretch from Banús to Marbella town centre — passing Marbella Club, Nikki Beach and Ocean Club from offshore. Continue past Marbella Marina to anchor briefly off Río Real with the La Concha mountain backlit behind you. Slow turn back as the sun drops, drinks served, photos taken. Return to Puerto Banús just after twilight, marina lights coming on. From Marbella Marina the route inverts: head east to Cabopino first, anchor and return. From Cabopino, you stay east of Marbella town, with the same anchor-and-watch pattern. All three routes deliver the same sunset and very similar prices — pick by the closest marina to your hotel. Which boat fits a sunset cruise best? Couples (2): a 10 m motor yacht with a generous bow sun-pad. Intimate, fast, you can step ashore for dinner at 22:00. The romantic default. Small group (4–8): a 12 m motor yacht delivers space and the same speed. Most popular sunset booking in Marbella. Larger group (10–16): a 40+ ft sailing or power catamaran. Flat deck for golden-hour photos, more table space for catering. See catamaran rental Marbella . Party / birthday (16+): a 22 m+ yacht with a flybridge. See boat party Marbella for group cruises with music systems. For comparison with full-day options, the boat rental Marbella hub lays out every boat type side-by-side. What's included Licensed skipper for the full 2 hours Fuel for the standard route Cooler with water, soft drinks, ice Snacks (olives, almonds, crackers, fruit) Towels Use of the on-board Bluetooth sound system (BYO playlist) Not included: alcohol (BYO — cava and prosecco are the local picks), prepared dinner (add a catered platter for €25–€60 per head), and the skipper tip (€20–€40 cash is normal for a great sunset). Photography tips from local skippers Shoot the half hour BEFORE the sun drops, not after — that's when the light is on the boat and on the coast simultaneously. Stand on the bow facing land for the Golden Mile shot, or face west for the silhouette-against-sun shot. iPhones in HDR mode handle the contrast better than many people expect; bring a microfibre cloth for sea spray on the lens. Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella "Every booking on this site lands on my phone first.
