We confirm the exact time the day before. How long is a typical sunset cruise? Two hours is the default. Three-hour cruises (€350) are available for groups who want a longer dinner on board. Which boat is best for sunset? 10–12 m motor yacht for couples; sail or power catamaran for 8+ guests. Avoid sportfishers and licence-free boats — they're working boats, not romantic ones. Can we have champagne and food on board? Yes, BYO welcomed on most boats (cava is the local choice). Catered platters €25–€60 per head, 24 hours notice. What if it's cloudy? Cloudy Marbella sunsets are often more dramatic than clear ones. Trip runs unless wind exceeds Force 4–5. Full refund or rebook for weather cancellations. TITLE: Terms &amp; Conditions — Boat Rental Marbella
DESCRIPTION: Terms and conditions for boat rentals booked through boatrentalinmarbella.com — booking process, pricing, payment, cancellation, weather policy, safety, liability and applicable law.

Terms & Conditions Last updated: 1 June 2026 These terms govern any booking, charter, enquiry or use of the website boatrentalinmarbella.com ("the Site") operated by Boat Rental Marbella ("we", "us"). By submitting an enquiry, accepting a quote, paying a deposit or stepping on board a chartered vessel you confirm that you have read, understood and accepted these terms. 1. About us Boat Rental Marbella is an independent charter agency based in Marbella, Andalucía, Spain. We coordinate skippered yacht, catamaran, motorboat and jet-ski charters operating out of Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo Virgen del Carmen), Cabopino, Estepona and Sotogrande. Charters are operated by licensed Spanish skippers and partner companies holding the appropriate maritime and commercial licences. WhatsApp / phone: +358 400 406 194 2. Booking process You send us an enquiry via the website booking form, WhatsApp, email or Instagram/Facebook DM, including the date, group size and preferred boat or experience. We reply with availability and a written quote (typically within a few hours, often within minutes during European working hours). The booking is confirmed only when (a) we send a written confirmation and (b) the agreed deposit or full payment has been received. You receive the departure address, skipper contact, meeting time and a short briefing by WhatsApp at least 24 hours before the charter. 3. Prices, payment and what's included Prices are quoted in Euros (€), include Spanish IVA (VAT, currently 21%) and — unless we explicitly state otherwise — include the licensed skipper, fuel for the standard coastal route, soft drinks and water, snorkel gear, paddleboard, towels and standard insurance. The following are typically not included unless stated: catered lunch, premium drinks, water-toy fuel (e.g. jet-ski excursion outside the charter), berthing fees in third-party ports, gratuities, and any optional add-ons (DJ, photographer, decoration). Payment options vary by operator — typically a 30–50% deposit by card, Stripe link, Revolut or SEPA bank transfer to confirm, with the balance due in cash or card on the day of the charter. Specific terms appear on the written quote. 4. Cancellation, modification and refund policy Unless your written confirmation states otherwise, the standard cancellation terms are: 7 or more days before departure — 100% refund of any amount paid. 3 to 6 days before departure — 50% refund. Less than 48 hours before departure — no refund. No-show — no refund. Date changes are free of charge if requested at least 72 hours before departure and subject to availability. 5. Weather policy The skipper is the sole authority on whether the trip can run safely. If the forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 (approx. 20+ knots), sea state is unsafe, or a port authority issues a navigation warning, the skipper will postpone the charter — typically the evening before, or by 08:00 on the morning of the trip. In that case you are offered either: a new departure date at no extra cost (subject to availability), or a full refund of all amounts paid. Light rain alone is not a cancellation reason on the Costa del Sol. If you cancel because of weather while the skipper still considers the trip safe, the standard cancellation policy in section 4 applies. 6. Safety, conduct and the skipper's authority On board, the captain has full legal and operational authority over the vessel and its passengers under Spanish maritime law. The captain may, at their sole discretion and without refund: refuse boarding to anyone visibly intoxicated, on drugs, or behaving aggressively; alter the route, shorten the trip or return to port for safety reasons; require passengers to wear life jackets; refuse to allow swimming, jumping from the boat, or operating water toys when conditions are unsafe. All passengers must follow the captain's instructions and the on-board safety briefing. Children under 12 must wear a life jacket at all times when the boat is underway. Pets are accepted only when agreed in advance and must travel with a dog-safe life jacket supplied by the owner. 7. Behaviour, damage and cleaning Drugs of any kind are strictly forbidden on board — the captain is legally obliged to return to port and notify the Guardia Civil. The customer is liable for any damage to the vessel, fittings, water toys or third-party property caused by the customer or their guests through negligence or wilful misconduct, including but not limited to broken glassware, stained upholstery, lost or damaged snorkel gear and excessive cleaning (e.g. vomit, food spillage requiring deep clean — typically charged at €150–€500). 8. Late arrival and overruns The charter slot begins at the agreed departure time. If you arrive late, the charter still ends at the originally agreed return time and no extension or refund is owed. Extending the charter on the day is usually possible if the next slot is free — additional time is charged at 80–100% of the standard hourly rate and must be agreed with the skipper before the booked slot ends. 9. Insurance Every chartered vessel carries the third-party hull and passenger insurance required by Spanish law. This does not cover personal items (phones, cameras, jewellery, sunglasses), pre-existing medical conditions, or losses caused by your own breach of safety instructions. We strongly recommend you take out your own travel insurance with cruise/charter cover, particularly for high-value bookings or group trips. 10. Photography and content From time to time the skipper or a drone operator may take photos or short videos during the charter. By boarding you consent to non-identifying use of this material on our website and social channels (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube). If you do not want to appear in any published material, tell the skipper before departure and we will exclude you. You may withdraw permission for previously published images by messaging us on WhatsApp at +358 400 406 194 . 11. Liability To the maximum extent permitted by Spanish law, our liability for any loss arising from your charter is limited to the total amount you paid for that charter. We are not liable for indirect or consequential losses (missed flights, accommodation costs, loss of enjoyment) caused by weather cancellations, force majeure (storms, government orders, strikes, pandemics, port closures), or third-party acts. Nothing in these terms excludes liability for death, personal injury caused by negligence, or any other liability that cannot be excluded under Spanish consumer law. 12. Affiliate disclosure Some links on this Site point to third-party operators or booking platforms. When we list a third-party operator we may receive a commission if you book through us. This never affects the price you pay and we only feature operators we would book ourselves. 13. Intellectual property All text, photos, videos and graphics on this Site are © Boat Rental Marbella or used under licence. You may share individual pages on social media and link to the Site freely. You may not republish, scrape, or use the content for AI model training without our written permission. Boat brand names (Astondoa, Azimut, Mangusta, Ferretti, etc.) belong to their respective manufacturers and are used only for descriptive reference. 14. Applicable law and jurisdiction These terms are governed by Spanish law. Any dispute between us and a consumer customer will be heard by the courts of the customer's place of residence within the EU, or by the courts of Marbella (Málaga) for non-EU customers. Consumer customers may also use the EU Online Dispute Resolution platform at ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr . 15. Changes to these terms We may update these terms from time to time — the "Last updated" date at the top reflects the current version. The terms that apply to your booking are the ones in force on the day we send the written confirmation. 16. Contact Any question on these terms or on a specific booking: WhatsApp +35

Yes — you board a Spanish-flagged charter, and the skipper logs passport numbers for insurance. EU national ID also works if you have dual citizenship. Can I use a UK ICC / RYA Day Skipper to drive the boat? For licence-free boats (up to 5m / 15hp): no licence needed at all. For larger boats: a UK ICC or RYA Day Skipper certificate let you charter bareboat in Spain — bring the original certificate, not a copy. Most UK guests book skippered anyway because Marbella's marina traffic is intense. Can I pay in £? We invoice in €. Pay with a UK debit/credit card and your bank handles the conversion. If the skipper cancels for weather, you always get a 100% refund or free reschedule. See full cancellation policy . UK travel insurance — does it cover yacht charter? Standard UK travel insurance often excludes private yacht charter. Look for policies with "watersports / cruise" cover or buy a specific add-on. Recommended for any booking over £1,500. TITLE: Yacht Charter Marbella 2026: Luxury Motor Yachts from €450
DESCRIPTION: Yacht charter Marbella from €749 for 2h. Our 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts (Astondoa 40 + Azimut 39) crewed by licensed skipper, departing Puerto Banús. Drinks, fuel, insurance and VAT included.

Home › Yacht Charter Marbella 2026: Luxury Motor Yachts from €450 Yacht charter in Marbella means stepping onto a 10-to-25-metre crewed motor yacht at Puerto Banús (occasionally Marbella Marina ), being handed a cold drink, and being driven up the Golden Mile coastline by a licensed skipper who knows where the wind drops in the afternoon. Prices start at €450 for a 4-hour trip and scale up to €4,500+ for a full day on a 25-metre superyacht with butler service. Yacht charter Marbella prices in 2026 Crewed charter is priced by hull length, season and duration — not by guest count up to the boat's licensed capacity. The pricing grid below covers the boats that actually run from Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina, with the skipper, fuel and standard inclusions baked in. Our fleet is two 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts — same price grid above applies to both. Larger yachts (16m+) and superyachts are not currently in our fleet; if you need a bigger boat, message us and we will source one through partners. Crewed vs bareboat Roughly 95% of yacht charters from Marbella are crewed — you and your group are guests, the skipper handles the helm. Bareboat (self-drive) charter exists but requires either a Spanish PNB/PER licence or a recognised foreign equivalent (RYA Day Skipper with the ICC stamp is the most common one we see from UK customers). Even with the licence, most groups still prefer crewed because Puerto Banús's berthing manoeuvres in summer wind are not the part of your day you want to remember. Departing from Puerto Banús Puerto Banús is the natural home for yacht charter in Marbella. Yachts above 12 metres almost all berth here, and the marina infrastructure (deep water, large pontoons, walking distance restaurants for pre-board lunch) is built for charter operations. Walk down from the multi-storey carpark, your skipper meets you at the gangway with a passenger list, the safety briefing takes 90 seconds, and you're off the pontoon within 15 minutes of arrival. If you prefer something quieter, larger yachts can also pick up from Marbella Marina by arrangement (10-minute reposition motor across the bay) for the same charter price — but most guests just enjoy the Puerto Banús experience. Typical 8-hour itinerary 10:30 Boarding at Pier 3, welcome drinks, safety briefing. 11:00 Depart Puerto Banús, cruise west at 12 knots past Estepona. 12:30 Anchor off Cala del Faro, swim, paddleboard, snorkelling, lunch on board (BYO or catered). 14:30 Re-anchor at Sotogrande or push back east towards Marbella. 16:00 Optional tender ride to a beach club, or anchor near Ocean Club for a swim-up. 18:30 Return to Puerto Banús, arrive with sunset light on the Golden Mile. Skippers happily modify — heading east towards dolphin watching grounds and Cabopino is a popular variation, especially if you've done the western route before. What's included on a yacht charter Licensed skipper for the full duration Fuel for a standard coastal cruising itinerary (high-speed laps cost extra) Soft drinks, water and ice in the galley fridge Light snacks (fruit, crisps, biscuits) Snorkelling gear (masks, fins) and at least one inflatable toy (donut or SUP) Towels for every guest Marina exit and re-entry fees from the home port Not included: alcohol (BYO welcome on most boats — ask when you book), catered lunch (€25–€60 per head from the dockside caterers), berth taxes for stops at non-home marinas, and skipper tip. For the wider boat rental Marbella options including smaller and cheaper boats, compare on the main hub. Yacht charter Marbella vs. yacht charter elsewhere on the Costa del Sol The Marbella stretch — particularly Puerto Banús — concentrates more luxury motor yachts than anywhere else on the Costa del Sol. Estepona has roughly a quarter of Puerto Banús's fleet, with Sotogrande further west adding smaller numbers. If you specifically want a sailing yacht or catamaran rather than a motor yacht, see our dedicated catamaran rental Marbella page — catamarans cluster in slightly different berths. How to book a yacht charter in Marbella Message us on WhatsApp with date, duration, group size and any preferences (sun pads vs shaded cockpit, swim-heavy vs cruising, specific lunch caterer). We send back 2–3 yacht options with photos and pricing within an hour during business hours. Confirm with a 50% deposit, balance on the boarding day. Cancellation up to 7 days before is fully refundable; weather cancellations are always 100% refundable. Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Follow @boatrentalmarbella Frequently asked questions How much is a yacht charter in Marbella? Crewed motor yacht charter in Marbella costs from €450 for a 4-hour trip on a 10-12 metre yacht in low season, rising to €900 for the same 4 hours on an 18-metre yacht in August. A full 8-hour day on a 12-metre yacht is €750–€900; on a luxury 20-metre yacht €1,800–€2,400. Skipper, fuel and drinks included. Do I need a licence to charter a yacht in Marbella? Not when you book a crewed charter — the skipper holds the licence. If you want bareboat (self-drive) on anything above 5 m / 15 hp, you need a PNB or PER Spanish licence , or a recognised foreign equivalent such as the RYA Day Skipper with the ICC stamp. What's the difference between a yacht charter and a boat rental? In Marbella, 'yacht charter' usually means a motor or sail yacht 10 metres or longer, professionally crewed, departing from a marina with full marina services. 'Boat rental' is the broader term that also covers smaller licence-free runabouts and 6-8 m day boats. Anything over €400 for 4 hours is almost always a yacht charter. Can we anchor and swim during the charter? Yes. Standard itineraries include two anchor stops for swimming and snorkelling — typical spots are Cala del Faro west of Estepona, the anchorage off Cabopino dunes, or the deeper water off Río Verde. Your skipper will pick based on wind and chop on the day. Is gratuity expected? Tipping the skipper is customary, not compulsory. €20–€50 in cash at the end of a half-day is normal; 10% of the charter price on luxury yachts is the upper-end convention. Tips are split between captain and any deckhand. On the water with us Real charters with real groups across Marbella, Puerto Banús and the Costa del Sol.
